Title: Data Engineer II
Description:
Job Description

We are Lennar

Lennar is one of the nation's leading homebuilders, dedicated to making an impact and creating an extraordinary experience for their Homeowners, Communities, and Associates by building quality homes and providing exceptional customer service, giving back to the communities in which we work and live in, and fostering a culture of opportunity and growth for our Associates throughout their career. Lennar has been recognized as a Fortune 500® company and consistently ranked among the top homebuilders in the United States.

Join a Company that Empowers you to Build your Future

As a Data Engineer, you are responsible for analyzing large amounts of business data, solving real world problems, and developing metrics and business cases that will enable Business Insights. This is done by leveraging data from various platforms such as Jira, Portal, Salesforce. You will work with a team of Product Managers, Software Engineers and Business Intelligence Engineers to automate and scale the analysis, and to make the data more actionable to manage business at scale. You will own many large datasets, implement new data pipelines that feed into or from critical data systems.



A career with purpose.
A career built on making dreams come true.
A career built on building zero defect homes, cost management, and adherence to schedules.



Your Responsibilities On The Team



Design, implement and support an analytical data infrastructure and working knowledge of Modern Data Warehouse concepts.
Design, build, and maintain efficient and scalable data pipelines and ETL processes to process large volumes of structured and unstructured data.
Optimize data storage and retrieval methods to ensure performance, scalability, and cost-efficiency.
Manage AWS resources including EC2, S3, Glue, Lambda, API’s, IAM, Cloud Watch etc.
Interface with other technology teams to extract, transform, and load data from a wide variety of data sources using SQL and AWS big data technologies
Explore and learn the latest AWS technologies to provide new capabilities and increase efficiency
Collaborate with Data Scientists and Business Intelligence Engineers (BIEs) to recognize and help adopt best practices in reporting and analysis
Help continually improve ongoing reporting and analysis processes, automating or simplifying self-service support for customers
Maintain internal reporting platforms/tools including troubleshooting and development. Interact with internal users to establish and clarify requirements in order to develop report specifications.
Work with Engineering partners to help shape and implement the development of BI infrastructure including Data Warehousing, reporting and analytics platforms.
Contribute to the development of the BI tools, skills, culture and impact.
Write advanced SQL queries and Python code to develop solutions.
Working Knowledge of Snowflake.
Collaborate across teams to align AI initiatives with organizational goals and understanding of AI concepts
Knowledge on continuous integration/continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines and working on deployments when necessary.



Requirements



Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
3-5 years of experience in data engineering or a related role, with demonstrated success in delivering data solutions.
AWS Glue, Lambda, S3, EC2, CloudWatch, Cloud Trail.
Dbt, Snowflake, SQL, Python, Qlik.
Proficient in SQL, with the ability to write complex queries, perform query optimization, and conduct performance tuning.
Experience with NoSQL databases, such as MongoDB, Cassandra, or DynamoDB, and an understanding of their appropriate use cases.
Strong programming skills in Python, Java, or Scala, with experience in data processing frameworks (e.g., Apache Spark, Hadoop).
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and data services, such as AWS Redshift, Azure Synapse, or Google BigQuery.
Knowledge of big data technologies, including Hadoop, Spark, Kafka, and HBase, with experience in distributed data processing.
Familiarity with data orchestration tools, such as Apache Airflow for scheduling and managing data workflows.
Experience with data versioning and testing tools, such as DVC (Data Version Control) and dbt (data build tool).
Understanding of data security practices, including encryption, access controls, and data masking.



Physical & Office/Site Presence Requirements:

Job Description

We are Lennar

Lennar is one of the nation's leading homebuilders, dedicated to making an impact and creating an extraordinary experience for their Homeowners, Communities, and Associates by building quality homes and providing exceptional customer service, giving back to the communities in which we work and live in, and fostering a culture of opportunity and growth for our Associates throughout their career. Lennar has been recognized as a Fortune 500® company and consistently ranked among the top homebuilders in the United States.

Join a Company that Empowers you to Build your Future

As a Data Engineer, you are responsible for analyzing large amounts of business data, solving real world problems, and developing metrics and business cases that will enable Business Insights. This is done by leveraging data from various platforms such as Jira, Portal, Salesforce. You will work with a team of Product Managers, Software Engineers and Business Intelligence Engineers to automate and scale the analysis, and to make the data more actionable to manage business at scale. You will own many large datasets, implement new data pipelines that feed into or from critical data systems.


  • A career with purpose.
  • A career built on making dreams come true.
  • A career built on building zero defect homes, cost management, and adherence to schedules.


Your Responsibilities On The Team


  • Design, implement and support an analytical data infrastructure and working knowledge of Modern Data Warehouse concepts.

  • Optimize data storage and retrieval methods to ensure performance, scalability, and cost-efficiency.
  • Manage AWS resources including EC2, S3, Glue, Lambda, API’s, IAM, Cloud Watch etc.
  • Interface with other technology teams to extract, transform, and load data from a wide variety of data sources using SQL and AWS big data technologies
  • Explore and learn the latest AWS technologies to provide new capabilities and increase efficiency
  • Collaborate with Data Scientists and Business Intelligence Engineers (BIEs) to recognize and help adopt best practices in reporting and analysis
  • Help continually improve ongoing reporting and analysis processes, automating or simplifying self-service support for customers
  • Maintain internal reporting platforms/tools including troubleshooting and development. Interact with internal users to establish and clarify requirements in order to develop report specifications.
  • Work with Engineering partners to help shape and implement the development of BI infrastructure including Data Warehousing, reporting and analytics platforms.
  • Contribute to the development of the BI tools, skills, culture and impact.
  • Write advanced SQL queries and Python code to develop solutions.
  • Working Knowledge of Snowflake.
  • Collaborate across teams to align AI initiatives with organizational goals and understanding of AI concepts
  • Knowledge on continuous integration/continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines and working on deployments when necessary.


Requirements

  • 3-5 years of experience in data engineering or a related role, with demonstrated success in delivering data solutions.
  • AWS Glue, Lambda, S3, EC2, CloudWatch, Cloud Trail.
  • Dbt, Snowflake, SQL, Python, Qlik.
  • Proficient in SQL, with the ability to write complex queries, perform query optimization, and conduct performance tuning.
  • Experience with NoSQL databases, such as MongoDB, Cassandra, or DynamoDB, and an understanding of their appropriate use cases.
  • Strong programming skills in Python, Java, or Scala, with experience in data processing frameworks (e.g., Apache Spark, Hadoop).
  • Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and data services, such as AWS Redshift, Azure Synapse, or Google BigQuery.
  • Knowledge of big data technologies, including Hadoop, Spark, Kafka, and HBase, with experience in distributed data processing.
  • Familiarity with data orchestration tools, such as Apache Airflow for scheduling and managing data workflows.
  • Experience with data versioning and testing tools, such as DVC (Data Version Control) and dbt (data build tool).
  • Understanding of data security practices, including encryption, access controls, and data masking.

Title: Data Analytics Engineer at GFiber
Description:
This role is Remote in US!!

This role is not eligible for immigration sponsorship or transfers.

Please email your resume to Svitlana at ssikora@google.com, ensuring you include where you discovered this job opportunity.

Role Description

As a Data Analytics Engineer for GFiber, you will be working with multiple stakeholders across the organization to provide quantitative support, define metric rules and logic, deliver useful tables and reports, model business scenarios, and solve a wide range of business problems. To do so, you will be expected to create new pipelines from scratch, merging broad financial and ERP data sets together into a cohesive technical infrastructure. You will be responsible for engineering foundational table resources using Google Cloud Platform (GCP): BigQuery, Dataform, LookML, Dataplex, etc. The pipelines, metrics, tools, and analyses you develop will be used by our leaders to make strategic decisions and serve as a key input in decision-making.

Responsibilities

Create foundational pipelines and data extractions with large data sets using SQL and other GCP technologies demonstrating comfort with software-engineering-level best practices and approaches to data pipelines and management.

Work with large, complex data sets to solve difficult analysis problems, assess the impact of initiatives, and create on-going tooling/resources to run the business.

Collaborate with cross-functional partners to understand business context for solutions, analysis, and tooling needed to become the single point of contact for all financial data engineering questions.

Undertake specific tasks, bugs, and maintenance work. Additionally, own complex projects, proving mastery through accuracy, timeliness, and volume of work, with minimal guidance.

Minimum Qualifications

Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.

5 years of experience in analytics, data science, and/or computer science engineering.

5 Years of experience with using SQL, writing queries from scratch on a daily basis, data engineering, architecture, pipeline management, and Extract, Transform, Load (ETL).

Expertise using GCP tools.

Nice To Have

Experience in HTML, Python, or other coding languages.

Experience using data to identify opportunities for business improvement and defining/measuring the success of those initiatives.

Ability to use written and verbal communication to build relationships with cross-functional partners and conduct presentations for stakeholders, including executive leadership.

Ability to engage with PMO teams to prioritize, provide level of effort estimations, manage bug queues and bandwidth.

The US base salary range for this full-time position is between $105,600 - $154,900 + bonus + cash award + benefits.

Title: Accelerating Insights with Power BI for Enhanced Dashboard Functionality for Canada Via Rail
Description:
Streamlining Data Visualization for Brinker with Power BI

The Challenge

A leading company referred to here as the client, sought to enhance its data visualization and analytics capabilities by transitioning from Domo Dashboards to Microsoft Power BI. The client’s primary objective was to replicate their existing dashboards in Power BI with a refreshed focus on their customer, “Brinker.” A significant challenge was the integration of Power BI with NetSuite via an ODBC connection, especially given the large volumes of data involved. The Customer table alone contained over 400,000 records, leading to prolonged data loading times. Additionally, the client required the dashboards to be developed within a tight deadline of a few days, posing a considerable challenge given the complexity of the task.

The Solution

Our Consultancy Agency Stepped In To Assist The Client With Their Power BI Dashboard Creation, Addressing Each Challenge With Strategic Solutions

Optimized Data Integration: Recognizing the limitations of the ODBC connection, especially with the Oracle database’s compatibility issues with Power BI, we recommended an alternative approach. We suggested leveraging NetSuite Analytics Warehouse, a cloud-based BI solution that could seamlessly integrate with Power BI, thus improving data loading times and overall dashboard performance.

Custom Dashboard Development: Drawing from the example attachments and specific requirements provided by the client, we meticulously crafted Power BI dashboards that mirrored the desired layout and functionality seen in their Domo Dashboards. This included replicating various charts and widgets focusing on Brinker, ensuring a familiar yet improved user experience.

Language and Filter Adjustments: Addressing the client’s request for language updates, we ensured all dashboard labels, including those on the Backorder dashboard, were presented in English. Although adding numerous filters was identified as a time-consuming task, we engaged in a detailed discussion with the client about the implications. We agreed to prioritize essential updates to meet the project’s tight deadline.

Summary Dashboard Creation: We proposed and implemented a summary tab to enhance the dashboard’s utility. This tab featured key charts from each dashboard section, carefully selected based on the client’s specifications. For charts lacking titles, we added descriptive titles such as “Open Orders by Product Category” to ensure clarity and usefulness in the summary view.

The Outcome

The successful implementation of the Power BI dashboards significantly improved the client’s data visualization and analysis capabilities. The transition from Domo to Power BI, coupled with the strategic use of NetSuite Analytics Warehouse, resulted in faster data loading times, enhanced dashboard performance, and more insightful data analytics tailored specifically to Brinker.

The project was delivered within the ambitious deadline set by the client, thanks to our team’s expertise and efficient project management. The client was particularly pleased with the custom solutions provided, including the language corrections and the highly functional summary dashboard, which offered a consolidated view of key metrics across all tabs.

Conclusion

This case study exemplifies our consultancy agency’s ability to navigate complex data integration challenges, deliver custom Power BI solutions, and meet the specific needs of our clients within tight deadlines. Our approach not only addresses immediate technical challenges but also ensures that the solutions we provide are scalable, efficient, and tailored to our clients’ strategic goals.

Title: ENTRY LEVEL - VIRTUAL JOBS (REMOTE) - PART-TIME - BEGINNER FRIENDLY & START ASAP
Description:
Role Description This is a beginner-friendly, part-time, remote role for entry-level virtual team members who are available to start as soon as possible. Day-to-day tasks may include supporting project documentation, organizing files and data, assisting with basic research related to architecture and design, and helping prepare presentation materials. The role can involve coordinating with team members via digital collaboration tools, updating project trackers, and performing simple administrative or operational tasks as directed. Depending on the candidate’s interests and background, responsibilities may also include contributing to design concepts, assisting with client communication drafts, and learning standard workflows used in architectural and interior design projects. Training and guidance will be provided to help new team members grow their skills in a professional design environment.

Qualifications

Interest in architecture, interior design, urban design, or the built environment, with a willingness to learn and support design-related tasks.
Basic computer literacy, including comfort with productivity tools (such as word processing, spreadsheets, and presentation software) and online collaboration platforms.
Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple small tasks while meeting deadlines in a remote setting.
Effective written and verbal communication skills, including clear online communication with team members and professional email etiquette.
Self-motivated, reliable, and able to work independently with guidance, demonstrating a proactive approach to asking questions and seeking feedback.
Flexible availability suitable for part-time remote work, with a stable internet connection and a suitable work environment.
Relevant academic background or coursework in architecture, interior design, engineering, project management, or a related field is a plus but not required.
Prior internship, freelance, or project experience in design or administrative support is beneficial, but candidates with no professional experience are encouraged to apply.

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking an Excel Expert to work on a contract basis. You will support cross-functional teams by delivering high-quality spreadsheets and dashboards that drive business decisions.

• Strong understanding of financial modeling principles and Excel’s Power Query/Pivot tools.

• Attention to detail to ensure accuracy in financial data and calculations.

This role offers a unique opportunity to work with a global leader in the Financial Services industry, contributing to streamlined financial reporting and data-driven decision-making. The position supports critical business functions with measurable impact.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Your Expedition (Position Overview)

We are seeking a skilled and motivated Data Engineer to join our data team in building robust and scalable data pipelines that power analytics and business decisions in the fast-paced travel industry. You will work with large datasets from booking systems, customer touchpoints, pricing engines, and third-party APIs to create a unified data environment that supports personalization, demand forecasting, route optimization, and customer experience enhancements.

This is a remote position.

Going The Extra Mile (Responsibilities)


Design, build, and maintain scalable ETL/ELT pipelines for ingesting data from internal and external travel-related sources (e.g., GDSs, OTAs, CRM systems).
Collaborate with data analysts, data scientists, and product teams to understand data needs and deliver high-quality, reliable datasets.
Integrate data from various platforms including booking engines, mobile apps, customer service systems, and web analytics tools.
Develop and optimize data models and schemas in cloud data warehouses (e.g., Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift).
Implement data quality checks and monitoring to ensure the accuracy and reliability of data.
Support real-time data streaming for dynamic pricing, inventory updates, and customer interactions.
Ensure data privacy, governance, and compliance with regulations like GDPR and CCPA.


Your Passport To Success (Qualifications)


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field.
4+ years of experience in data engineering or backend data development and integration.
Proficiency in SQL and at least one programming language (Python, Java, Scala).
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and modern data stacks (Airflow, dbt, Kafka, IDMC, etc.).
Knowledge of data warehousing concepts and tools.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience in the travel or hospitality industry.
Familiarity with travel data formats and systems (e.g., PNRs, GDS, IATA standards).
Exposure to predictive analytics, personalization, or recommender systems.
Understanding of API integrations with travel platforms and third-party services.


Benefits Onboard

In addition to Medical, Dental, and Vision benefits Direct Travel offers an employee rewards and recognitions program, Total Rewards Package which includes Wellness, Sustainability, DE&I initiatives, and Mental Health Support.

Our Brand Voyage: Direct Travel

Direct Travel is a leading provider of corporate travel management services. By leveraging both the expertise of its people and innovative solutions, Direct Travel enables clients to derive the greatest value from their travel program in terms of superior service, progressive technologies, and significant cost savings. HTML:
Your Expedition (Position Overview)

We are seeking a skilled and motivated Data Engineer to join our data team in building robust and scalable data pipelines that power analytics and business decisions in the fast-paced travel industry. You will work with large datasets from booking systems, customer touchpoints, pricing engines, and third-party APIs to create a unified data environment that supports personalization, demand forecasting, route optimization, and customer experience enhancements.

This is a remote position.

Going The Extra Mile (Responsibilities)

  • Design, build, and maintain scalable ETL/ELT pipelines for ingesting data from internal and external travel-related sources (e.g., GDSs, OTAs, CRM systems).
  • Collaborate with data analysts, data scientists, and product teams to understand data needs and deliver high-quality, reliable datasets.
  • Integrate data from various platforms including booking engines, mobile apps, customer service systems, and web analytics tools.
  • Develop and optimize data models and schemas in cloud data warehouses (e.g., Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift).
  • Implement data quality checks and monitoring to ensure the accuracy and reliability of data.
  • Support real-time data streaming for dynamic pricing, inventory updates, and customer interactions.
  • Ensure data privacy, governance, and compliance with regulations like GDPR and CCPA.

Your Passport To Success (Qualifications)

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field.
  • 4+ years of experience in data engineering or backend data development and integration.
  • Proficiency in SQL and at least one programming language (Python, Java, Scala).
  • Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and modern data stacks (Airflow, dbt, Kafka, IDMC, etc.).
  • Knowledge of data warehousing concepts and tools.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in the travel or hospitality industry.
  • Familiarity with travel data formats and systems (e.g., PNRs, GDS, IATA standards).
  • Exposure to predictive analytics, personalization, or recommender systems.
  • Understanding of API integrations with travel platforms and third-party services.

Benefits Onboard

In addition to Medical, Dental, and Vision benefits Direct Travel offers an employee rewards and recognitions program, Total Rewards Package which includes Wellness, Sustainability, DE&I initiatives, and Mental Health Support.

Our Brand Voyage: Direct Travel

Direct Travel is a leading provider of corporate travel management services. Title: Insights Analyst
Description:
1. About Our Client:

Datassential operates within the food and beverage industry, addressing the challenge of effectively developing, marketing, and selling products by leveraging extensive data and AI technology. The organization provides an intelligence platform used by over 90% of the world’s largest food and beverage brands. With a global presence enhanced by acquisitions in France and Canada and support from private equity investments, the company focuses on advancing product development and research to deliver valuable insights.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Insights Analyst role is part of the Insights team responsible for delivering clear, data-driven presentations and actionable business recommendations to clients in the food and beverage sector. This position contributes by mastering syndicated products and supporting clients through tailored reports and presentations, ultimately enhancing client decision-making and satisfaction.




3. Responsibilities:

• Use syndicated products to extract data and create insightful, impactful stories addressing client needs

• Provide actionable insights to clients

• Deliver excellent customer service with a client-focused approach

• Suggest project enhancements beyond initial requests to improve storytelling and identify upsell opportunities

• Represent client feedback internally to improve company solutions




4. Requirements:

• BA/BS in Marketing, Business, Psychology, Economics, or related field

• 3-5 years of experience in market research, data insights, or similar analytical roles

• Strong analytical skills to synthesize diverse data into clear narratives

• Excellent organizational skills to manage multiple projects

• Effective communication and consultative skills for diverse client audiences

• Familiarity with G-Suite, intermediate PowerPoint and Excel skills

• Experience in foodservice is a plus




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Base salary range: $70,000-$75,000

• Annual performance-based bonus: 7%

• Actual compensation based on skills, experience, and location




6. Benefits & Perks:

• Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance

• 401(k) with company match

• Annual bonus

• Unlimited paid time off

• Wellness reimbursement and access to headquarters gym

Title: Associate
Description:
Job Description

RBC Dominion Securities located in downtown Toronto, ON is currently seeking a licensed Associate for a Chairman's Council team. The successful candidate will possess 3-5 years of industry experience, a passion for service excellence, and thrive in a fast paced team environment. This individual will be responsible for supporting the work of the Portfolio Manager, Associate Portfolio Manager and Associate Wealth Advisor. They will also support trade operations for clients and have the unique opportunity for mentorship, and skill development as they work in a supporting role on the team. This is an exciting, challenging role in one of the largest Branches in the system. The ability to learn and grow in this role is vast.

What you will do:


Support client-facing team in preparing analyses and presentations
Follow-up on trading and monitoring
Maintain client accounts by reviewing daily activity online and on applicable reports
Utilize contact management system for daily task management
Provide world class client service to our valued clients
Investigate and resolve inquiries related to accounts and refer to the client-facing team where appropriate


What do you need to succeed?

Must-have


Canadian Investment Regulatory Exam, CIRE
Strong skills working with business applications/technology, including Microsoft Office Suite
High quality time management and organization skills
Demonstrated skills in providing world-class client service that will come across as professional, warm, and friendly to our clients
Exceptional verbal and written communication skills


Nice-to-have


Knowledge of RBC Dominion Securities’ systems and procedures
Experience in the securities industry is an asset
Business Degree with major in Finance


What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, to think progressively to keep growing, and to work together to deliver trusted advice that can help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other and collectively work hard to reach our potential, make a difference to our communities, and achieve

success that is mutual. You will also benefit from:


A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, commissions, and stock where applicable. The total expected compensation for this position is in the $50,000 - $100,000 range.
Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
An opportunity to make a difference and have a lasting impact on the lives of others
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
The chance to work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
A world-class training program in financial services

Account Management, Customer Success, Decision Making, Financial Regulation, Group Problem Solving, Interpersonal Relationships, Investment Risk Management

Title: Income Verification QA Analyst, Truework
Description:
About Checkr

Checkr is building the data platform to power safe and fair decisions. Over 140,000 companies and millions of people rely on Checkr for AI verification in the moments that matter most: getting a new job, a new place to live, a car ride, childcare, even a date. Customers include Uber, Pennymac, Airbnb, Doordash, Amazon, and Anthropic.

We’re a team that thrives on solving complex problems with innovative solutions that advance our mission. Checkr is recognized on Forbes Cloud 100 2025 List and is a Y Combinator 2024 Breakthrough Company.

About The Role

Truework, recently acquired by Checkr, provides access to crucial employment, income information, and asset information needed for mortgage loans, apartment rentals, background checks, and more. We are disrupting a $5B+ industry, dominated by legacy incumbents and outdated processes, with a better product and intense customer focus. Within Checkr, Truework operates independently, allowing rapid iteration while also being able to leverage the strengths and assets from our core business. Think of it like joining a startup within a startup!

As a QA Specialist, you will safeguard the integrity of Truework verifications by auditing HR Reports and Outreach workflows for accuracy, completeness, compliance, and professionalism. You’ll apply rulesets used in Report QA (identity/entity matching, data consistency/completeness, correctness), and assess adherence to Smart Outreach cadences across phone, email, and fax. Your work will help to directly improve verifier satisfaction, reduce rework, and support on-time delivery targets.

What You’ll Do


Audit completed verification reports against Report QA rulesets, validating identity and entity matching, field completeness, internal consistency, and reasonableness of data.
Execute Outreach QA reviews on a randomized sample of manual-source verifications (email, fax, phone, third-party providers), ensuring required actions are logged, follow-ups meet cadence expectations, and communications meet tone and professionalism standards.
Use the Internal Validator findings to confirm required fields are populated (e.g. employment status with corresponding dates), and flag illogical data (e.g. inactive target with missing end date).
Document QA outcomes precisely (pass, soft fail, hard fail, warning), citing evidence and rule references to enable rapid fixes and learning feedback loops.
Collaborate with Outreach and Transcription team leadership to resolve discrepancies, coach to standard work, and reduce repeat errors over time.
Support SLA and KPI attainment by promptly reviewing queued items and escalating risk on time-sensitive verifications.


What You Bring


Experience with or interest in QA, operations auditing, or compliance within background screening, VOE/VOI lending, HR operations, or similar data-sensitive environments.
Working knowledge of the Truework verification workflows, including manual outreach methods and transcription best practices; familiarity with Report QA concepts (rulesets and outcomes).
Detail orientation with structured note-taking and the ability to cite evidence for each QA finding; strong written communication and professional etiquette.
Analytical judgment to distinguish between soft fails and hard fails, escalate appropriately, and recommend course corrections.
Exposure to Looker-based sampling and/or dashboards for audit selection and metrics tracking.
Understanding of FCRA-adjacent practices and secure data handling expectations.
An A-player mindset with a strong bias for action: you raise the bar, move with urgency, stay resilient through ambiguity, and take ownership to deliver meaningful outcomes.


Pay Transparency Disclosure

We use geographic cost of labor as an input to develop ranges for our roles and as such, each location where we hire may have a different range. If this role is remote, we have listed the top to the bottom of the possible range, but we will specify the target range for an exact location when you are selected for a recruiting discussion. For more information on our compensation philosophy, see our website.

Hourly Pay Range (Nashville, TN)

$18.27 - $23.08 USD

What We Offer


A fast-paced and collaborative environment
Learning and development allowance
Competitive cash and equity compensation, and opportunity for advancement
100% medical, dental, and vision coverage
Up to $25K reimbursement for fertility, adoption, and parental planning services
Flexible PTO policy
Monthly wellness stipend


At Checkr, we believe an in office work environment strengthens collaboration, drives innovation, and encourages connection. Our hub locations are Denver, CO; San Francisco, CA; Nashville, TN; and Santiago, Chile. Individuals are expected to work from the office 3+ days a week. In-office perks are provided, such as lunch five times a week, a commuter stipend, and an abundance of snacks and beverages. A relocation stipend may be available for those willing to relocate to a Checkr hub location.

Equal Employment Opportunities at Checkr

Checkr is committed to building the best product and company, which requires hiring talented and qualified individuals with a diverse set of perspectives and lived experiences. Checkr believes in hiring people of all backgrounds, including those whose histories are impacted by the justice system in accordance with local, state, and/or federal laws, including the San Francisco’s Fair Chance Ordinance.

Applicant Privacy Policy

If you are a California resident or are located in Alberta or British Columbia, our Applicant Privacy Policy applies to our collection and processing of your personal information when you apply for a role with us or otherwise participate in our recruitment process.


Legitimate Checkr emails will always include our official domain name after the @ symbol (e.g., name@checkr.com or name@ext.checkr.com).

Title: Administrative & Project Coordinator
Description:
About The Company

The American Heart Association has been a leader in cardiovascular health since its founding in 1924. Over nearly a century, the organization has made significant strides in reducing cardiovascular disease deaths by half, demonstrating a steadfast commitment to improving public health. With a focus on science, policy, and community engagement, the American Heart Association strives to create a world where longer, healthier lives are accessible to all. The organization offers a dynamic and inclusive work environment that emphasizes professional development, work-life balance, and community impact. Its mission-driven culture encourages innovation, collaboration, and dedication to advancing heart and brain health across diverse populations.

About The Role

The American Heart Association is seeking a dedicated and detail-oriented Strategic Initiatives Coordinator for Clinical Studies. This full-time, benefits-eligible position can be performed remotely, with a preferred location within the eastern time zone. The role is grant-funded with an initial duration of approximately one year, with potential for extension based on funding and organizational needs. The Coordinator will provide essential administrative support and project coordination within the Clinical Studies Team, facilitating effective communication, documentation, and progress tracking on various initiatives. This position offers an excellent opportunity for individuals interested in health-related research and project management, supporting the organization’s mission to advance cardiovascular health through innovative clinical studies.

Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in business administration, healthcare management, or a related field (preferred)
Minimum of three (3) years of administrative support experience or relevant roles
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook)
Strong organizational and process management skills
Excellent written and verbal communication abilities
Experience with project management tools and software applications
Knowledge of business English, spelling, grammar, and punctuation
Ability to work independently with minimal supervision and collaboratively within a team
Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail
Customer service-oriented mindset with excellent interpersonal skills

Title: Research Data Specialist
Description:
The University of Mississippi — fondly referred to as Ole Miss — stands as a premier public research institution with a proud legacy of academic distinction. We are devoted to nurturing a vibrant, inclusive community where every member — student, faculty, and staff — can achieve their fullest potential.

Join the Ole Miss Family — Build Your Legacy Here!

Department

Office for Research & Econ Development (ORED) empowers University of Mississippi research that advances knowledge, addresses social challenges, and propels economic growth locally and nationally. We support researchers and creative scholars, foster collaboration, and facilitate the translation of innovation into progress. As our legacy home-grown information system comes to the end of its life, we have licensed and are implementing Cayuse, a state-of-the art, cloud-based enterprise system to manage the increasingly complex landscape of research compliance and grant administration. At the same time, UM is in the process of migrating the HR and payroll enterprise to Workday, the student information system to Ellucian Banner, and the faculty activity reporting system to Watermark Faculty Success. A central data warehouse and analytics and reporting tool called Insights will also be implemented to bring in data from these and other campus systems to enable faster insights and more informed decisions-making campus wide. Meanwhile, the federal research funding environment is rapidly changing, creating new challenges and opportunities for responding to new priorities, competing for grants, and thriving in a shifting regulatory environment.

Job Summary

The Office for Research and Economic Development (ORED) at the University of Mississippi is seeking an experienced data and systems professional who is interested in applying technical expertise to support a growing research enterprise. Working alongside research administrators, this position will design, develop, and maintain data solutions that improve institutional decision-making and strengthen research administration. Initially, the position will focus on supporting the implementation of Cayuse, including system configuration, data management, reporting, technical support, integration with Workday, and migration of legacy research administration data into the University's enterprise data environment. As these systems mature, the position will expand to developing institutional datasets, dashboards, reports, predictive models, and other analytical tools that help leadership monitor research performance, identify opportunities, improve operational effectiveness, strengthen regulatory compliance, and support strategic investment in research.

Job Description

Examples of Work Performed:


Data Engineering: Develop Extract-Transform-Load (ETL) scripts to extract data or clean data, automate tasks, supports business processes
Liaise with vendors and central IT professionals to identify, propose, develop, and test integrations across enterprise systems
Develop data dashboards and reports for ORED leaders and campus research stakeholders
Communicate with research administration subject matter experts to define business needs and propose data-driven solutions
Interact with technical experts to identify and implement cross-system integrations (e.g., moving data from Cayuse to Workday and vice versa)
Collaborate across institutional units to leverage data resources and subject matter expertise in support of analytical and reporting needs
Use data extraction tools (SQL/NoSQL/Python) to develop queries to answer research and analysis questions
Develop and implement data solutions and analyses to help leaders and stakeholders understand, comply with, and thrive under the large, evolving, and complex federal rules and regulations related to grants and contracts.


Job Responsibilities

Bachelor's degree—preferably in Data Science, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related discipline. If the candidate holds a bachelor’s in an unrelated field but has substantial experience in related roles, they are encouraged to apply and should explain their qualifications and expertise as it relates to this position in their cover letter.

Applicants should submit a resume and a brief narrative (no more than two pages) describing the following:


A complex data, systems, or reporting challenge you personally helped solve, including your specific role, the technologies you used, and the outcome.
Why this position at the University of Mississippi interests you and how your experience would contribute to advancing the University's research enterprise.


The strongest applications will demonstrate thoughtful analysis, technical depth, and an understanding of how data can be used to improve organizational decision-making.

Education Qualifications

Bachelor's (Required)

Experience

Relevant experience | 6 Years | Required

Compensation

$75,545.60 - $113,318.40

Interview Requirement

Interview Requirements: Any candidate who is called for an interview must notify the Department of Equal Opportunity/Regulatory Compliance in writing of any reasonable accommodation needed prior to the date of the interview.

EEO Statement

The University of Mississippi provides equal opportunity in any employment practice, education program, or education activity to all qualified persons. The University complies with all applicable laws regarding equal opportunity and does not unlawfully discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment based upon race, color, gender, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, religion, national origin, ethnicity, citizenship, age, disability, military status, protected veteran status, genetic information, or any other legally protected status.

Background Check Statement

The University of Mississippi is committed to providing a safe campus community. UM conducts background investigations for applicants being considered for employment. Background investigations include a criminal history record check, and when appropriate, a financial (credit) report or driving history check.

**Primary Function:**

Detail-oriented and analytical Data Analyst to support both sales

operations and project management functions within a manufacturing

environment. This position is responsible for collecting, organizing,

analyzing, and maintaining data used for project tracking, sales

reporting, forecasting, estimating, and operational decision-making. The

ideal candidate will work with sales, project managers, operations, and

leadership to ensure data accuracy, identify trends and red flags, and

provide actionable insights.

**Essential Duties and Responsibilities:**


Perform front-end data analysis for sales and project management


activities


Develop, maintain, and improve sales tracking and project management


templates


Monitor project performance metrics, estimates at completion (EAC),


budgets, schedules, and forecasting data


Collect, organize, validate, and summarize data from multiple


departments and systems


Ensure all required project and sales data is complete, accurate,


and submitted on time


Coordinate with project managers, sales teams, production, quality,


engineering, operations, and leadership to obtain missing or

incomplete information


Identify data discrepancies, trends, risks, and operational red


flags and communicate findings to management


Generate reports, dashboards, and visual summaries to support


business decisions


Maintain data integrity and support ongoing data management


processes


Analyze sales performance, project costs, productivity metrics, and


operational KPIs


Assist with process improvement initiatives related to reporting,


tracking, and data collection


Support management with forecasting, budgeting, and operational


reporting needs


Create and maintain documentation for reporting processes and data


procedures


Assist with implementation and optimization of tracking tools and


reporting systems

**Minimum Qualifications/Experience:**


Associates or Bachelors degree in Business, Data Analytics, Finance,


Engineering, Project Management, or related field preferred


2+ years of experience in data analysis, project coordination, sales


support, or manufacturing operations preferred


Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills with


high attention to detail


Advanced Microsoft Excel skills, including formulas, pivot tables,


and data analysis tools


Experience with Microsoft Power BI or other reporting and data


visualization platforms preferred


Experience with ERP systems, project tracking software, or


manufacturing databases is a plus


Ability to manage multiple priorities, meet deadlines, and


communicate effectively with teams


Ability to identify trends, inconsistencies, risks, and process


improvement opportunities


Ability to handle confidential business and financial information


**Preferred Skills**


Dashboard creation and reporting automation
Forecasting, KPI tracking, and estimate-at-completion (EAC)


reporting


Manufacturing or project-based business experience

The physical demands and work environment described here are

representative of the conditions that must be met by an employee to

Successfully Perform The Essential Functions Of This Job. Regular,

predictable, and on-site attendance is an essential function of this

position. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals

with disabilities to perform the essential functions of this job.

Premier Technology is a federal contractor, as such, this position will

work on government contracts and requires that you are a US Citizen (not

just authorized to work in the US).

**Physical Demands:**

While pe forming the duties of this Job, the employee is regularly

required to sit in a stationary position for extended periods of time

while working at a computer. The employee is frequently required to walk

and talk or hear. The employee is occasionally required to stand. The

employee must frequently lift and/or move up to 10 pounds and

occasionally lift and/or move up to 25 pounds. Specific vision abilities

required by this job include close vision, color vision and the ability

to adjust focus.

**Work Environment:**

The noise level in the work environment is usually quiet typical of what

is generally found in an office setting. The employee will occasionally

find it necessary to enter a manufacturing environment. The

manufacturing environment is typical of that type of setting employees

may be exposed to inclement weather, loud noises, tripping hazards; and

there may be exposure to sparks, welding arc, open flame, and moving

equipment.

Medical, Dental, Vision - 100% Employee Paid Coverage

Company Paid Life Insurance

Flexi

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
Turner Supply Company is hiring for a Business Data Analyst in Mobile, AL!

About Turner Supply Company

Founded in 1905, Turner Supply Company is a family-owned industrial distributor serving manufacturers nationwide. Customers rely on Turner for a comprehensive product assortment, competitive pricing, expert problem-solving, and cost-reduction programs that improve operational performance.

Our leadership team actively champions our core values—Empowerment, Development, Execution, Teamwork, Innovation, and Leadership—values that helped Affiliated Distributors recognize Turner Supply as a Top Workplace in 2022 ⭐.

📍 Headquartered in Mobile, Alabama, Turner operates 8 locations across the United States.

Total Rewards

Turner offers a competitive pay and benefits package, including:

·        Medical, Dental, and Vision coverage

·        Wellness Program

·        Company-paid Life Insurance

·        Company-funded Health Reimbursement Arrangement (HRA)

401(k) with a generous company match




Job Title:                        Business Data Analyst

Department:                  Information Technology

FLSA Status:                  Salaried Exempt            

Direct Report:               VP, Technology & Procurement

Supervisory

Responsibility:             None

Location:                      Mobile, AL

                                      Onsite M-F (7:30am – 4:30am CT)




Job Summary:

The Business Data Analyst will be responsible for collecting, processing, and analyzing data to provide valuable insights and support strategic decision-making within the organization. They will work with large datasets, employ statistical methods, and utilize data visualization tools to communicate findings effectively. They will analyze and interpret business data including sales, e-commerce, procurement and operations information to increase sales/profitability. The role requires a keen analytical mindset, attention to detail, and the ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams.

Responsibilities:

Data Collection and Processing:
Gather and collect data from various sources within the wholesale distribution system
Usage of APIs to extract, transform, and load data from external sources
Apply API integration tools and platforms to streamline data retrieval and updates
Clean, preprocess, and organize data to ensure accuracy and completeness
Data Analysis:
Perform statistical analysis on sales, e-commerce, inventory, and operational data to identify trends and patterns
Generate reports and dashboards to communicate key insights to stakeholders
Utilize data visualization tools to present findings in a clear and understandable manner.
System Administration:
Perform standard system administration duties for several key systems (ERP, PIM applications, eCommerce applications & websites)
Create and maintain product data exports as needed for web site and other uses.




 

Support Forecasting and Predictive Modeling:
Review & develop models to forecast sales, demand, and inventory levels throughout Turner Supply Company
Support implementing predictive analytics to optimize inventory management and procurement processes
Performance Metrics:
Report and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) that evaluate business performance
Collaborate with relevant teams to identify areas for improvement and efficiency
Business Strategy Support:
Work closely with management to understand business objectives and provide data-driven recommendations
Contribute to strategic planning by offering insights based on data analysis
Data Quality Assurance:
Implement measures to ensure data integrity and accuracy
Conduct regular audits of data sources and processes
Collaboration:
Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including sales, operations, and finance, to gather relevant data and insights
Communicate effectively with non-technical stakeholders to convey findings and recommendations




Required Qualifications:

Bachelor's degree in a related field (e.g., Data Science, Business Analytics) preferred
3+ years relevant data analysis experience
3+ years relevant experience using ERP applications and data

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Ability to work independently and collaborate effectively in a team

·        Ability to learn and apply various software tools/systems used at Turner Supply Company, such as AD EnterWorks PIM, Optimizely B2B Web Platform, INFOR SX.e/CSD, Google Merchant, Amazon Seller Central, Acenda Marketplace Manager, epaCUBE

·        Self-starter with motivation to work with minimal direction and supervision

·        Excellent written, communication and presentation skills

·        Fluency in English (reading, writing and speaking)

Prior experience with Distributor and/or Manufacturing organizations
Experience with advanced statistical analysis and machine learning
Knowledge of supply chain and logistics

Turner Supply Company is hiring for a Business Data Analyst in Mobile, AL!

About Turner Supply Company

Founded in 1905, Turner Supply Company is a family-owned industrial distributor serving manufacturers nationwide. Customers rely on Turner for a comprehensive product assortment, competitive pricing, expert problem-solving, and cost-reduction programs that improve operational performance.

Our leadership team actively champions our core values—Empowerment, Development, Execution, Teamwork, Innovation, and Leadership—values that helped Affiliated Distributors recognize Turner Supply as a Top Workplace in 2022 ⭐.

📍 Headquartered in Mobile, Alabama, Turner operates 8 locations across the United States.

Total Rewards

Turner offers a competitive pay and benefits package, including:

·        Medical, Dental, and Vision coverage

·        Wellness Program

·        Company-paid Life Insurance

·        Company-funded Health Reimbursement Arrangement (HRA)

401(k) with a generous company match


Job Title:                        Business Data Analyst

Department:                  Information Technology

FLSA Status:                  Salaried Exempt            

Direct Report:               VP, Technology & Procurement

Supervisory

Responsibility:             None

Location:                      Mobile, AL

                                      Onsite M-F (7:30am – 4:30am CT)


Job Summary:

The Business Data Analyst will be responsible for collecting, processing, and analyzing data to provide valuable insights and support strategic decision-making within the organization. They will work with large datasets, employ statistical methods, and utilize data visualization tools to communicate findings effectively. They will analyze and interpret business data including sales, e-commerce, procurement and operations information to increase sales/profitability. The role requires a keen analytical mindset, attention to detail, and the ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams.

Responsibilities:

  1. Data Collection and Processing:
  • Gather and collect data from various sources within the wholesale distribution system
  • Usage of APIs to extract, transform, and load data from external sources
  • Apply API integration tools and platforms to streamline data retrieval and updates
  • Clean, preprocess, and organize data to ensure accuracy and completeness
  1. Data Analysis:
  • Perform statistical analysis on sales, e-commerce, inventory, and operational data to identify trends and patterns
  • Generate reports and dashboards to communicate key insights to stakeholders
  • Utilize data visualization tools to present findings in a clear and understandable manner.
  1. System Administration:
  • Perform standard system administration duties for several key systems (ERP, PIM applications, eCommerce applications & websites)
  • Create and maintain product data exports as needed for web site and other uses.


 

  1. Support Forecasting and Predictive Modeling:
  • Review & develop models to forecast sales, demand, and inventory levels throughout Turner Supply Company
  • Support implementing predictive analytics to optimize inventory management and procurement processes
  1. Performance Metrics:
  • Report and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) that evaluate business performance
  • Collaborate with relevant teams to identify areas for improvement and efficiency
  1. Business Strategy Support:
  • Work closely with management to understand business objectives and provide data-driven recommendations
  • Contribute to strategic planning by offering insights based on data analysis
  1. Data Quality Assurance:
  • Implement measures to ensure data integrity and accuracy
  • Conduct regular audits of data sources and processes
  1. Collaboration:
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including sales, operations, and finance, to gather relevant data and insights
  • Communicate effectively with non-technical stakeholders to convey findings and recommendations


Required Qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree in a related field (e.g., Data Science, Business Analytics) preferred
  • 3+ years relevant data analysis experience
  • 3+ years relevant experience using ERP applications and data
  • Proficiency in data analysis tools such as Excel, Python, R, SQL, and data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI)
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Ability to work independently and collaborate effectively in a team

·        Ability to learn and apply various software tools/systems used at Turner Supply Company, such as AD EnterWorks PIM, Optimizely B2B Web Platform, INFOR SX.e/CSD, Google Merchant, Amazon Seller Central, Acenda Marketplace Manager, epaCUBE

·        Self-starter with motivation to work with minimal direction and supervision

·        Excellent written, communication and presentation skills

·        Fluency in English (reading, writing and speaking)


Preferred Skills:

  • Prior experience with Distributor and/or Manufacturing organizations
  • Experience with advanced statistical analysis and machine learning
  • Knowledge of supply chain and logistics
  • Familiarity with e-commerce applications & ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) systems such as: Infor SX.e, Infor CloudSuite Distribution, Optimizely, Acenda, AD EnterWorks
  • Experience with SOAP & RESTful APIs and their application in extracting relevant business data

Supervisory Responsibility: This position does not have direct report responsibility.


Work Environment: This job operates in a professional office environment as well as warehouse and outside sales. This role routinely uses standard office equipment such as computers, phones, photocopiers, filing cabinets, and fax machines.

This job could require being able to lift 20 lbs.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Permanent Full Time

We are seeking a highly analytical and solutions-oriented Data Analyst to support Workplace Benefits and Retirement.

This role offers the opportunity to work closely with cross-functional teams and our Field Sales and Service partners. The successful candidate will possess a deep curiosity for understanding data, adept at identifying anomalies, investigating discrepancies, and the ability to design and implement efficient data-driven solutions.

In addition, the Data Analyst will build and evolve analytical reporting using Power BI, Excel, and SQL. By digging into performance results, identifying key trends, and translating complex data into clear insights, the role provides the information needed for data‑driven action to help move our business grow.

What You Will Do


Analyze, interpret, and validate large data volume to identify trends, inconsistencies, and anomalies
Provide technical and functional support on complex compensation programs, support decision-making with sound judgement backed by data, and mentor team
Write and optimize SQL queries to support data retrieval and reporting
Maintain tools and scripts using VB.NET to support business needs
Translate complex datasets into clear, compelling insights and business narratives
Work with stakeholders to improve data availability, quality, and drive automation
Ensure data accuracy and integrity through validation, reconciliation, and quality checks
Help resolve issues, perform analysis, and support application enhancements
Document data flows, models, and solutions to ensure sustainability


What You Will Bring


3+ years of hands-on experience with:
Data modeling concepts and tools
SQL (query development, optimization)
VB.NET
Power Automate
Power Apps
Power BI
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to break down complex problems into manageable components
Ability to model, structure, validate, and document complex datasets to support accurate and reliable decision‑making
Experience improving manual operational processes, including the ability to streamline or automate validation workflows
Proven experience identifying errors, inconsistencies, and anomalies in data
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment
A proactive mindset — you seek to understand the purpose behind requests and consistently look for opportunities to improve processes and outcomes


The base salary for this position is between $65,800 - $109,700 annually. This represents base salary only and does not represent other variable compensation components of our total compensation ( i.e. annual bonus, commission etc). If you are selected to move forward in our recruitment process, your recruiter will be able to discuss additional details of our total rewards program with you.

Career opportunities will be open a minimum of 5 business days from the date of posting, closing dates will vary depending on the search activity. All applications received will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

Grow with Canada Life

We’re united by a shared purpose: to improve the financial, physical and mental well-being of Canadians. Our company is trusted by 1 in 3 Canadians and contributes to the strength of communities across the country.

We’re looking for people who live our values everyday: we step up, we do the right thing, and we deliver – for our customers, communities and each other. Are you someone who always strives to do the right thing, who steps up for themselves and others, and who delivers with impact? Then we want to hear from you!

What we offer:

We’re committed to supporting our employees through every stage of their career. Here’s what you can expect as a full-time or part-time permanent team member:


Career Development: Opportunities for career advancement, access to industry-leading learning programs and up to $2,000 annually towards education reimbursement.
Health & Wellness: Flexible health and dental benefits, plus a $5,000 mental health benefit to support your well-being.
Time Off: In addition to regular vacation and personal days, we support community involvement with a volunteer day. 
Financial Security: Company-matching pension plan, share ownership program and additional investment options.
Rewards and Recognition: Employee recognition programs, service milestone celebrations, employee discounts and more!
Emphasis on Community: We provide a workplace where employees feel connected and supported through Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), mentorship programs, social clubs and events.


Learn more about Canada Life.

We’re committed to removing barriers and ensuring equal access to employment. Applicants requiring reasonable accommodation during the application process may contact talentacquisitioncanada@canadalife.com. All information provided will be handled in accordance with applicable laws and Canada Life policies.

Canada Life would like to thank all applicants, however only those who qualify for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Analyst 1
Description:
Come join Pattison Food Group Ltd., a Jim Pattison business, and Canada’s largest Western-based provider of food and health products.

Pattison Food Group Ltd. is a certified Great Place to Work® and consists of Save-On-Foods and other well-known grocery banners. With nearly 300 retail locations, we proudly employ nearly 30,000 team members.

We have an exciting opportunity for a full-time Data Analyst 1 to join our Data & Analytics team in Langley, BC.

Reporting to the Lead Data Engineer, the Data Analyst 1 role supports business decision-making through data analysis, reporting, and insight generation. This position works closely with business stakeholders to understand key questions, translate them into analytical approaches, and deliver clear, actionable insights.

This role contributes to a range of initiatives across areas such as customer analytics, loyalty, marketing, retail operations, and merchandising. Using large and complex datasets, the Data Analyst 1 helps identify trends, measure outcomes, support experimentation, and uncover opportunities to drive business value.

You will be responsible for: (Responsibilities)


Partnering with business stakeholders to understand priorities, business questions, processes, and KPIs.
Analyzing data to generate insights that support decision-making and continuous improvement.
Building reports, dashboards, and visualizations that communicate findings clearly and effectively to stakeholders.
Maintaining dashboards, reports, and other data assets in production to support their ongoing accuracy, reliability, and performance.
Writing SQL queries to extract, manipulate, and prepare data for analysis and reporting.
Using Python and notebook-based tools for exploratory analysis, data wrangling, and statistical work where needed.
Supporting the design, execution, and interpretation of experiments such as A/B testing and other hypothesis-driven analyses.
Validating, troubleshooting, and improving data used in reporting and analysis.
Working with large datasets in cloud-based or modern analytics environments.
Supporting the governance, administration, and cost optimization of data visualization and reporting tools.
Collaborating with data and cross-functional partners to support reporting, analysis, and business needs.
Identifying trends, opportunities, and actionable recommendations through thoughtful analysis.
Building knowledge of core datasets, reporting logic, and business context within the assigned analytics domain.
Supporting broader Data & Analytics team priorities and cross-functional projects as required.



You have: (Requirements)


A degree in a relevant field such as statistics, mathematics, computer science, engineering, economics, or another quantitative discipline.
3+ years of relevant background in data analytics, business analytics, reporting, or a related field.
Working knowledge of SQL for querying and transforming data.
Familiarity with Power BI, Databricks AI/BI, Tableau, or similar visualization and reporting tools.
Ability to build dashboards or reports that support business decision-making.
Exposure to large or complex datasets.
Working knowledge of Python for data analysis is preferred.
Exposure to experimentation, test design, or statistical analysis is an asset.
Familiarity with data modeling or data quality validation is an asset.
Exposure to cloud data environments such as Azure, AWS, or GCP is an asset.
Experience working with Databricks is an asset.



You are: (Competencies)


Able to translate business needs into practical data solutions.
Able to translate data into clear, practical insights for non-technical stakeholders.
An effective written and verbal communicator.
Detail-oriented and committed to quality.
Able to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Self-motivated and comfortable working both independently and collaboratively.
Curious, proactive, and eager to learn new tools, datasets, and business contexts.



What we offer:

In addition to a highly competitive salary, we offer a great range of benefits, a company pension plan, free parking, exclusive team member offers, and opportunities for development and career progression.

If you’re looking to join a team that puts people first, you’re in the right place. Apply online today!

Compensation Details:

$75,509.00 - $94,387.00

The compensation offered for this position will take into consideration location, education, skills, experience and other factors.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst II
Description:
The next part of your journey is right around the corner — with The Standard.

A genuine desire to make a difference in the lives of others is the foundation for everything we do. With a customer-first mindset and an intentional focus on building strong teams, we’ve been able to uphold our legacy of financial stability while investing in new, innovative technologies that support the needs of our customers. Our high-performance culture focused on operational excellence thrives thanks to remarkable people united by compassion and a customer-first commitment. Are you ready to make a difference?

Job Summary

The Business Intelligence Analyst supports data‑driven decision‑making by analyzing complex business problems, identifying trends, and translating data into clear insights for non‑technical leaders. This role develops reporting requirements, builds analytical solutions, and partners with internal teams to deliver accurate, meaningful information. The analyst contributes to process improvements, supports project execution, and applies BI best practices across data management, analysis, and visualization.

Key Responsibilities


Analyze business problems using internal and external data to identify trends, patterns, and key drivers.
Translate business needs into reporting specifications and develop solutions for presenting information clearly and effectively.
Build and maintain queries, reports, and dashboards to support projects and operational decision‑making.
Partner with internal teams to research and resolve data‑related inquiries of moderate complexity.
Contribute to continuous improvement efforts, including process enhancements, team knowledge‑sharing, and best‑practice development.


Skills And Background You’ll Need

Education: High school diploma or equivalent required. B.A. preferred.

Experience: Minimum of 2 years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or a related field.


Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel.
Basic to moderate experience developing SQL queries and procedures.
Foundational understanding of reporting automation and data modeling concepts.
Experience with R, Python, Azure, or similar analytical tools.
Familiarity with data visualization tools such as Power BI or DAX Studio.
Working knowledge of Agile or Lean methodologies.
Ability to apply data quality concepts to ensure accuracy and consistency.


Key Behaviors of a Successful Candidate


Adaptability: Adjusts quickly to changing priorities and sees possibilities in evolving circumstances.
Collaboration: Builds strong working relationships and partners effectively across teams.
Analytical Thinking: Uses sound reasoning to evaluate alternatives, solve problems, and develop new approaches.


Why Join The Standard?

We have built an enduring legacy of stability, financial strength and exceptional customer service through the contributions of the service-oriented people who choose to work at The Standard. To ensure we can attract and retain the best talent, when you join The Standard you can expect:


A rich benefits package including medical, dental, vision and a 401(k) plan with matching company contributions
An annual incentive bonus plan
Generous paid time off including 11 holidays, 2 wellness days, and 8 volunteer hours annually — PTO increases with tenure
A supportive, responsive management approach and opportunities for career growth and advancement
Paid parental leave and adoption/surrogacy assistance
An employee giving program that double matches your donations to eligible nonprofits and schools


In addition to the competitive salary range below, our employee-focused benefits support work-life balance. Learn more about working at The Standard.


Eligibility to participate in an incentive program is subject to the rules governing the program and plan. Any award depends on a variety of factors including individual and organizational performance.


The actual compensation for this role will be based on a combination of education and experience, knowledge and skills, position budget, internal equity, and market data.

Salary Range:

$66,000.00 - $111,750.00

Positions will be posted for at least 5 days from original posting date.

Standard Insurance Company, The Standard Life Insurance Company of New York, Standard Retirement Services, Inc., StanCorp Mortgage Investors, LLC, StanCorp Investment Advisers, Inc., and American Heritage Life Insurance Company and American Heritage Service Company, marketed as The Standard, are Affirmative Action/Equal Opportunity employers. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, sex, national origin, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability or veteran status or any other condition protected by federal, state or local law. Except where precluded by state or federal law, The Standard will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance. The Standard offers a drug- and alcohol-free work environment where possession, manufacture, transfer, offer, use of or being impaired by an illegal substance while on The Standard's property, or in other cases which the company believes might affect operations, safety or reputation of the company is prohibited. The Standard requires a criminal background investigation and employment, education and licensing verification as a condition of employment. After any conditional offer of employment is made, the background check will include an individualized assessment based on the applicant’s specific record and the duties and requirements of the specific job. Applicants will be provided an opportunity to explain and correct background information. All employees of The Standard must be bondable.
