Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Job Number: R0244112

Data Scientist

The Opportunity:

As a data scientist, you’re excited at the prospect of unlocking the secrets held by a data set, and you’re fascinated by the possibilities presented by IoT, machine learning, and artificial intelligence. In an increasingly connected world, massive amounts of structured and unstructured data open new opportunities. As a data scientist at Booz Allen, you can help turn these complex data sets into useful information to solve global challenges. Across private and public sectors, from fraud detection to cancer research, to national intelligence, we need you to help find the answers in the data.

On our team, you’ll use your analytical skills and data science knowledge to create real-world impact. You’ll work closely with your clients to understand their questions and needs, and then dig into their data-rich environments to find the pieces of their information puzzle. You’ll develop algorithms and systems and use the right combination of tools and frameworks to turn sets of disparate data points into objective answers to help clients make informed decisions. Ultimately, you’ll provide a deep understanding of the data, what it all means, and how it can be used. Work with us as we use data science for good.

Join us. The world can’t wait.

You Have:


3+ years of experience with data exploration, data cleaning, data analysis, data visualization, or data mining
3+ years of experience with statistical and general-purpose programming languages such as Python, for data analysis
2+ years of experience creating and maintaining report visuals, structure, and processes with BI dashboard tools and development such as Tableau, Qlik Sense, or Power BI
Ability to analyze structured and unstructured data sources
Ability to develop predictive data models, quantitative analyses, and visualization of targeted data sources
Ability to travel up to 25% of the time
Secret clearance
HS diploma or GED



Nice If You Have:


2+ years of experience working in or with the DoD or military
2+ years of experience developing in React and Typescript
Experience working with Palantir Foundry
Knowledge of Agile or Scrum processes
TS/SCI clearance
Master’s degree



Clearance:

Applicants selected will be subject to a security investigation and may need to meet eligibility requirements for access to classified information; Secret clearance is required.

Compensation

At Booz Allen, we celebrate your contributions, provide you with opportunities and choices, and support your total well-being. Our offerings include health, life, disability, financial, and retirement benefits, as well as paid leave, professional development, tuition assistance, work-life programs, and dependent care. Our recognition awards program acknowledges employees for exceptional performance and superior demonstration of our values. Full-time and part-time employees working at least 20 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in Booz Allen’s benefit programs. Individuals that do not meet the threshold are only eligible for select offerings, not inclusive of health benefits. We encourage you to learn more about our total benefits by visiting the Resource page on our Careers site and reviewing Our Employee Benefits page.

Salary at Booz Allen is determined by various factors, including but not limited to location, the individual’s particular combination of education, knowledge, skills, competencies, and experience, as well as contract-specific affordability and organizational requirements. The projected compensation range for this position is $77,600.00 to $176,000.00 (annualized USD). The estimate displayed represents the typical salary range for this position and is just one component of Booz Allen’s total compensation package for employees. This posting will close within 90 days from the Posting Date.

Identity Statement

As part of the hiring process, we will ask you to complete an identity verification process that leverages advanced biometrics and artificial intelligence to ensure authenticity and protect against identity fraud. You are expected to be on camera during interviews and assessments. We reserve the right to take your picture to verify your identity and prevent fraud.

Candidate AI Usage Policy

AI is a part of our daily work at Booz Allen, and we are committed to the responsible and ethical use of AI tools. However, we want to ensure a fair candidate process based on your own skills and knowledge. As part of this commitment, the use of artificial intelligence (AI) or other tools to assist with responses during interviews (whether in-person or virtual) is prohibited unless permission is explicitly provided.

Work Model

Our people-first culture prioritizes the benefits of collaboration, whether it occurs in person or virtually. To support engagement and effective communication, employees working virtually are generally expected to have their cameras on during meetings.


Remote: If this position is listed as remote, there may still be occasions when you are required to work in person at a Booz Allen or customer facility.
Hybrid: If this position is listed as hybrid, you will be expected to work from a Booz Allen facility frequently, in alignment with leadership expectations and the needs of the role. You may also be required to work from or visit a customer facility.
Onsite: If this position is listed as onsite, work will primarily be performed at a Booz Allen office or customer facility, where employees will collaborate directly with colleagues and customers as required by the role.



Commitment to Non-Discrimination

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to disability, status as a protected veteran or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, local, or international law.

Title: Data Analyst AS6462
Description:
Campus

OSU-Stillwater

Contact Name & Email

Larry Burns, larry.burns@okstate.edu

Work Schedule

Monday through Friday, 8:00 am - 5:00 pm, with occasional evenings and weekends.

Appointment Length

Regular Continuous/Until Further Notice

Hiring Range

$47,000 - $69,000

Salary

Special Instructions to Applicants

OSU will not sponsor applicants for work visas for this position.

Please submit a resume and list of references.

About This Position


The position will play a vital role in supporting institutional decision-making and will be heavily involved with budget, finance and human resource data across the OSU System to deliver high-quality analytics that service leadership.
In addition, the Data Analyst position includes the following responsibilities:
Develops and maintains data extraction programs, prepares analytical reports, and applies statistical and mathematical models to support institutional decision-making at Oklahoma State University.
Works closely with OSU administrators, faculty, and external agencies to deliver timely and accurate institutional data.
Assists in developing and maintaining ePrint and other specialized reports available to colleges and other offices across campus.
Research and develop statistical models in response to OSU management objectives.
Develop and implement recommendations for maintaining efficient and streamlined data extraction programs.

Required Qualifications


Bachelor's Business, Mathematics, Computer Science, MIS, Statistics, Education, or a related technology field. (degree must be conferred on or before agreed upon start date)
Two (2) years of experience in analytics, statistical or mathematical modeling, or SQL programming.
Certifications, Registrations, and/or Licenses:
Proficiency in SQL or SAS for data extraction and analysis
Working knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Access, PowerPoint, Outlook)
Skills, Proficiencies, and/or Knowledge:
Excellent oral and written communication skills
Ability to maintain constructive relationships and collaborate effectively with diverse stakeholders
Strong organizational skills: ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously, work under pressure, meet deadlines, and prioritize appropriately
Self-motivated, conscientious, and dedicated with an eye for detail

Preferred Qualifications


Master's Business, Mathematics, Computer Science, MIS, Statistics, Education, or a related technology field
Microsoft SQL Server or Oracle SQL Developer experience
SAS Certified Base Programmer
SAS Certified Predictive Modeler using SAS Enterprise Miner 14
SAS Certified Platform Administrator

Title: Data Analytics Coordinator
Description:
Position Summary

The Data Analytics Coordinator plays a supporting role as a steward of PRPA's data warehouses, from data ongestion through to the creation and maintenance of reports. They are responsible for ensuring the quality and integrity of data used for various activities from infrastructure planning to business development.

Position Accountabilities


PRPA's primary data steward for all forms of data, including geospatial (GIS) data, which support day-to-day operations at PRPA.
Load data assets into PRPA’s data warehouses following best practices.
Support and maintain PRPA’s data warehouse.
Maintenance of reports and dashboards.
Training of PRPA staff in end user reporting tools available for accessing PRPA’s data.


WORKING CONDITIONS


Works predominantly in an office environment.
Regular, full-time position (37.5 hours per week). Regular working hours are Mondays to Fridays, 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM with 1 hour unpaid lunch break.


JOB SPECIFICATIONS

Formal Education


College diploma in Analytics, Computer Science Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or Information Technology.


Related Experience


Three to five years' experience in the industry


Other Requirements


Experience with reporting tools and software including SSRS, PowerBI, Informatica.
Experience with SSIS or other data ingestion and transformation tools.
Experience using Safe FME for processing geospatial data.
Experience with report writing, organizing, and presenting data in support of decision making
Demonstrates statistical and empirical analytical skills
Knowledge of relational database structures, theories, principles, and practices is an asset
Knowledge of ETL methodologies, T-SQL and ArcGIS is an asset
Familiar with the transportation and logistics industry
Demonstrates ability to learn new skills and acquire industry knowledge
Good spoken and written communication skills
Strong problem solving and organizational skills
Accuracy and attention to detail, with an analytical eye
Superior judgment and tact in dealing with sensitive issues and confidential information.
Demonstrates ability to manage multiple projects concurrently and to effectively handle changing priorities.
Enthusiastic and self-motivated. Works well individually as well as in a team environment.
Creative and intuitive thinker.
Demonstrates commitment to the Port’s Vision.
Resilient. Embraces change and thrives on change.
Proficient in MS Office, especially Microsoft Word, Excel, Visio and PowerPoint; some exposure to MS Access and Project.
Able to obtain security clearance under Transport Canada's Security Clearance Program.


The base salary range for this role is $77,689 - $97,112. This position is eligible for a performance-based variable incentive targeted at 10% of the base salary.

Equity, Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging are at the heart of PRPA’s culture and play a vital role in our growth strategy. We are committed to ensuring that everyone feels a sense of belonging and can contribute to their maximum potential. Our differences are the reason great things happen here. If you are an applicant in need of accommodation or special assistance throughout the interview process, please submit a request by email to careers@rupertport.com.

Individuals of Indigenous descent are strongly encouraged to apply.

Title: Pricing & Reporting Analyst
Description:
Adventure is our Culture. Join a team that celebrates a lifestyle as bold as the terrain we love. At Backcountry, we are rooted in adventure, recognition, and wellbeing on and off the mountain. We spotlight employee stories, celebrate milestones, and offer exclusive outdoor perks. Whether you are at HQ, in a retail store, or remote, you will be part of a team that thrives on energy, exploration, and connection.

Reports to: Sr. Pricing Analyst

Location: Cottonwood Heights, Utah (Hybrid - 3x/week in-office)

About The Role

The Merchandise Planning team needs a data-driven analyst to own the day-to-day mechanics of pricing across Backcountry's four banners—competitive pricing audits, markdown execution, MAP/MSRP compliance, and the reporting that powers pricing decisions.

As Pricing & Reporting Analyst, you will work directly with the Sr. Pricing Analyst to maximize profitability through smart pricing execution. By 6–12 months, you will be running pricing operations independently, regularly making recommendations that move sell-through, margin, or working capital, and serving as the team's go-to for AI-augmented workflows.

This is a lean team. You will own a lot, move fast, and make decisions with full end-to-end responsibility.

About The Role


Pull pricing, margin, and sales data from the data warehouse for ad hoc analysis
Use AI tools (Claude, Claude Code, Copilot, or similar) daily to write formulas, draft SQL, automate repetitive tasks, summarize results, and accelerate your own learning
Execute daily, weekly, and seasonal price changes across four banners, ensuring accuracy and on-time delivery
Monitor competitive pricing across key categories and partner brands; surface risks and opportunities to the Sr. Pricing Analyst and Buying teams
Track MAP and MSRP compliance across the assortment, flagging and correcting violations in a timely manner
Bring analytical judgment to pricing and strategy conversations—surface trends, audit outcomes, flag risks, and recommend pricing actions that improve sell-through, margin, or working capital
Build and maintain pricing reports and dashboards that Planning, Buying, and Marketing teams rely on
Document and improve pricing processes—turn one-off analyses into repeatable AI-powered workflows
Partner with Marketing on promotional pricing setup and post-promo recaps
Maintain a strong feedback loop with the Sr. Pricing Analyst on what is working, what is not, and where to push next


What You'll Do


Pull pricing, margin, and sales data from the data warehouse for ad hoc analysis
Use AI tools (Claude, Claude Code, Copilot, or similar) daily to write formulas, draft SQL, automate repetitive tasks, summarize results, and accelerate your own learning
Execute daily, weekly, and seasonal price changes across four banners, ensuring accuracy and on-time delivery
Monitor competitive pricing across key categories and partner brands; surface risks and opportunities to the Sr. Pricing Analyst and Buying teams
Track MAP and MSRP compliance across the assortment, flagging and correcting violations in a timely manner
Bring analytical judgment to pricing and strategy conversations—surface trends, audit outcomes, flag risks, and recommend pricing actions that improve sell-through, margin, or working capital
Build and maintain pricing reports and dashboards that Planning, Buying, and Marketing teams rely on
Document and improve pricing processes—turn one-off analyses into repeatable AI-powered workflows
Partner with Marketing on promotional pricing setup and post-promo recaps
Maintain a strong feedback loop with the Sr. Pricing Analyst on what is working, what is not, and where to push next


Required Qualifications


3–5 years of analyst experience, preferably in retail, ecommerce, or a related data-driven role
Demonstrated daily use of AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini, or similar) for real work—you iterate on prompts, evaluate outputs critically, and know when AI is the right tool versus when it is not
High proficiency with Microsoft Excel (pivots, lookups, formulas) and a strong aptitude to learn technical applications quickly
Solid understanding of retail math (margin, markdown, sell-through, IMU) and financial concepts (GMROI, NPV, CM%)
Awareness of customer pricing behavior—anchoring, discount perception, and how merchandising and pricing decisions land on a product detail page
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to work with messy datasets
Excellent attention to detail—pricing errors are expensive, and you treat that responsibility seriously
Excellent written and verbal communication; able to translate analysis into a clear, concise recommendation
Able to work both independently and collaboratively across Planning, Buying, Marketing, and Finance
Bachelor's degree in Business, Economics, Finance, Statistics, or a related field preferred; equivalent demonstrated experience considered
Passion for the outdoor industry and knowledge of outdoor gear is a plus.


Why Join

The people who do best here are builders. They take ownership, move fast, and want to see the direct impact of their work.


Cross-Functional Impact: Your pricing work carries weight beyond the Pricing team—recommendations you make will shape decisions across Planning, Buying, Marketing, and Finance.
AI-First Skill Building: AI is a first-class tool here, not a buzzword. You will use Claude, Copilot, and similar tools daily and actively shape how the team adopts them.
Real Ownership: This role pairs tactical pricing execution with strategic contributions. You will report to an experienced Sr. Pricing Analyst with growth-focused mentorship and high visibility from day one.
Competitive Benefits: Paid time off policies, 401(k) match, medical/dental/vision and a variety of supplemental policies, and employee discounts across our portfolio of brands.


Interview Process


Recruiter Screen - A 30-minute conversation with our recruiting team to align on the role, your background, and what you are looking for.
Hiring Manager Interview - Conversation with the Sr. Pricing Analyst focused on your analytical approach, pricing experience, and AI tool proficiency.
Take-Home Assignment - A pre-built test requiring high-level analysis, the use and documentation of AI as a working partner, and advanced communication skills to convey complex business recommendations.
In-Person Interview - We'd like to meet you at our Cottonwood Heights office. Details and logistics will be arranged with your recruiter.
Reference Checks - Conducted in parallel with the final stages where possible.
Offer - We move quickly for the right candidate.


Interview process is subject to change. Any updates will be communicated promptly and clearly.

CSC Generation is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

The CSC Generation family of brands is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities in our job application procedures. If you need assistance or accommodation due to a disability, please contact [email protected] .

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

  • Document and improve pricing processes—turn one-off analyses into repeatable AI-powered workflows
  • Partner with Marketing on promotional pricing setup and post-promo recaps
  • Maintain a strong feedback loop with the Sr. Pricing Analyst on what is working, what is not, and where to push next

Required Qualifications

  • 3–5 years of analyst experience, preferably in retail, ecommerce, or a related data-driven role
  • Demonstrated daily use of AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini, or similar) for real work—you iterate on prompts, evaluate outputs critically, and know when AI is the right tool versus when it is not
  • High proficiency with Microsoft Excel (pivots, lookups, formulas) and a strong aptitude to learn technical applications quickly
  • Solid understanding of retail math (margin, markdown, sell-through, IMU) and financial concepts (GMROI, NPV, CM%)
  • Awareness of customer pricing behavior—anchoring, discount perception, and how merchandising and pricing decisions land on a product detail page
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to work with messy datasets
  • Excellent attention to detail—pricing errors are expensive, and you treat that responsibility seriously
  • Excellent written and verbal communication; able to translate analysis into a clear, concise recommendation
  • Able to work both independently and collaboratively across Planning, Buying, Marketing, and Finance
  • Bachelor's degree in Business, Economics, Finance, Statistics, or a related field preferred; equivalent demonstrated experience considered
  • Passion for the outdoor industry and knowledge of outdoor gear is a plus.

Why Join

The people who do best here are builders. They take ownership, move fast, and want to see the direct impact of their work.

  • Cross-Functional Impact: Your pricing work carries weight beyond the Pricing team—recommendations you make will shape decisions across Planning, Buying, Marketing, and Finance.
  • AI-First Skill Building: AI is a first-class tool here, not a buzzword. You will use Claude, Copilot, and similar tools daily and actively shape how the team adopts them.
  • Real Ownership: This role pairs tactical pricing execution with strategic contributions. You will report to an experienced Sr. Pricing Analyst with growth-focused mentorship and high visibility from day one.
  • Competitive Benefits: Paid time off policies, 401(k) match, medical/dental/vision and a variety of supplemental policies, and employee discounts across our portfolio of brands.

Interview Process

  • Recruiter Screen - A 30-minute conversation with our recruiting team to align on the role, your background, and what you are looking for.
  • Hiring Manager Interview - Conversation with the Sr. Pricing Analyst focused on your analytical approach, pricing experience, and AI tool proficiency.
  • Take-Home Assignment - A pre-built test requiring high-level analysis, the use and documentation of AI as a working partner, and advanced communication skills to convey complex business recommendations.
  • In-Person Interview - We'd like to meet you at our Cottonwood Heights office. Details and logistics will be arranged with your recruiter.
  • Reference Checks - Conducted in parallel with the final stages where possible.
  • Offer - We move quickly for the right candidate.

Interview process is subject to change. Any updates will be communicated promptly and clearly.

CSC Generation is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

The CSC Generation family of brands is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities in our job application procedures. If you need assistance or accommodation due to a disability, please contact [email protected] .

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
TTM Technologies, Inc. – Publicly Traded US Company, NASDAQ (TTMI) – Top-5 Global Printed Circuit Board Manufacturer

About TTM

TTM Technologies, Inc. is a leading global manufacturer of technology products, including mission systems, radio frequency (“RF”) components, RF microwave/microelectronic assemblies, and technologically advanced printed circuit boards (“PCB”s). TTM stands for time-to-market, representing how TTM's time-critical, one-stop design, engineering and manufacturing services enable customers to reduce the time required to develop new products and bring them to market.

Additional information can be found at www.ttm.com

TTM Technologies is seeking a Data Analyst with and understanding of Quality Production Engineering. This role will design, develop, and maintain dashboards and reports that provide actionable insights for forecasting and managing demand across our product lines.

Job Duties:


Diagnose problems in the production line and provide recommendations and training.
Assess and analyze current production methods
Develop and write program improvements for production and other areas to improve efficiency
Develop and draft an improved production process
Design and implement cost-reductive changes
Maximize productivity of machinery and workers
Lead Capacity studies for assigned processes
Collaborate with the production engineering staff in preparing & maintaining capacity models and plans
Determine the correct data sets and variables
Collect large sets of structured and unstructured data from disparate sources
Clean and validate the data to ensure accuracy, completeness, and uniformity
Devise and apply models and algorithms to mine the stores of big data
Analyzing the data to identify patterns and trends
Interpret the data to discover solutions and opportunities
Communicate findings to stakeholders using visualization and other means
Identify the data-analytics problems that offer the greatest opportunities to the organization


Qualifications:


Bachelor’s degree in Programming, Production Engineering or Industrial Engineering with 2-5 years of experience, or Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, or an equivalent combination of 5+ years’ practical experience and coursework is preferred.
Superb analytical, problem-solving, and critical thinking skills.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office is preferred
Demonstrated proficiency with a programming language such as Python, Matlab or Visual Basic is preferred.
Experience or familiarity with CAD is preferred
Familiarity with standardized work combination tables, Takt time, cycle time, etc.
Fundamental concepts in data science and analytics
Understanding of SQL and relational databases
Experience in SQL Join and conditional flow statements is preferred
RStudio for programming experience is preferred


#ISBU

Compensation and Benefits:

TTM offers a variety of health and well-being benefit programs. Benefit options include medical, dental, vision, 401K, Flexible Spending Account, Health Savings Account, accident benefits, life insurance, disability benefits, paid vacation & holidays. Benefits are available 1st of the month following date of hire.

Compensation for roles at TTM Technologies varies depending on a wide array of factors including but not limited to the specific office location, role, skill set and level of experience. As required by local law, TTM provides a reasonable range of compensation for roles that may be hired in New York, California and Colorado. For California-based roles, compensation ranges are based upon specific physical locations.

Export Statement:

Must comply with TTM Export Control Policies and Procedures and all applicable laws including ITAR, EAR and OFAC including but not limited to: a) being able to identify ITAR product on the manufacturing floor and understand that access to these products and related technical data is restricted to only US Citizens and US Permanent Residents; b) recognition of Foreign Person visitors by badge differentiation; c) understand and follow authorization procedures for bringing foreign visitors into facilities (VAL); d) understand the Export and ITAR requirements for shipments leaving the US; e) manage vendor approvals for ITAR manufacturing and services.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, citizenship, disability or protected veteran status.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Purpose of this Position:

The Enterprise Data Analyst is responsible for both designing and developing our data architecture and performing data analysis to support business decision-making. This role encompasses the end-to-end process of data collection, transformation, storage, and analysis. The Enterprise Data Analyst will work with departments across the organization to provide data solutions to our internal teams, ensuring data is reliable, accessible, and actionable, driving insights and strategic initiatives throughout the organization. They will also assist in deploying special projects for the other departments as requested.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities:

Design, implement, and maintain data pipelines and ETL processes to support data reporting and dashboards.
Organize, model, and store raw data in our data warehouse and data lake.
Analyze and interpret data using statistical methods and tools.
Create and maintain reports, dashboards, and visualizations to communicate findings to stakeholders
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand business requirements and deliver data-driven solutions.
Ensure the reliability, efficiency, and quality of data by developing and implementing processes for data cleaning, transformation, and validation.

Competencies:

Ability to learn internal software and systems.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Ability to manage competing projects and priorities effectively.
Ability to interact with varying organizational levels confidently.
Ability to communicate technical information coherently to a variety of stakeholders.
Ability to confidentially interact with and interpret organizational information.
Strong analytical skills.

Qualifications:

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

Education/Experience:

Bachelor's degree and 2+ years of experience in data analysis, interpretation, and visualization; or an equivalent combination of education and experience, including 6+ years of experience in data analysis, interpretation, and visualization.
Experience with enterprise software or the transportation and logistics industry is a plus.
Advanced proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite.
Working knowledge of at least one major programming language (e.g., Python, C#, Java, or Scala) required.
Proficiency with statistical analysis and data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Looker) required.
Familiarity with big data technologies and platforms such as Delta Lake, Spark, and Databricks required.
Experience with SQL and relational databases is highly preferred.
Knowledge of schema design, data modeling, and data analysis is preferred.
Familiarity with data orchestration tools such as Dagster, Airflow, or Prefect is preferred.
Preferred certifications in Azure, Power BI, Data Analytics, or Data Engineering include, but are not limited to:
Azure Data Engineer Associate
Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst (PL-300)
Microsoft Azure Fundamentals
DataCamp Data Engineer Certification
DataCamp Data Analyst Certification
DataCamp Data Scientist Certification
Other related cloud, analytics, or data engineering certifications

Why McLeod?

At McLeod Software, we believe that our employees are our greatest competitive advantage. Each employee contributes directly to McLeod Software’s growth and success. When you join McLeod, you’re joining a company that’s built for the long haul—with a clear vision, strong leadership, and a commitment to investing in our people.

Title: Revenue Analyst
Description:
Company Description

Discover the essence of Maui at Fairmont Kea Lani, Hawai‘i’s only all-suite and villa luxury resort. Nestled on the pristine white sands of Wailea’s Polo Beach, this award-winning oceanfront paradise defines quintessential Hawaiian luxury with a state-of-the-art spa, island inspired cuisine and authentic cultural experiences.

From full-moon staff hikes and beach clean-ups to appreciation luncheons and birthday holidays, our team provides endless opportunities to learn and grow in both your personal and professional development. Live and work in a place you love!

What Is In It For You


Comprehensive benefits package (Medical, Vision and Dental) including extended benefits like; Basic insurance, TDI and Long-Term Disability for regular Full-time and Part-time employees
401(k)
One complimentary duty meal for all employees that work more than 6 hours per shift
Food & Beverage discount at Fairmont Kea Lani (venue specific and discount may vary)
Wellness Offerings
Employee benefit card offering discounted rates in Accor worldwide
Learning programs through our Academies
Opportunity to develop your talent and grow within your property and across the world!
Ability to make a difference through our Corporate Social Responsibility activities


Job Description

Hourly Rate: $31.74

There is no room for the ordinary in anything we do. As a key player in the Revenue Management Team, you will be playing an active role in the hotel's strategic decision-making process. Your analysis and recommendations will act as the cornerstones in the decision-making process. Your direct engagement with the hotel’s senior leadership team will pave the way to a strong career path for you to progress in the Revenue Management world.

ANALYTICS: REPORTING

Accurate & Timely Reporting


Generate precise Revenue Management reports.
Support team and senior leadership with ad-hoc reports.
Produce daily, weekly, and monthly reports.
Present 14-day rooms forecast.
Assist in 30, 60, and 90-day revenue forecasts.
Contribute to Food & Beverage and Spa reporting.


Tool Ownership


Manage external tools (Lighthouse, D360/A360, STR).
Ensure data accuracy and enhance tool automation.


STRATEGY: IMPLEMENTATION

OTA Management


Oversee OTA extranets for rate and inventory accuracy.
Maintain rate parity across platforms.


Content Audit


Monthly audit of hotel content on distribution channels.


Rate And Promotion Handling


Load rates, promotions, and packages for proper distribution.


Documentation And Meetings


Maintain Hotel Rate Code and package allocation template.
Participate in weekly Revenue Maximization Meetings.


Strategic Proposals


Provide proposals for long and short-term hotel strategies.


Schedule: Due to the nature of the hospitality industry, employees are required to work varying schedules, including overnight, weekends and holidays, to accommodate the business and demands of the hotel.

Qualifications

Education


Bachelor’s degree in Hotel Management, Accounting, Finance, Mathematics, or Business Management preferred


Experience


Minimum 2 years of Reservations Management experience is preferred
Minimum 2 years of Revenue Analytics/Strategy (or equivalent) experience is highly preferred
Experience with Opera (PMS and S&C) and Passkey GroupMax is highly preferred
Hospitality industry experience is highly preferred


Additional Information

All Candidates must be in possession of identification proving authorization to work in the United States as defined in the Immigration Reform and Control Act of 1986 (IRCA). This Position does not offer sponsorship of Work Visas. Resumes submitted that cannot fulfill these requirements will not be considered.

Visa Requirements: Applicants must be legally eligible to work the United States

Our Commitment To Diversity & Inclusion

We are an inclusive company and our ambition is to attract, recruit and promote diverse talent.

Join our ‘ohana today: Visit our website to learn more about living and working for Fairmont Hawai’i. www.fairmonthawaiijobs.com

Why work for Accor? Discover a world where life pulses with passion!

Join the Accor Group, an ecosystem of over 45 brands, 5,600 hotels, 10,000 restaurants and lifestyle places, that welcome you. With us, your personality is valued, your opportunities for growth know no boundaries. Every action you take has a positive and memorable impact on the experience of our customers, your colleagues, and also, on the planet, contributing to pioneering the art of responsible hospitality.

Hospitality is a work of heart! #fairmontcareers #alwayskealani

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are seeking a Data Analyst to join our growing Data & Analytics team at Funko. In this role, you will leverage data to deliver actionable insights that support business growth, operational efficiency, and smarter decision-making. You’ll work closely with cross-functional partners to build scalable reporting, analyze trends, and help drive a more data-informed culture.This is a hands-on individual contributor role ideal for someone who enjoys translating data into meaningful business impact.What You’ll Do
Partner with Operations, Sales, Marketing, Finance, and other teams to support data-driven decisions
Build and maintain dashboards, reports, and KPI tracking in BI tools (Power BI, Tableau Looker, etc.)
Develop AI-enabled and automated reporting solutions to enhance insight generation and reduce manual work
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify trends, risks, and opportunities
Translate complex datasets into clear insights and business recommendations
Write and optimize SQL queries to work with large, complex datasets
Identify predictive modeling opportunities and build models
Collaborate with Data Engineering to improve data models, data quality, and reporting scalability
Support reporting and analytics needs tied to ERP and operational systems
What You’ll Bring
Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or related field (or equivalent experience)
3–5+ years of hands-on experience in data analytics
Strong SQL skills and experience working with large datasets
Proficiency in BI and data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker, etc.)
Data science skill set: knowledgeable in statistical modeling (e.g. linear and logistical regression, classification, clustering) and tooling (python, R, etc.) with broad domain applicability
Experience with AI-driven analytics, automated reporting, or leveraging machine learning outputs in reporting workflows
Familiarity with cloud data platforms (Snowflake, AWS, GCP, Azure, etc.)
Experience working with ERP systems (Microsoft D365, Oracle, or similar) strongly preferred
Solid understanding of data warehousing and data modeling concepts
Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving skills, and ability to manage multiple priorities
Clear communication skills with the ability to present insights to non-technical stakeholders 
Salary InformationThe base salary range for this position in the selected city is $90,000 - $110,000 annually.​Compensation may vary outside of this range depending on a number of factors, including a candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and experience, and location. Base pay is one part of the Total Package that is provided to compensate and recognize employees for their work, and this role may be eligible for additional discretionary bonuses/incentives, and restricted stock units.

What Funko Offers
Funko offers a competitive compensation package with full benefits and a 401(K) plan with matching contributions from the company. Most importantly, we offer a creative work environment with people who love pop culture just as much as you do. Can’t wait to gush about your latest binge? Neither can we! Looking for a place where your favorite pop culture t-shirt will receive the compliments it deserves? We know how you feel!


This role will be based in our Everett, WA HQ office. We will prioritize local candidates first and candidates located near a local office. This is NOT a remote opportunity at this moment.

Funko offers a competitive compensation package with full benefits and a 401(K) plan with matching contributions from the company. Most importantly, we offer a creative work environment with people who love pop culture just as much as you do. Can’t wait to gush about your latest binge? Neither can we! Looking for a place where your favorite pop culture t-shirt will receive the compliments it deserves? We know how you feel!

This role will be based in our Everett, WA HQ office. We will prioritize local candidates first and candidates located near a local office. This is NOT a remote opportunity at this moment.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
The Data Analyst is the single point of accountability for the accuracy, integrity, and consistency of Allied Business Solutions' core business data across our ERP, CRM, and PSA platforms. This is a hands-on, technical role focused on keeping data clean, aligned, and trustworthy as it moves between systems — through disciplined data standards, active auditing, direct ownership of integrations and data synchronization, and targeted automation that eliminates manual entry and enforces quality. The Data Analyst ensures that every department works from a single, reliable source of truth, and provides reporting that turns well-governed data into usable insight for sales, service, and finance.




Key Responsibilities:




Cross-System Data Stewardship & Integrity

Serve as the single point of accountability for data accuracy, completeness, and consistency across the ERP, CRM, PSA, and related business systems.
Establish and enforce data standards, naming conventions, and entry rules so records remain consistent across systems and users.
Perform regular audits to identify and resolve errors, duplicates, gaps, and cross-system mismatches.
Own master and reference data (customers, products, pricing, equipment) so a single source of truth is reflected consistently everywhere it appears.
Maintain data dictionaries and documentation covering field definitions, ownership, and standards.

Data Integration & Synchronization

Own the flow and synchronization of data between the ERP, CRM, PSA, and other platforms, ensuring records stay aligned across all systems.
Write and maintain SQL queries to validate, extract, and reconcile data.
Build and maintain API-based integrations; troubleshoot sync failures and drive root-cause resolution with vendors or internal stakeholders.
Monitor integration health and proactively catch and correct data drift before it reaches downstream teams.

Automation for Data Quality & CRM Workflows

Design, build, and maintain automated routines that reduce manual entry and enforce data quality — deduplication, validation, reconciliation, and exception alerting.
Build and maintain CRM workflow automations that streamline sales and service processes and keep records current.
Document automations and adapt them as business needs evolve.

Product, Pricing & Proposal Data

Maintain accurate product records, configurations, and pricing across systems.
Keep proposal templates and pricing data current so quotes and proposals generate from clean, correct source data.

Lease & Asset Data Reconciliation

Maintain lease and asset records and reconcile them against related data across systems for consistency.
Monitor key dates — renewals, expirations, and term changes — and flag them to the appropriate stakeholders.

Reporting & Data Support

Generate routine and ad hoc reports to support the sales, service, and finance teams.
Support cross-departmental data requests and help translate data into usable insight.
Identify opportunities to improve data workflows, accuracy, and reporting efficiency.




Qualifications:




Required

Associate's or Bachelor's degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Business, or a related field, or equivalent relevant experience.
Proven experience managing data across CRM, ERP, PSA, or comparable business systems.
Hands-on SQL skills for querying, validation, and reconciliation.
Experience building and maintaining integrations (APIs) and troubleshooting data-sync issues.
Experience with process automation.
Strong attention to detail and a high standard for data accuracy.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and comfort working with large datasets.
Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities.

Preferred

Familiarity with data governance and master data management practices.
Experience with data reporting and visualization/dashboarding tools.
Familiarity with lease or asset tracking systems.
Scripting for automation (e.g., Python or PowerShell) or experience with low-code automation platforms.
Background in the managed IT, technology, or copier/print industry.

Competencies

Ownership and accountability for data integrity
Problem-solving and analytical thinking
Technical aptitude and adaptability
Clear written and verbal communication
Dependability and follow-through
Collaboration across teams

Title: Supplies North America Business Analyst
Description:
The North America Supplies Business Analyst / Reporting & Data Specialist is responsible for translating complex business data into decision-ready reporting, clear business insights, and actionable recommendations. This role will collect, clean, validate, analyze, and communicate data from multiple business sources, applying data science, statistical analysis, and business analytics techniques to identify trends, performance drivers, risks, and opportunities.

Responsibilities


Support the collection, cleaning, preprocessing, validation, and organization of data from multiple business sources to ensure reporting accuracy, integrity, and consistency.
Apply data science, statistical analysis, and business analytics techniques to identify trends, drivers, risks, and opportunities across key North America Supplies business performance areas.
Generate recurring reports and ad-hoc analysis that highlight key metrics, business trends, performance drivers, and emerging risks or opportunities.
Help create and maintain repeatable reporting processes, templates, dashboards, and documentation to reduce manual work and improve consistency.
Use AI-enabled tools to improve speed and quality of analysis, identifying opportunities for modernization through automation, simplification, and process improvement
Collaborate with Category subject matter experts to align reporting logic, business definitions, data assumptions, methodology, and procedures for collecting and communicating business data.
Translate complex analysis outputs into clear charts, tables, summaries, and actionable recommendations for internal stakeholders.


Education & Experience Recommended

Four-year or graduate degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, Finance, Business Analytics, Computer Science, Data Science, Data Analytics, or any other related discipline; or commensurate work experience or demonstrated competence.

Typically has 3–5 years of work experience, preferably in data analytics, data science, business analytics, reporting, database management, statistical analysis, or a related field.

Preferred Certifications

Programming Language/s Certification (SQL, Python, or similar).

Knowledge & Skills


Data Analysis
Data Science
AI-Enabled Analytics
Statistical Analysis
Business Analytics
Business Intelligence
Advanced Excel
Excel Modeling
PivotTables
Power Query
Data Management
Data Modeling
Data Quality
Data Visualization / Power BI
Extract, Transform, Load / ETL
Reporting Automation
Business Reporting
SQL
Python


Cross-Org Skills


Effective Communication
Results Orientation
Learning Agility
Digital Fluency
Customer Centricity


Impact & Scope

Impacts function and/or provides expertise to functional project teams and may participate in cross-functional initiatives.

Complexity


Works on complex problems where analysis of situations or data requires an in-depth evaluation of multiple factors.


Disclaimer

This job description describes the general nature and level of work performed in this role. It is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all duties, skills, responsibilities, knowledge, etc. These may be subject to change and additional functions may be assigned as needed by management.

Pay And Benefits

The pay range for this role is $83,000 to $127,800 USD annually with additional opportunities for pay in the form of bonus and/or equity (applies to United States of America candidates only). Pay varies by work location, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience.

Benefits

HP offers a comprehensive benefits package for this position, including:


Health insurance
Dental insurance
Vision insurance
Long term/short term disability insurance
Employee assistance program
Flexible spending account
Life insurance
Generous time off policies, including;
4-12 weeks fully paid parental leave based on tenure
11 paid holidays
Additional flexible paid vacation and sick leave (US benefits overview)


The compensation and benefits information is accurate as of the date of this posting. The Company reserves the right to modify this information at any time, with or without notice, subject to applicable law.

Title: Business Systems Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Business Systems Analyst

Job Category: Information Technology

Time Type: Full time

Minimum Clearance Required to Start: Secret

Employee Type: Regular

Percentage of Travel Required: Up to 10%

Type of Travel: Local

* * *

The Opportunity

CACI is seeking a Business Systems Analyst to support Shipyard personnel tasked to expedite execution of maintenance availabilities and accelerate on-time delivery of submarines. This position supports the Shipyard solely onsite in HI.

As The Business Systems Analyst, You Will


Leverage your data analytics and dashboard development experience and proven expertise while partnering with Shipyard personnel at all levels of the organization to improve processes, workflow, communications, collaboration, reporting, and supply chains that will lead to increases in Submarine Operational Availability.
Provide subject matter expertise in shipbuilding or ship repair activities, including best practices and lessons learned, that can be applied to reap improvements in Shipyard naval maintenance and modernization activities.
Provide consultation, tools, and methods to aid decision making, project management, and action planning.


Responsibilities


Work collaboratively within a local CACI team and across contractor and customer teams to improve naval maintenance and modernization activities.
Extract data from databases to build metrics, dashboards, and slide deck reports.
Establish and track metrics to determine effectiveness and ensure accountability of stated goals. Perform data collection and analysis.
Design, develop, and update operational and performance metrics and visualization dashboards that measure the status of objectives and their impact on the performance of stated Shipyard measures. Create and deliver slide deck reports and presentations that meet customer needs.
Apply subject matter expertise to determine the highest value-added goals and develop an effective course of action to achieve them.
Facilitate supported teams in taking specific, measurable actions at the agreed upon standard of quality and consistency to drive goal achievement.
Plan, facilitate, lead, and support customer meetings, presentations, briefings, strategic planning sessions, working groups, off-site events, etc. in support of contract activities. This includes pre- and post-administrative support/coordination.
Provide information for various Shipyard management systems.
Support and coordinate with customers in preparation of documentation and deliverables.
Assist Shipyard Trade Shops, Support Codes, and Project Teams in focusing on process improvements that will add the most value to their work.
Establish standards, track highest-performing teams’ behaviors, and share them with across the Naval Maintenance Community.
Develop, maintain, and execute communication strategies that include all customer stakeholders.
Promote accountability through fostering a collective desire to hold each other accountable, celebrate victories, correct problems, and unify the team.
Support the execution of contract requirements, which may include submission of supporting data and metrics and timely completion of written reports to stakeholders, customers, and CACI leadership.
Other duties may be assigned in support of contract taskings and mission success.


Qualifications

Required:


Must be a US Citizen
Must have the ability to obtain a DOD interim Secret and subsequent final DOD Secret security clearance.
Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field. In lieu of the degree requirement, candidates with minimum 10 years of directly applicable work experience may be considered for this position.
Minimum 5 years of experience working in public or private industry Shipyards in the area of shipbuilding, modernization, or ship repair OR in a large commercial repair machine shop OR in a production shop or engineering department.
Extensive experience developing metrics from data mining and developing visualization dashboards using Excel or Power BI tools. High level of proficiency in extracting data from databases to build metrics, dashboards, and slide deck reports.
Expertise in Shipyard best practices with the ability to leverage that expertise and experience to identify best-in-class practices across the shipbuilding and ship repair community.
Demonstrated project management and research skills.
Experience facilitating meetings attended by senior leadership.
Self-starter who is comfortable working in ambiguous situations and in a demanding industrial environment.
Proven leadership ability and demonstrated focus on making the workplace and operating environment higher performing.
Ability to work both independently and within a team to resolve problems.
Strong customer service orientation and written/verbal communication skills with the ability to respectfully engage internal and external customers at all levels in a manner that achieves successful outcomes.
Outstanding organizational skills to efficiently manage competing priorities, multiple concurrent taskings, and firm deadlines.
Proficiency with Microsoft 365.


Desired


Active DOD Secret security clearance
Experience working with unions and leading teams of Shipyard skilled workers (e.g., First-Line Supervisor, Work Group Leader, Foreman, Zone Manager).
Project Management Professional (PMP) certification.
Certification in Agile, Lean, Six Sigma, Shingo, or Theory of Constraints.
Experience using cloud-based knowledge management systems (SharePoint, iNFUSION Suite, Jira, etc.).
Knowledge of Naval Shipyard data systems.
Work experience with the Naval Maintenance Community, United States Navy, or the Department of Defense. Experience working with Carrier Planning Activity, SUBMEPP, SHAPEC, PMS312, PMS 392, etc. is preferred.


What You Can Expect

A culture of integrity.

At CACI, we place character and innovation at the center of everything we do. As a valued team member, you’ll be part of a high-performing group dedicated to our customer’s missions and driven by a higher purpose – to ensure the safety of our nation.

An environment of trust.

CACI values the unique contributions that every employee brings to our company and our customers - every day. You’ll have the autonomy to take the time you need through a unique flexible time off benefit and have access to robust learning resources to make your ambitions a reality.

A focus on continuous growth.

Together, we will advance our nation's most critical missions, build on our lengthy track record of business success, and find opportunities to break new ground — in your career and in our legacy.

Pay Range

There are a host of factors that can influence final salary including, but not limited to, geographic location, Federal Government contract labor categories and contract wage rates, relevant prior work experience, specific skills and competencies, education, and certifications. Our employees value the flexibility at CACI that allows them to balance quality work and their personal lives. We offer competitive compensation, benefits and learning and development opportunities. Our broad and competitive mix of benefits options is designed to support and protect employees and their families. At CACI, you will receive comprehensive benefits such as; healthcare, wellness, financial, retirement, family support, continuing education, and time off benefits.

The Proposed Salary Range For This Position Is

$71,800 - $150,800

CACI is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, age, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any other protected characteristic.

Title: Data Analyst - Coquitlam
Description:
Data Analyst

Are you a Data Analyst with a passion problem solving? Factors Group are a local and fast-growing manufacturing and health and wellness company where your work will have a direct and meaningful impact on business growth. As part of a collaborative and continuous improvement environment, you’ll have the opportunity to both help us as we migrate to Fabric, and work on projects you’re passionate about, all while building your skills and advancing your career.

Permanent, Full-time

Monday – Friday, 8:00am – 4:30pm

Hybrid – 2000 Brigantine Drive, Coquitlam, BC

$75,000 – $90,000 per annum

In this role, you will:


Analyze and interpret data from multiple business systems to support informed decision-making.
Design and maintain dashboards and reports using Power BI, Tableau, and Excel.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to gather requirements and improve reporting solutions.
Identify and resolve data discrepancies to ensure data integrity and consistency across systems.
Support data governance initiatives and contribute to ongoing improvements in data quality and accessibility.


You have:


A Bachelor’s degree or diploma in Computer Science, Business Systems Analysis, or a related field.
3+ years of experience in data analysis, including data modeling and analytics.
Strong proficiency in Power BI, Tableau, Excel, and Power Query (DAX/LOD).
Working knowledge of t-SQL for data querying and manipulation (Python is an asset).
Experience working with diverse data sources, including databases, cloud platforms, and APIs.


You get:


Great benefits package (healthcare, health & personal spending accounts, wellness days, Employee & Family Assistance Program)
Company-provided RRSP
$300 per year to spend on company products
Employee discount pricing
Employee appreciation events & rewards
Free onsite parking
Bus pass reimbursement
Exclusive discounts with various vendors


Why us?


We’re much bigger than you think – 30+ natural health brands loved across North America.
Proudly Canadian-owned & operated
We are committed to sustainability across people, planet, and profit, embedding responsible practices into how we operate and grow as a business.


Learn more about us here: https://ca.naturalfactors.com/pages/our-nature

FGOffice

This position is open to all qualified applicants who are legally entitled to work in the country in which this job is located. Only electronic applications can be accepted. This position will close when a competitive candidate pool has been identified. We thank you all for your applications and a member of our team will contact you soon. All the best!

All Internal applicants will be given priority for consideration for the first five business days that this position is posted. After that five days, Internal applicants will be considered along with all other applicants; however, are guaranteed at minimum a call from the Talent Acquisition team.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At UBC, we believe that attracting and sustaining a diverse workforce is key to the successful pursuit of excellence in research, innovation, and learning for all faculty, staff and students. Our commitment to employment equity helps achieve inclusion and fairness, brings rich diversity to UBC as a workplace, and creates the necessary conditions for a rewarding career.




Job Summary




UBC Sauder School of Business is one of the world’s leading business faculties. The school consistently ranks among the top 100 in the world and is recognized internationally for excellence in research and learning, an outstanding faculty and unmatched global partnerships. A collaborative and respectful culture within which all members of our community can thrive is a key principle in our strategic plan, and one that underpins our educational mission.




We acknowledge that UBC Sauder at the Vancouver Point Grey campus is situated on the traditional, ancestral and unceded territory of the Musqueam. We acknowledge that UBC Sauder at UBC Robson Square is situated on the traditional territory of the Musqueam, Squamish and Tsleil-Waututh.




The Sauder IT & Analytics team partners with faculty, staff, students, and other institutional stakeholders to deliver and sustain relevant, reliable, and innovative information technology solutions. Operating within a dynamic and fast-paced environment, we are committed to driving evidence-based decision-making in support of academic and operational excellence.

The Data Analyst sources and manages data from multiple systems-of-record, including raw data from databases and application APIs, curated extracts, and ad-hoc datasets. This position supports data management and analytics for the UBC Sauder School of Business, while working closely with institutional data governance and analytics teams.




This role focuses on identifying, sourcing, cleaning, managing and transforming data to provide clear, actionable insights for multiple UBC Sauder stakeholders. The Data Analyst is responsible for identifying and sourcing required data, preparing trusted and analytics-ready datasets, developing impactful visualizations, and enabling self-service analytics for business users using modern technologies such as Microsoft Fabric and AI-enabled analytics tools. The Data Analyst is responsible for a number of analytic areas, including, but not limited to, data preparation and management, data mining, statistical analysis, visual analysis, temporal data analysis and/or text analysis.




This role is ideal for a team-focused professional who specializes in identifying and preparing high-quality data and visualizations that empower business users. This position will play a central role in enabling data-driven decision-making, communicating insights, and advancing a modern data management and analytics environment for the UBC Sauder School of Business.




Work Performed




Business Engagement & Delivery




Partner with stakeholders to understand business needs, gather requirements and translate them into analytical datasets, dashboards, reports and analytical solutions.
Work with stakeholders to identify relevant data sources, clarify data definitions, and understand how data flows across systems.
Support reporting needs across key domains (e.g., finance, HR, learning systems, marketing, admissions).
Enable self-service analytics by creating reusable datasets, semantic models, and reports.
Provide guidance and training to users on how to interpret and use data effectively.
Support business users in understanding the end-to-end flow of data.




Data Discovery, Dataset Development & Data Preparation




Identify, source, and bring together data from multiple information systems, files, databases, and business processes to support reporting and analytics needs.
Build and maintain curated, analytics-ready datasets within Microsoft Fabric (Lakehouse, Warehouse, OneLake).
Clean, transform, standardize and model data using SQL, Power Query, and Fabric Dataflows.
Apply business rules and data transformation logic to ensure datasets are accurate, consistent, and aligned with reporting needs.
Collaborate with other analysts, data managers, and technical partners to source, refine, and prepare data for analytics consumption.
Collaborate with other analysts and data managers to optimize data for performance, accuracy, and long-term maintenance.




Data Quality & Governance




Validate and reconcile data to ensure accuracy, completeness, consistency, and reliability across reports and dashboards.
Document data definitions, calculations, transformation logic, assumptions and lineage for transparency and reuse.
Contribute and adhere to data governance practices, including the use of standardized data definitions, metrics and metadata.
Identify and troubleshoot data issues, working with upstream teams to resolve them.




Dashboarding & Data Visualization




Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI within Microsoft Fabric.
Create clear, intuitive and visually compelling data products that communicate key metrics, trends, insights, and business unit performance.
Define and standardize KPIs and reporting layers.
Promote best practices in data visualization, usability, and performance, with an emphasis on empowering business user self-service where appropriate.




Advanced Analytics & Continuous Improvement




Perform exploratory data analysis to uncover trends, patterns, and opportunities.
Support advanced analytics initiatives by preparing high-quality datasets.
Use novel technologies, including artificial intelligence, to support data management and analysis.
Leverage Microsoft Fabric capabilities (e.g., Notebooks, Pipelines, Data Agents) to enhance productivity and insight generation.
Manage version control of notebooks and data deployments across environments.
Improve dashboards, datasets, and reporting processes based on user feedback, and changing business needs.
Identify, source, clean, prepare and manage data from multiple independent systems-of-record.
Ensure data is continuously available in data lakes and/or data warehouses
Validate data for accuracy, completeness, consistency, and reliability across datasets.
Build dashboards, reports, and curated datasets.
Maintain documentation for datasets, data definitions, KPIs, calculations, and reporting logic.
Ensure alignment with enterprise data standards and governance practices.
Support the full analytics lifecycle, from data discovery and preparation through to reporting, visualization, and insight delivery.
Contribute to the evolution of the organization’s analytics and reporting capabilities.
Performs other duties as assigned.




Minimum Qualifications




Undergraduate degree in a relevant discipline. Minimum of two years of related experience, or the equivalent combination of education and experience.




- Willingness to respect diverse perspectives, including perspectives in conflict with one’s own

- Demonstrates a commitment to enhancing one’s own awareness, knowledge, and skills related to equity, diversity, and inclusion




Preferred Qualifications




Undergraduate or graduate degree in Business Operations, Data Analytics, Computer Science, or a related field.
Experience in data management, data analysis, business intelligence, or other analytics roles.
Experience in proactively identifying, collecting and preparing data for use in data pipelines, reporting, and analytics.
Experience building dashboards and preparing datasets in enterprise environments.
Experience working with large and complex datasets from multiple independent systems of record.




People Skills




Excellent communication and data storytelling abilities.
Strong stakeholder engagement and collaboration skills.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights.
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy, quality and documentation.




Technical Skills

Strong experience with Python and Microsoft Fabric (including Power BI, Dataflows, and Lakehouse/Warehouse environments).
Strong experience with SQL (T-SQL, PL/SQL) for data querying, transformation, and validation.
Advanced skills in DAX, semantic modeling, and building interactive reports within the Fabric ecosystem.
Expertise in building Power BI dashboards, reports, and semantic models.
Experience with OneLake, Delta tables, and Medallion architecture (Bronze/Silver/Gold) concepts.
Proficiency in Power Query M and/or Microsoft Fabric Dataflows for data preparation.
Familiarity with notebook-based analytics.
Understanding of data modeling concepts (star schemas, dimensional modeling).




Additional Assets




Experience with enterprise systems such as Workday, CRM/Salesforce, and Student Information Systems.
Knowledge of data governance, data quality, and metadata management practices.
Familiarity with cloud-based analytics platforms (Microsoft Azure and AWS preferred).
Experience enabling self-service analytics in large organizations.
Familiarity with Data Agents/Copilot in Microsoft Fabric.
Experience working in Higher Education.




Compensation Range

$5,964.75 - $8,573.08 CAD Monthly

The Compensation Range is the span between the minimum and maximum base salary for a position. The midpoint of the range is approximately halfway between the minimum and the maximum and represents an employee that possesses full job knowledge, qualifications and experience for the position. In the normal course, employees will be hired, transferred or promoted between the minimum and midpoint of the salary range for a job.




Posting End Date: July 30, 2026




Note: Applications will be accepted until 11:59 PM on the Posting End Date.




Job End Date: Ongoing




Applications will only be accepted via the hiring portal - please ensure you do not send via LinkedIn

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Role summary: As a Data Analyst, you will transform complex data into clear, meaningful insights that support decision-making across the business. Using Power BI, SQL, and Python, you will develop high-quality reports and dashboards, ensure data integrity, and work closely with cross-functional teams to deliver accurate, actionable analysis within a highly regulated engineering environment.

What You Will Be Doing


Developing clear, insightful, and visually engaging dashboards, reports, and data visualisations using Power BI to support informed decision-making.
Analysing and manipulating data using SQL and Python to identify trends, patterns, and actionable insights.
Supporting the definition and refinement of key performance indicators (KPIs) and metrics to track business and technical performance.
Collaborating with engineering, project management, and consultancy teams to understand requirements, shape analytical approaches, and communicate findings.
Monitoring data quality, identifying anomalies, and implementing controls to maintain data accuracy, consistency, and integrity.
Providing training, guidance, and ongoing support to users of data platforms and analytics tools.


Formal qualifications or training:


We’d love to hear from you if you have hands-on experience in data analysis, manipulation, and visualisation. Strong experience using a Microsoft data technology stack (including Power BI and SQL) is essential, with working knowledge of Python for analysis and automation highly desirable.


Make a Difference with DBD:

At DBD, we know you're looking for more than just a job - you aspire to make a real impact in the nuclear industry. We offer unique opportunities for growth, empowering you to take influential roles within client organisations.

Our dedicated team collaborates with clients to positively influence projects across Defence, Decommissioning and New Builds. Having doubled in headcount year-on-year, over the past two years, we continue to welcome new, talented individuals to our team.

We're committed to your success. DBD invests in your development and supports your career trajectory in any direction you want to take it.

Join us to play a key role in shaping the nuclear sector's future.

Make a difference today with DBD.

Title: Decision Science Analyst
Description:
Come join Pattison Food Group Ltd., a Jim Pattison business, and Canada’s largest Western-based provider of food and health products.

Pattison Food Group Ltd. is a certified Great Place to Work® and consists of Save-On-Foods and other well-known grocery banners. With nearly 300 retail locations, we proudly employ nearly 30,000 team members.

We have an exciting opportunity for a full-time Decision Science Analyst to join our More Rewards team in Langley, BC.

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Customer Data & Analytics, you will be responsible for leveraging all aspects of data and analytics to support large-scale projects, experimentation, and continuous improvement initiatives that guide decision-making and deliver high-quality insights. In this role, you will support the development and management of customer data and insights to support the More Rewards Loyalty Program, Marketing, Retail Operations and Merchandising.

You will be responsible for:


Collaborating with stakeholders to understand business questions, processes and KPIs.
Formulating testable hypotheses aligned with business questions and conducting statistical experiments in a live environment.
Writing SQL scripts to automate data pipelines and workflows.
Using Python scripts in a Jupyter Notebook environment for exploratory data analysis, data wrangling and statistical testing.
Developing and maintaining dashboards and visualizations in Power BI to convey insights, report on KPIs and test results.
Learning cloud-based analytics environments and big data tools to build analytics workflows on large datasets.
Discovering unique and creative insights to identify new opportunities for driving business value.
Becoming an expert in the relevant datasets within your analytics domain.
Supporting the Data & Analytics team with key projects.



You have:


A degree in a STEM discipline with 3+ years of relevant experience in data and analytics.
Advanced Python coding skills and understanding of SQL.
Experience with additional programming languages is an asset.
Experience using experimental design principles such as A/B and multivariate testing and working with business stakeholders to formulate testable hypotheses.
An understanding of advanced analytics and data science methods, such as anomaly detection, forecasting, and supervised & unsupervised learning.
Working knowledge of cloud environments, such as Azure, GCP or AWS. Experience with big data tools such as Spark and Databricks is an asset.



You are:


A confident communicator, both verbally and written, with strong interpersonal skills and the ability to communicate a story with data and insights to non-technical stakeholders.
A problem solver with strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
Effective in task management and delivering high-quality work.
Capable of multitasking and thriving in a fast-paced environment.
Self-motivated with the ability to work independently and collaboratively.



What we offer:

In addition to a highly competitive salary, we offer a great range of benefits, a company pension plan, free parking, exclusive team member offers, and opportunities for development and career progression.

If you’re looking to join a team that puts people first, you’re in the right place. Apply online today!

Compensation Details:

$83,394.00 - $104,243.00

The compensation offered for this position will take into consideration location, education, skills, experience and other factors.
