Title: IT Data Technologist - Facilities Services - UTK
Description:
Job Description

This position supports and improves the technology systems, applications, and data resources used within Facilities Services. This role ensures reliable application performance, accurate and meaningful data reporting, and effective technical support for end users to support daily operations and informed decision-making. By maintaining system functionality, analyzing operational data, assisting with technology improvements, and supporting enterprise platforms (e.g. Oracle Fusion Cloud, TDX, Kahua, SCADA/BAS systems, etc.) the position helps enhance efficiency, service delivery, and continuous improvement across the department.

Responsibilities


Collect, validate, maintain, and analyze data from Facilities Services systems to support operational and strategic decision-making.
Develop and distribute reports, dashboards, and data visualizations that provide actionable insights for leadership and departmental stakeholders.
Monitor data quality, resolve discrepancies, and maintain reporting standards, documentation, and data consistency across systems.
Identify trends and opportunities for process improvement, operational efficiency, and resource optimization through data analysis.
Collaborate with staff, vendors, and technical teams to support reporting tools, system integrations, application enhancements, and continuous improvement initiatives.
Performs data integration functions by designing, developing, and maintaining ETL processes that support the efficient extraction, transformation, and loading of data from multiple sources into usable datasets for analysis and reporting.
Develops and maintains scripts and automated workflows to support efficient data collection, processing, and preparation for analysis and reporting.
Administer, configure, maintain, and support Facilities Services enterprise applications and technology platforms, (e.g. Oracle Fusion Cloud, TDX, Kahua, SCADA/BAS platforms, etc.) and related operational technologies.
Troubleshoot and resolve application, software, system access, and user support issues while providing responsive customer service and technical assistance to end users.
Manage user accounts, permissions, security roles, and system access in accordance with university policies, procedures, and security standards.
Assist with system upgrades, testing, deployments, integrations, and small-scale technology projects to ensure reliable system performance and continuity of operations.
Collaborate with supervisors, technical staff, vendors, and departmental stakeholders to evaluate system improvements, recommend technology solutions, and support continuous improvement initiatives.
Design, build, and maintain systems for data collection, storage, and accessibility to support reliable data management and business operations.
Design, build, and maintain data warehouses, lake houses, and cloud data platforms to support scalable, secure, and reliable analytics and reporting.
Provide responsive customer service and technical support to Facilities Services staff by addressing technology, application, and process-related questions and issues.
Assist in maintaining effective service delivery by documenting procedures, tracking support requests, and ensuring timely resolution of user concerns.
Support training and knowledge-sharing efforts by creating user guidance, communicating system updates, and assisting end users with technology adoption.
Identify opportunities to improve workflows, customer experience, and operational efficiency through feedback, process evaluation, and collaboration with stakeholders.
Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives by supporting process standardization, service enhancements, and alignment with departmental goals and best practices.
Develop, maintain, and update user guides, standard operating procedures, process documentation, and training materials for Facilities Services systems and applications.
Provide end-user training and functional support for departmental technologies, applications, and reporting tools to promote effective system utilization.
Assist with on-boarding and ongoing education for staff by delivering one-on-one instruction, group training sessions, and system demonstrations as needed.
Document system changes, workflows, reporting standards, and technical procedures to ensure consistency, knowledge retention, and operational continuity.
Collaborate with departmental stakeholders and technical staff to identify training needs, improve user understanding, and support technology adoption and process improvement initiatives


Qualifications


Required Education and Experience:
Associate’s degree or vocational/technical training with at least 3 years of professional experience required in at least two of the following areas: data analysis, data warehousing, data visualization, application development, business intelligence, enterprise application support, statistical programming, or data prep.
Required Knowledge, Skills, Abilities:
Knowledge of data analysis principles, reporting methods, and data management practices used to support operational and strategic decision
making. Skill in troubleshooting, supporting, and administering enterprise applications, software systems, and end-user technologies.
Ability to analyze complex information, identify trends or discrepancies, and develop practical recommendations for process improvement and
operational efficiency.
Skill in developing documentation, training materials, and delivering end-user instruction for technology systems and business processes.
Ability to communicate effectively and provide professional customer service while collaborating with technical staff, vendors, leadership, and end
users. Knowledge of information technology standards, security practices, system access controls, and applicable policies and procedures related to enterprise systems and data management.
Proficiency in data analysis tools and programming languages (e.g., SQL, Python, R, etc.)
Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, etc.)
Solid understanding of AI and machine learning and the ability to engineer AI prompts.
Other Conditions:
Valid Driver’s license
Applicants must be legally authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis without need now or in the future for sponsorship for employment-based visa status.

Preferred Qualifications


Bachelors degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Business Administration, or a related field


Preferred Experience


A Bachelor’s degree plus 3 years of relevant experience
or an Associate’s degree plus 5–7 years of directly related experience,
or a combination of education, certifications, and progressively responsible experience demonstrating comparable competency


Work Location


University of Tennessee – Knoxville, TN Campus
This is an on-site position


Compensation And Benefits


UT market range: MR09
Find more information on the UT Market Range structure here
Find more information on UT Benefits here


About The College/Department/Division

UT Facilities Services is responsible for the care and keeping of more than 300 facilities and utilities systems in and around Knoxville. As the University of Tennessee Knoxville’s largest non-academic department, team members are responsible for facilities maintenance and repair, custodial needs, landscaping and lawncare, utility work, in-house and capital construction projects, facilities planning and design, environmental initiatives, pest control, campus event services, and facilities administration functions for both the flagship campus and the agricultural institute. The Facilities Services staff provides around-the-clock coverage to ensure students, staff, faculty, and fans have a consistent and positive experience every time they step foot on Rocky Top.

About Us

The University of Tennessee, Knoxville, has shaped leaders, changemakers, and innovative thinkers since its founding in 1794. The university is home to more than 38,000 students and 10,000 statewide employees—the Volunteers—who uphold the university’s tradition of lighting the way for others through leadership and service.

UT Knoxville offers over 900 programs of study across 14 degree-granting colleges and schools. As Tennessee’s flagship land-grant university, its footprint spans the entire state. The university holds the highest Carnegie classification for research activity and has deep partnerships with industry leaders and the US Department of Energy’s largest multidisciplinary laboratory, Oak Ridge National Laboratory.

The Knoxville campus serves and recruits for UT Knoxville, including the Institute of Agriculture and the Space Institute, as well as the UT Institute of Public Service.

UT Knoxville considers its employees its number one asset. With values that focus on work-life balance, compensation, and innovation leadership, all Vols are supported to advance professionally. Employees have access to career development and coaching, continued education, and an extensive list of development and training possibilities. The Volunteer employee experience implements structures and practices to attract and retain top-tier talent, fostering a strong staff community and supporting a culture of involvement and engagement for everyone.

The university holds a strong commitment to its land-grant mission of learning and engagement, with a tradition of service and leadership that carries that Volunteer spirit throughout the state and around the world. It has been ranked nationally as “Best Employer for New Graduates,” “One of America’s Best Large Employers,” and “Best Workplace for Women,” and has been designated as “Best Place for Working Parents” by Forbes Magazine.

Apply today and join the Tennessee Volunteer community!

Title: Research Analyst
Description:
CHAMINADE UNIVERSITY OF HONOLULU

MISSION STATEMENT

Chaminade University offers its students an education in a collaborative learning environment that prepares them for life, service and successful careers. Guided by its Catholic, Marianist and liberal arts educational traditions, Chaminade encourages the development of moral character, personal competencies, and a commitment to build a just and peaceful society. The University offers both the civic and church communities of the Pacific region its academic and intellectual resources in the pursuit of common aims.

I. Position Information

Title: Research Analyst

Division: Academic Affairs/Vice Provost

Department: Office of Institutional Research & Institutional Effectiveness

Exempt

Full Time

1.0 FTE

Staff

EAM/Grade: TBD by Human Resources

II. Position Summary

The Research Analyst is the primary technical and analytical staff role for the Office of Institutional Research & Institutional Effectiveness. The incumbent serves as the primary technical coordinator for assigned institutional research, institutional reporting, assessment-support, and compliance-reporting functions; produces and validates institutional reports; develops dashboards, one-pagers, census and post-census packages, and recurring data packets; supports required federal, state, accreditation, and other official reporting; and maintains the documentation, methodology notes, data dictionary, change log, and reporting repository needed to make institutional reporting repeatable, accurate, and dependable over time. The Research Analyst also supports a culture of evidence by helping institutional stakeholders understand what the data mean, what the limits of the data are, and how information should be interpreted for planning and improvement. The position may use institutionally approved AI-enabled tools to improve workflow efficiency, documentation, summarization, query support, data-cleaning processes, and draft reporting products while maintaining human review, data privacy, methodological transparency, and responsibility for validated institutional data.

III. Reports To

Direct Report to: Assistant Provost for Institutional Effectiveness

IV. Essential Duties And Responsibilities

Decision Support (25%)


Provides ad hoc analysis and decision-support for Cabinet, deans, chairs, program directors, committees, and accreditation work when requests align with office priorities and governance.
Supports request intake, triage, tracking, and prioritization for data and reporting requests, ensuring that requests are aligned with office priorities, data-governance expectations, and available capacity.
Translates technical findings into plain-language summaries, interpretation notes, dashboards, one-pagers, and other visual reporting products that are useful to non-technical audiences.
Supports a culture of evidence by helping units understand the meaning, appropriate use, limitations, and planning implications of institutional data.
Contributes to survey design, administration, reporting, environmental scans, benchmarking, and special studies as assigned.


Standard Reporting and Analytics (45%)


Produces and maintains recurring institutional reports and dashboards, including enrollment, census and post-census reporting, retention, graduation, student success, and other standard university metrics.
Develops and maintains program review data packets, fact book content, cabinet- and dean-level one-pagers, and other standard reporting products that reduce one-off work and improve consistency.
Supports assessment and academic program review reporting infrastructure, including WEAVE, assessment archives, program-review evidence packets, assessment documentation, and related reporting repositories as assigned.
Coordinates or supports the course/faculty evaluation survey process, including survey setup, data validation, summary reporting, documentation, and communication of results as assigned.
Supports grant-performance reporting and other externally funded project reporting by preparing validated data extracts, recurring performance summaries, and methodology documentation as assigned.
Rebuilds and improves high-use reports in the institution’s reporting environment so commonly requested information can be produced more accurately, more quickly, and with less reliance on manual workarounds.
Maintains a shared reporting repository, data dictionary, change log, documentation of systems and routines, and methodology notes so that reports can be understood, checked, and continued over time.
Conducts data quality review and validation in partnership with units such as the Registrar, Finance, Student Affairs, Academic Affairs, Information Technology, and Student Success.
Crafts reports and dashboards in PowerBI or similar reporting and visualization tools for use by operational units in the performance of their functional responsibilities.
Uses institutionally approved AI-assisted tools, when appropriate, to support documentation, report drafting, code or query review, survey-theme summaries, data-cleaning workflows, and workflow efficiency; verifies AI-assisted outputs before use in institutional reports, dashboards, or official submissions.


External Reporting and Compliance Support (30%)


Serves as the primary technical preparer and coordinator for assigned official reports, including IPEDS, Common Data Set, Financial Value Transparency/Gainful Employment, NSLDS student-level and program-level reporting, National Student Clearinghouse, state submissions, WSCUC/SARA-related reporting, accreditation data requests, and other required federal, state, accreditor, or institutional submissions as assigned.
Supports required external reporting by preparing data extracts, validating definitions, reconciling discrepancies, documenting methodology, and coordinating timelines for federal, state, accreditor, and other official submissions.
Coordinates with responsible offices on coding decisions, definitions, validation procedures, data-quality checks, interpretation notes, and repeatable workflows for high-stakes reporting.
Maintains the annual reporting calendar for assigned external and institutional reports, including timelines, definitions, data sources, responsible offices, validation steps, submission records, and methodology notes.
Documents assumptions, validation steps, limitations, and human-review procedures for AI-assisted analysis or reporting workflows when such tools are used.
Works with Information Technology, Data Governance, and the supervisor to ensure AI-enabled tools are used consistently with institutional policy, FERPA, data privacy, security expectations, and official-reporting standards.
Completes responses to surveys by publishers and other organizations when aligned with office priorities.
Performs other job-related duties as assigned.


V. Disclaimer

This list of responsibilities is not exhaustive. Details relating to both essential and non-essential functions of the job are not precise and are only an approximation, which can vary on a day-to-day basis and are subject to change on an as-needed basis and/or in the sole discretion of supervisory management.

VI. Education And Experience

Minimum


Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field such as higher education, social science, statistics, public policy, data analytics, information systems, or a related discipline.
Demonstrated experience in institutional research, higher education reporting, data analysis, business intelligence, or another role involving complex data management and interpretation.
Demonstrated ability to work accurately with large datasets, apply definitions consistently, maintain documentation, and use strong quality assurance practices.


Preferred


Master’s degree in a relevant field.
Experience in higher education institutional research, assessment support, accreditation reporting, or decision-support work.
Experience with student information systems, enterprise reporting tools, business intelligence platforms, or relational databases used in higher education.
Experience supporting IPEDS, Common Data Set, National Student Clearinghouse, WSCUC/SARA-related reporting, Financial Value Transparency/Gainful Employment, or other federal, state, accreditation, compliance-related, or official reporting submissions.
Experience developing dashboards, methodology notes, data documentation, data dictionaries, and recurring institutional reports.
Experience with assessment management systems, survey platforms, program-review documentation, academic assessment reporting, or course/faculty evaluation reporting.
Experience supporting grant-performance reporting or externally funded project reporting.
Knowledge of enrollment, retention, graduation, financial aid, student success, and academic program data structures.
Familiarity with AI-assisted features in Microsoft 365, PowerBI, SQL/query environments, survey tools, documentation tools, or similar platforms.


VII. Required Knowledge, Skills & Abilities


Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to interact and work with individuals at all levels of the University.
Ability to explain technical information clearly to faculty, academic leaders, operational units, and other non-technical users.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite or related software, with advanced skill in spreadsheets and data management tools.
Demonstrated skill in reporting tools, data visualization tools, and database or query environments appropriate to institutional reporting work.
Advanced skills in SQL and demonstrated experience working with relational databases. Familiarity with Ellucian Colleague, Slate, Canvas, PowerBI, or similar systems preferred.
Ability to manage data from disparate sources and reconcile differences across systems, definitions, timing, and reporting requirements.
Organized with meticulous attention to detail, thoroughness, documentation, and quality assurance.
Excellent project management skills and the ability to manage multiple recurring deadlines, prioritize work and resources, and maintain a predictable reporting calendar.
Broad experience in data reduction, analysis, documentation, interpretation, and presentation of research findings.
Ability to work collaboratively with offices such as the Registrar, Finance, Student Affairs, Academic Affairs, Information Technology, Student Success, and accreditation leaders.
Demonstrated AI literacy, including understanding of appropriate and inappropriate uses of generative AI in institutional research, reporting, documentation, and decision-support workflows.
Ability to use approved AI-enabled tools responsibly for productivity, documentation, summarization, and workflow support while protecting confidential student, employee, financial, and institutional data.
Ability to validate AI-generated summaries, code, formulas, visualizations, or analytic suggestions against source data, institutional definitions, and approved reporting methodology.
Understanding of privacy, confidentiality, FERPA-sensitive data handling, and institutional restrictions on entering protected or sensitive data into public or unapproved AI tools.


VIII. Physical Requirements

Duties involve light work in an office and community setting and require a minimum of physical effort with intermittent sitting, standing, climbing stairs, and walking inclines. Must be able to lift 20 pounds, walk the campus with ease, and have excellent motor skills. Must have finger dexterity. Must be able to communicate information and ideas so that others will understand.

IX. Other Requirements


This is a full-time, exempt, 1.0 FTE staff position. Forty hours per week (M-F) will be the general rule. However, depending on project cycles, external reporting deadlines, and workflow demands of the office, additional hours or workday schedule changes may occasionally be required. Must be willing and able to work occasional nights and weekends and a varying schedule.
Performs other related duties as assigned by Supervisor.
Observes all safety and health regulations and works in accordance with safe work procedures and policies of the University. Reports unsafe acts/conditions and injuries in a timely and proper manner.


X. Marianist Identity/Native Hawaiian & Pacific Island Serving

An expressed willingness to respect and support the Catholic and Marianist identity of Chaminade University of Honolulu and to perform all duties in a manner consistent with Marianist educational values and mission of the University. Understanding of and respect for the University’s designation as an institution that serves students of Native Hawaiian and Pacific Island ancestry.

Chaminade University offers its students an education in a collaborative learning environment that prepares them for life, service and successful careers. Title: Employee Benefits Data Analyst
Description:
Position Summary

An Employee Benefits Data Analyst plays a key role in supporting LGWIS Employee Benefits clients—particularly self-funded and captive accounts—by transforming complex data into clear, actionable insights. This role analyzes employee benefit plan data to evaluate financial performance, identify trends and risks, and support data-driven decision-making.

This position partners closely with Account Management, Sales, and Leadership teams to ensure data accuracy, integrity, and meaningful reporting. The Data Analyst also develops client-focused recommendations and communicates insights effectively to diverse audiences, including leadership, HR, finance teams, and benefit committees.

This is an evolving role that adapts to industry changes, client needs, and reporting requirements. The ideal candidate is highly analytical, detail-oriented, and able to translate complex data into clear, impactful insights.

This position may be performed out of our offices located in Billings, Bozeman, Great Falls or Helena, Montana. Candidates must reside or relocate to Montana to be considered for this position. After successful training, employees may transition to a hybrid work schedule.

Key Responsibilities


Analyze and interpret data related to self-funded and captive employee benefit plans
Identify trends, risks, cost drivers, and utilization patterns impacting healthcare spend
Ensure data accuracy by identifying and resolving discrepancies or incomplete data
Develop, maintain, and optimize databases and reporting systems
Transform complex data into clear, actionable reports and dashboards
Apply statistical methods to analyze trends and support forecasting and decision-making
Conduct data quality assessments to ensure accuracy, completeness, and consistency
Collaborate with internal teams to develop client recommendations and strategies
Present findings in a clear and tailored manner to both technical and non-technical audiences
Support client reporting, renewal processes, and strategic initiatives
Identify opportunities for process improvement, automation, and enhanced data governance
Assist with sales initiatives by supporting reporting and risk analysis efforts
Participate in client meetings, conferences, and presentations (up to 5% travel)


Qualifications


5+ years of experience in health insurance (carrier or TPA preferred)
7+ years of experience in data analytics/reporting, or a bachelor’s degree in a related field (e.g., Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Business, Information Systems)
Strong knowledge of medical claims (professional and facility)
Advanced Excel and proficiency in Microsoft 365 tools
Active Life & Health Insurance license (or ability to obtain)
Understanding of compliance requirements (HIPAA, ERISA, COBRA, ACA)
Experience in employee benefits, healthcare analytics, or financial analysis preferred


Technical Skills


Data cleaning, transformation, and validation
Statistical analysis (e.g., regression, correlation, hypothesis testing)
Database management and querying
Data visualization and dashboard development
Experience with reporting and analytics tools
Familiarity with automation and basic machine learning concepts (preferred)


Core Competencies


Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy
Ability to work independently and manage shifting priorities
Effective communication and presentation skills
Client-focused mindset with a solutions-oriented approach
Strong collaboration and teamwork skills
High level of professionalism, integrity, and accountability
Adaptability in a fast-paced, evolving environment


Additional Information


Occasional overtime during peak reporting periods
Participation in client meetings and industry events as needed
Commitment to ongoing professional development and compliance training


Benefits

Leavitt Great West offers the strength of a national brand with the heart of a local agency. We foster a culture of trust, collaboration, and professional growth.

You’ll Enjoy


A people-first culture that values work-life balance
Ongoing training and career development opportunities including paid license renewal and continuing education
The tools and support you need to succeed
The chance to work with reputable carriers and a diverse book of business
Recognition and rewards
Attractive Benefits package, including 401k and wellness program that allow you to earn dollars toward your HSA account
Health, Vision, Dental, HSA/FSA, & Life Insurance
Paid time off (vacation & sick time)


Ready to make your next move?

Apply today and help us continue delivering excellent service to our clients!

As a national company, the Leavitt Group is one of the largest privately-held insurance agency brokerage in the nation, with over 275 locations across 27 states. We broker commercial and personal lines insurance which manages the risk for individuals and businesses. The agency is appointed with the top insurance markets and enjoys exclusive representation with multiple insurance companies. We are always looking for talented individuals who want to further their career and grow with us.

Title: Data Specialist
Description:
Ready to help shape the future of data at Cass County Electric Cooperative? We’re looking for a full-time Data Specialist to join our Information Technology team at our headquarters in Fargo, ND.

In this role, you’ll play an important part in how we collect, validate, and use advanced metering infrastructure (AMI) data. Your work will help keep our metering systems accurate, reliable, and connected to the billing systems our members count on every day. You’ll also support meter data projects, software upgrades, system integrations, and process improvements that help move the Cooperative forward.

If you enjoy solving problems, working with technology, and finding meaning in the details, this could be a great next step in your career. The Data Specialist role offers a pathway into future growth opportunities in data analytics and data engineering.

What Will Help You Succeed


An associate’s degree in computer science, data analytics, information technology, or a related technical field; closely related, progressive work experience may substitute for a portion of the education requirement
Electric utility experience is preferred
Strong attention to detail and the ability to review, validate, and reconcile large volumes of data
Comfort using technology, databases, reporting tools, and software applications to process, organize, and interpret data
The ability to effectively use programming language
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Strong analytical, critical-thinking, and problem-solving skills
Proficiency with Microsoft Office 365


Why join us?

At Cass County Electric Cooperative, we’re proud to serve our members and support the communities we call home. We value collaboration, innovation, and people who are eager to make a difference. If you’re looking for meaningful work, a supportive team, and an opportunity to grow your career, we’d like to hear from you.

How To Apply

For a complete position description and to apply, please visit our website, https://casscountyelectric.com/careers

Application deadline: July 17, 2026

Cass County Electric Cooperative is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

The Kentucky Department of Education is a service agency of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, and part of the Education and Labor Cabinet. The department provides resources and guidance to Kentucky’s public schools and districts as they implement the state’s P-12 education requirements. The department also serves as the state liaison for federal education requirements and funding opportunities.

The department is led by an appointed commissioner of education, who is hired by and answers to the 11-member Kentucky Board of Education. Offices are administered by associate commissioners, and divisions within those offices are administered by division directors.

The department employs approximately 425 Frankfort-based workers; 175 employees in support of the Kentucky School for the Blind (Jefferson County) and Kentucky School for the Deaf (Boyle County); and about 518 employees at the 50 Area Technology Centers across the state.

Our Mission

Collaboration
Integrity


The Kentucky Department of Education (KDE), Office of Finance and Operations, Division of District Support, Student Tracking and Data Branch has an opening for an Data Analyst. This role represents a fantastic opportunity to work in a dynamic, challenging and professional environment. We invite you to apply for the opportunity to allow you to expand your learning potential, while receiving a steady income and benefits. We look forward to reviewing your application!

Responsibilities Include But Are Not Limited To The Following


Performs data analysis and research. Uses data-oriented programming languages and tools to collect, query, analyze, and report on data. Provides technical assistance to local school district personnel and other state educational agencies in the development, coordination, implementation and monitoring of the District and School Collection Repository (DASCR).
Analyzes, verifies and reports on student data in assigned school districts using the Kentucky Student Information System. Assists districts with student data tracking processes to improve data quality. Serves as data steward for DASCR, System Admin Schools and Districts and graduation code responsibilities. Makes system updates per Office of Education and Licensure Effectiveness (OELE) for colleges or universities that need to be added for high school and college credit.
Collects, analyzes, verifies, reviews and processes student, school and district data, including Functional Data Requirements (FRDs) for student attendance data, graduation codes (G Codes) and end-of-year processing. Collaborate with stakeholders to address data-related needs.
Oversees data request and ad hoc reports to ensure accuracy. Verifies information in the Person Role Manager System to maintain demographic information on administration, student & grade movement within the school system. Completes attendance and financial data program tracking sheets for school districts. Reviews and revises attendance audit reports.
Provides training and technical assistance to personnel in assigned school districts, including updates on legislative, regulatory and agency policy changes relating to attendance. Attends regional meetings in assigned school districts related to attendance and presents at regional and state meetings.
Provides training on cross-agency initiatives to include leadership initiatives and collaborative efforts to meet the Commissioner's objectives.


Preferred Skills

Familiar with working in the District and School Repository system and Infinite Campus.

Paid time off (sick and vacation) plus paid holidays
Overtime and compensatory time
Affordable health insurance with optional Health Reimbursement Account
Life insurance
Retirement
Wellness incentives program

Employee discounts on electronics; health and wellness; retail; travel, events and entertainment; and services and education
A collaborative and innovative work environment.
Flexible work schedules
Employee resource groups
Employee recognition

Minimum Requirements

EDUCATION: Graduate of a college or university with a bachelor's degree OR a graduate of a college or university with an associate's degree in information technology (IT) or related field OR certification in an IT-related field.

EXPERIENCE, TRAINING, OR SKILLS: Two years of professional experience in IT data analysis.

Substitute EDUCATION for EXPERIENCE: A master's degree in computer science will substitute for one year of the required experience.

Substitute EXPERIENCE for EDUCATION: Two years of additional related experience in IT data analysis will substitute for the education requirement.

SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS (AGE, LICENSURE, REGULATION, ETC.): NONE

Incumbents working in this job title typically perform duties in an office setting. May require minimal travel.

This job has an initial and promotional probationary period of 6 months, except as provided in KRS 18A.111.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Position Overview

Impact Scale is seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Analyst to support business operations by gathering, interpreting, and analyzing data to drive informed decision-making. The ideal candidate has experience working with data, identifying trends, developing reports, and providing actionable insights that improve business performance and operational efficiency.

In this role, you will collaborate with cross-functional teams to evaluate business processes, monitor key performance indicators (KPIs), identify opportunities for improvement, and support strategic initiatives through data-driven analysis.

Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis Reporting


Collect, analyze, and interpret data from multiple sources to identify trends, patterns, and business opportunities.
Develop and maintain reports, dashboards, and performance metrics for leadership and internal stakeholders.
Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and provide regular reporting on business performance.
Present findings and recommendations in a clear, concise, and actionable manner.


Business Analysis


Evaluate business processes and identify opportunities to improve efficiency, productivity, and performance.
Conduct research and analysis to support strategic planning and operational initiatives.
Assist in developing forecasts, business models, and performance analyses.
Support decision-making by providing data-driven insights and recommendations.


Process Improvement


Identify workflow inefficiencies and recommend process improvements.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to implement and monitor operational enhancements.
Document business processes, procedures, and best practices.


Cross-Functional Collaboration


Partner with departments across the organization to understand reporting needs and business objectives.
Support projects by gathering requirements, tracking progress, and analyzing results.
Communicate analytical findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.


Qualifications Skills


Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Mathematics, Data Analytics, Statistics, or a related field preferred.
2+ years of experience in an analyst, business analyst, data analyst, or similar analytical role.
Strong analytical and critical thinking skills with the ability to solve complex problems.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets.
Experience creating reports, dashboards, and data visualizations.
Strong attention to detail and organizational skills.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Experience working with business intelligence tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker Studio, or similar) is a plus.
Familiarity with SQL or other data querying tools is a plus.


What Success Looks Like


Delivers accurate, timely, and actionable reporting that supports business decisions.
Identifies trends and opportunities that improve operational efficiency and business performance.
Develops reliable dashboards and reporting tools that provide valuable insights to leadership.
Collaborates effectively across departments to solve business challenges.
Contributes to a culture of continuous improvement through data-driven decision-making.


Hours, Location Benefits


Flexible work hours, including remote work options.
Generous benefits package, including comprehensive health insurance, retirement savings, and paid time off.
Professional development opportunities and education reimbursement.

Title: Data Analytics Specialist
Description:
Are you looking for an exciting, innovative company with over 100 years of excellence and commitment to ethical business? Amentum is a trusted partner to United States government agencies, allied governments, and corporations worldwide supporting critical programs across a broad range of domains. We are seeking a highly skilled Data Analytics Specialist with 2-5 years of experience in data engineering and analytics. The ideal candidate will have strong expertise with toolsets for data mining, statistical modeling, optimization, and data visualization for the purpose of driving engineering insights.

Key Responsibilities


Design, develop, and optimize scalable data processing applications.
Write clean, efficient, and reusable Python code for data engineering and analytics tasks.
Analyze complex datasets to identify trends, patterns, and actionable engineering insights.
Implement data transformations, aggregations, and workflows to support analytics and reporting.
Ensure high data quality, integrity, and reliability across large-scale datasets.
Collaborate with scientists, engineers, analysts, and cross-functional teams to optimize data workflows and integrate solutions into existing systems.
Gather requirements from stakeholders and translate them into effective data solutions.
Participate in code reviews and uphold high coding standards and best practices.
Mentor junior team members and actively contribute to knowledge sharing within the team.
Stay current with emerging technologies and best practices in data automation, statistical simulation, and data visualization.


Requirements


2-5 years of hands-on experience in data engineering or analytics roles.
Bachelor of Science or advanced degree from an ABET accredited school.
Strong proficiency in Python.
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Ability to collaborate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Must have the ability to obtain and maintain a TS/SCI US Government Clearance. Interim Secret clearance required to start. Please note US Citizenship is required to obtain a Secret/ TS/SCI Clearance.
Experience mentoring junior developers is a plus.


Compensation Details:

$100,000 -$160,000 annually

The compensation range or hourly rate listed for this position is provided as a good-faith estimate of what the company intends to offer for this role at the time this posting was issued. Actual compensation may vary based on factors such as job responsibilities, education, experience, skills, internal equity, market data, applicable collective bargaining agreements, and relevant laws.

Benefits Overview:

Our health and welfare benefits are designed to support you and your priorities. Offerings include:


Health, dental, and vision insurance
Paid time off and holidays
Retirement benefits (including 401(k) matching)
Educational reimbursement
Parental leave
Employee stock purchase plan
Tax-saving options
Disability and life insurance
Pet insurance


Note: Benefits may vary based on employment type, location, and applicable agreements. Positions governed by a Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA), the McNamara-O'Hara Service Contract Act (SCA), or other employment contracts may include different provisions/benefits.

07/10/2026 - Until Filled

Title: Data Science Associate
Description:
University of Notre Dame in Notre Dame, IN, US

Analytics

" rel="noopener">

Notre Dame Athletics develops and challenges each student-athlete to pursue excellence and serve as a force for good in the world. Our vision is to lead collegiate athletics by defining the integrated student-athlete experience and forming champions. To do this, we are eager to attract, recruit, develop and retain individuals who positively contribute to a collaborative, connected culture where all may thrive.

As a prospective member of Notre Dame Athletics, we invite you to apply if you have a shared purpose for our distinctly integrated student-athlete experience, an unrivaled passion for people who propel a dynamic and high performing organization, and a proven record of success in your field.

The Data Science Associate will work at the intersection of data engineering and analytics, contributing to data processing, cleaning, and analysis in an agile manner across all domains of revenue efforts, with an emphasis on revenue generation, ticketing, marketing, content and partnerships. This role will leverage data engineering and analytical expertise to ensure data-driven decision-making across the Notre Dame Athletics revenue and business areas. Responsibilities include diagnosing system issues, refining code, and conducting functional testing to resolve application production challenges.

The Associate will collaborate closely with the Senior Associate AD for Revenue, Partnerships & Marketing and their team, the Back-End Database Engineer, Data Analytics Associate, and other functional area leaders to ensure seamless integration and functionality of data-driven revenue generation and business intelligence solutions.

Responsibilities & Duties.


Collaborate with the Back-End Database Engineer to seamlessly integrate data-driven solutions into existing and new systems, while partnering with revenue, ticketing, marketing, content and partnership staffs to ensure analytics tools align with and adapt to the evolving needs of Notre Dame Athletics.
Design, build, and maintain reliable data pipelines that support revenue, ticketing, marketing, content and partnership analytics.
Aligned with departmental best practices, implement and maintain data quality checks, logging, and monitoring to ensure accuracy and reliability of revenue-related datasets.
Issue optimized queries for use in stakeholder-facing tools like dashboards and other reporting mediums. Develop insights and build tools to address current and new revenue generating opportunities, enhance data accessibility, and support the revenue and external affairs teams.
Apply analytical expertise and programming skills to interpret and visualize data across all departmental needs of revenue-focused efforts (e.g., ticketing, partnerships, marketing, digital, fan experience, etc.).
Partner with functional leaders to shape metrics, definitions, and data models that drive actionable decision- making.
Facilitate internal research projects that contribute to novel and innovative revenue growth.
Other duties as assigned
*

Qualifications


Education Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree (4 years)
At least 3 years of professional experience
Degree Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in a data science related field; graduate-level training preferred.
Skill Qualifications:
Highly proficient with multiple data systems and experience in a variety of programming languages (Java, SQL, Python).
Proven experience with data in various forms (such as relational SQL, NoSQL, JSON, unstructured data environments).
Knowledge and skills required to formulate and pursue impactful questions regarding revenue generation, ticketing, marketing, CPM, CRM and partnerships.
Strong organizational skills, intellectual curiosity, stress tolerance, ability to handle uncertainty, and persistent desire to start and complete projects.
Exceptional verbal and written communication skills with the ability to dynamically adapt messaging to a diverse group of stakeholders.
Broad understanding of / and interest in sports business; Nights and weekends required at times.
*

Additional Information


Deadline to Apply (subject to change): Sunday, July 12th
Salary: up to 85K, commensurate with experience

Title: IT Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
To act as a key advisor to executive leadership, including but not limited to the CEO, CFO, COO, and CPO, shaping and advancing the organization’s data strategy to enable informed, enterprise-wide decision making. By collaborating across departments, this role transforms business requirements into automated, intuitive reporting and analytics solutions. Through the delivery of impactful insights and the promotion of data best practices, the position drives operational excellence and fosters a culture of continuous improvement. This position reports to the Vice President of Technology, who evaluates performance annually.

In addition, embodies the Goodwill Mission to: “Empower people to achieve their most abundant life” and upholds the Company Core Values of: Relentless Pursuit of Excellence, Customer First Mindset, Humble Confidence, Passion for Mission, and Do the Right Thing.

Compensation: $80,000

Through the delivery of impactful insights and the promotion of data best practices, the position drives operational excellence and fosters a culture of continuous improvement. This position reports to the Vice President of Technology, who evaluates performance annually.

In addition, embodies the Goodwill Mission to: “Empower people to achieve their most abundant life” and upholds the Company Core Values of: Relentless Pursuit of Excellence, Customer First Mindset, Humble Confidence, Passion for Mission, and Do the Right Thing.

Compensation: $80,000

Title: Strategic Assessment Analyst (AI & Data Systems)
Description:
Amentum is seeking a Strategic Assessment Analyst (AI & Data Systems) to provide operational planning, data integration, research, and analysis support to the US PACOM J5 Strategy Branch. This position serves as a critical member of the J5 Assessment Team, acting as the hub for assessment efforts among the directorates and our components.

The Strategic Assessment Analyst will support the J5 Strategy Branch by leveraging artificial intelligence, quantitative data modeling, and qualitative analysis to assess operational plans, programs, and strategic policies.

Primary Roles and Responsibilities:


Design, implement, and utilize Maven Smart System tools and agents to streamline and enhance operational and strategic assessment workflows.
Provide process methodology and analysis to execute comprehensive assessment functions, merging both quantitative and qualitative methodologies.
Engineer and maintain cross-domain data processes, ensuring that disparate data sources from various directorates and components are ingested, fused, and analyzed effectively.
Provide robust Knowledge Management solutions in the context of AI models, tools, and agents—specifically focusing on Maven Smart Systems—to ensure assessment data is accessible, reliable, and actionable.
Construct and deliver analytical products for use by the PACOM Commander and other senior leaders.
Develop automated dashboards, briefing documents, and decision papers on various topics to support senior leadership decision-making regarding recent, ongoing, or anticipated military activities.
Assist with the production and implementation of long-term, fully integrated Department of War (DoW) strategies by applying innovative AI approaches to campaign and contingency plan assessments.


Minimum Requirements:


Bachelor’s degree in data science, Operations Research, Information Technology, Systems Engineering, Applied Economics, National Security Studies, or related field.
Familiarity with military assessment processes, demonstrating proficiency in both quantitative and qualitative assessment design.
Proven ability to design, deploy, and use Maven Smart System tools and agents in a secure environment.
Demonstrated ability to use AI capabilities (e.g., large language models, machine learning algorithms, automated agents) to execute complex assessment functions.
Experience in engineering cross-domain data processes, with an understanding of data pipelines, architecture, and secure knowledge management principles.
At least 5 years’ experience working in military operations, either on an operations staff, in strategic planning, or within an intelligence/data fusion role.
Demonstrate excellent written and oral communications, with the ability to translate complex AI/data-centric findings into digestible strategic military advice.
Must have an active Top-Secret clearance, with TS/SCI eligibility later during employment. Note: US Citizenship is required to obtain Top-Secret Clearance.
This position may require a polygraph after employment.


Preferred Qualiﬁcations:


Master’s degree in operations research, Data Analytics, or a related technical discipline.
At least 3 years’ experience on a senior military staff (Service HQ, Major Command, Office of the Secretary of Defense, or the Joint Staff) specifically working within the Joint Planning Process (JPP).
Advanced proficiency in programming languages commonly used in data analysis and AI (e.g., Python, R, SQL).
Previous experience supporting a J5 Directorate or military strategy branch.


Compensation & Benefits

HIRING SALARY RANGE: $135K - $160K (Salary to be determined by the education, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities of the applicant internal equity, and alignment with market data.)

This position includes a competitive benefits package. For more detailed information on our Benefits and what it is like to work for Amentum: please visit our careers site: www.amentumcareers.com

Compensation Details:

$135K - $160K

The compensation range or hourly rate listed for this position is provided as a good-faith estimate of what the company intends to offer for this role at the time this posting was issued. Actual compensation may vary based on factors such as job responsibilities, education, experience, skills, internal equity, market data, applicable collective bargaining agreements, and relevant laws.

Benefits Overview:

Our health and welfare benefits are designed to support you and your priorities. Offerings include:


Health, dental, and vision insurance
Paid time off and holidays
Retirement benefits (including 401(k) matching)
Educational reimbursement
Parental leave
Employee stock purchase plan
Tax-saving options
Disability and life insurance
Pet insurance


Note: Benefits may vary based on employment type, location, and applicable agreements. Positions governed by a Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA), the McNamara-O'Hara Service Contract Act (SCA), or other employment contracts may include different provisions/benefits.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Role Description

Data Analyst

Associate III - Data Engineering

Who We Are

Born digital, UST transforms lives through the power of technology. We walk alongside our clients and partners, embedding innovation and agility into everything they do. We help them create transformative experiences and human-centered solutions for a better world.

UST is a mission-driven group of 29,000+ practical problem solvers and creative thinkers in more than 30 countries. Our entrepreneurial teams are empowered to innovate, act nimbly, and create a lasting and sustainable impact for our clients, their customers, and the communities in which we live.

With us, you’ll create a boundless impact that transforms your career—and the lives of people across the world.

Visit us at UST.com.

You Are

UST is seeking a product-oriented Data Analyst to design, analyze, and operationalize data objects, data quality rules, and analytics frameworks that drive pricing, search, and operational performance at scale.

The Opportunity


Analyze, document, and design data objects, dimensional models, and data quality rules for analytics and reporting use cases.
Ensure governance, compliance, documentation, and explainability across all data assets and analyses
Monitor and analyze key KPIs across business including established SLAs
Run Pricing and Search frameworks
Diagnose pricing vs. search vs. supply related performance issues through structured, hypothesis driven deep dive analysis.
Produce insights that inform operational levels, readiness for large sales events, and configuration changes.


This position description identifies the responsibilities and tasks typically associated with the performance of the position. Other relevant essential functions may be required.

What You Need


Experience range: 4-6 years of experience
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, a related technical field
3+ years of experience in data design, dimensional modeling, and analytics
Proven ability to work effectively both independently and in cross functional teams.
Excellent communication, documentation, and stakeholder management skills.
Strong customer focus with a high bar for quality and accuracy.
Ability to thrive in a fast paced, agile development environment.


Compensation can differ depending on factors including but not limited to the specific office location, role, skill set, education, and level of experience. UST provides a reasonable range of compensation for roles that may be hired in various U.S. markets as set forth below.

Role Location: Arkansas

Compensation Range: $62,000-$93,000

Benefits

Full-time, regular employees accrue a minimum of 10 days of paid vacation per year, receive 6 days of paid sick leave each year (pro-rated for new hires throughout the year), 10 paid holidays, and are eligible for paid bereavement leave and jury duty. They are eligible to participate in the Company’s 401(k) Retirement Plan with employer matching. They and their dependents residing in the US are eligible for medical, dental, and vision insurance, as well as the following Company-paid Employee Only benefits: basic life insurance, accidental death and disability insurance, and short- and long-term disability benefits. Regular employees may purchase additional voluntary short-term disability benefits, and participate in a Health Savings Account (HSA) as well as a Flexible Spending Account (FSA) for healthcare, dependent child care, and/or commuting expenses as allowable under IRS guidelines. Benefits offerings vary in Puerto Rico.

Part-time employees receive 6 days of paid sick leave each year (pro-rated for new hires throughout the year) and are eligible to participate in the Company’s 401(k) Retirement Plan with employer matching.

Full-time temporary employees receive 6 days of paid sick leave each year (pro-rated for new hires throughout the year) and are eligible to participate in the Company’s 401(k) program with employer matching. They and their dependents residing in the US are eligible for medical, dental, and vision insurance.

Part-time temporary employees receive 6 days of paid sick leave each year (pro-rated for new hires throughout the year).

All US employees who work in a state or locality with more generous paid sick leave benefits than specified here will receive the benefit of those sick leave laws.

What We Believe

We proudly embrace the values that have shaped UST since day one. We build our culture of Humility, Humanity, and Integrity. These values inspire us to nurture a people-first, human centric culture that fosters diversity, prioritizes sustainable solutions, and keeps our people and clients at the forefront of all decisions.

Humility

We will listen, learn, be empathetic and help selflessly in our interactions with everyone.

Humanity

Through business, we will better the lives of those less fortunate than ourselves.

Integrity

We honor our commitments and act with responsibility in all our relationships.

Equal Employment Opportunity Statement

UST is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any other applicable characteristics protected by law. We will consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records in accordance with state and local laws and “fair chance” ordinances.

UST reserves the right to periodically redefine your roles and responsibilities based on the requirements of the organization and/or your performance.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Permanent Full Time

We are seeking a highly analytical and solutions-oriented Data Analyst to support Workplace Benefits and Retirement.

This role offers the opportunity to work closely with cross-functional teams and our Field Sales and Service partners. The successful candidate will possess a deep curiosity for understanding data, adept at identifying anomalies, investigating discrepancies, and the ability to design and implement efficient data-driven solutions.

In addition, the Data Analyst will build and evolve analytical reporting using Power BI, Excel, and SQL. By digging into performance results, identifying key trends, and translating complex data into clear insights, the role provides the information needed for data‑driven action to help move our business grow.

What You Will Do


Analyze, interpret, and validate large data volume to identify trends, inconsistencies, and anomalies
Provide technical and functional support on complex compensation programs, support decision-making with sound judgement backed by data, and mentor team
Write and optimize SQL queries to support data retrieval and reporting
Maintain tools and scripts using VB.NET to support business needs
Translate complex datasets into clear, compelling insights and business narratives
Work with stakeholders to improve data availability, quality, and drive automation
Ensure data accuracy and integrity through validation, reconciliation, and quality checks
Help resolve issues, perform analysis, and support application enhancements
Document data flows, models, and solutions to ensure sustainability


What You Will Bring


3+ years of hands-on experience with:
Data modeling concepts and tools
SQL (query development, optimization)
VB.NET
Power Automate
Power Apps
Power BI
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to break down complex problems into manageable components
Ability to model, structure, validate, and document complex datasets to support accurate and reliable decision‑making
Experience improving manual operational processes, including the ability to streamline or automate validation workflows
Proven experience identifying errors, inconsistencies, and anomalies in data
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment
A proactive mindset — you seek to understand the purpose behind requests and consistently look for opportunities to improve processes and outcomes


The base salary for this position is between $65,800 - $109,700 annually. This represents base salary only and does not represent other variable compensation components of our total compensation ( i.e. annual bonus, commission etc). If you are selected to move forward in our recruitment process, your recruiter will be able to discuss additional details of our total rewards program with you.

Career opportunities will be open a minimum of 5 business days from the date of posting, closing dates will vary depending on the search activity. All applications received will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

Title: Data Analyst (Head Office)
Description:
Description

Position at Performance Auto Group

Drive for Excellence

Looking to join a fast-paced and an ever expanding organization? Be a part of our dynamic team at Performance Auto Group! We are currently seeking a Data Analyst to join our team.

The Data Analyst plays a pivotal role in driving strategy and decision-making across Performance Auto Group. By leveraging SQL, Python, and Google Suite, this role translates complex datasets into actionable business intelligence, creating dashboards and reports that inform key stakeholders.

Working collaboratively across departments, the Data Analyst identifies performance trends, ensures data integrity, and optimizes analytical methodologies to support organizational growth.

In this role, maintaining meticulous order is essential. Beyond technical analysis, the Data Analyst must implement rigorous, structured data management practices to ensure accuracy, facilitate reproducibility, and guarantee that complex information remains accessible and reliable for the entire organization.

About Us

Performance Auto Group, is a leading automotive group, whose purpose is to be the preferred automotive group by fostering fair and respectful relationships that positively impact our team, customers, partners, and communities. Our core values as an organization are the principles we were built on that guide us today:

Great Experience: Every customer is entitled to a great experience

Integrity: We are honest and transparent in our actions

Diversity: We are stronger through the inclusion of people, brands and ideas

Innovation: We passionately embrace opportunities for new and continuous improvements

Empowerment: We encourage and support our people in taking initiative

Giving Back: We positively impact the communities in which we live, work and raise families

Here’s What You’ll Deliver


Lead the ongoing evolution of our measurement and analysis tools and processes;
Manage tracking codes and pixels, along with related technology, throughout our marketing channels;
Mine data from multiple systems and provide comprehensive analysis and insights;
Work collaboratively with team members to deliver actionable data in friendly, digestible formats, including dashboards and reports;
Assist with the development and execution of data-driven marketing campaigns, working with vendors and other team members to execute;
Actively participate in team meetings to drive improvements to campaign performance and overall website metrics;
Other duties as assigned.


What do you need to succeed?


Post-secondary degree/Diploma in Computer Science or related field required.
3-5 years of work experience as a Data Analyst or in a related field is preferred.
Proficient with Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, and exposure to emerging tracking and data collection tools.
Experience with Google Data Studio or experience utilizing data visualization tools is highly desirable.
Must have a valid Ontario Driver's License and good driving record.
Proficient with SQL and Python
Expert command of spreadsheets and reporting tools.
Strong attention to detail, with excellent organization and project management skills.
Driven yet collaborative - you work well in an engaged, busy team environment.
Savvy and up-to-date with current digital marketing practices.
Proactive, organized, and results driven.
Proficient verbal and written communication skills.


What’s in it for you?


Excellent management support and guidance
Opportunities through our Internal career development program
Access to health, dental and vision insurance
Disability, critical illness and life insurance for the unexpected
Our Employee Assistance Plan (EAP) for you and your family's wellness
Discount on vehicles, service and parts for you and your family

Description

Position at Performance Auto Group

Drive for Excellence

Looking to join a fast-paced and an ever expanding organization? Be a part of our dynamic team at Performance Auto Group! We are currently seeking a Data Analyst to join our team.

The Data Analyst plays a pivotal role in driving strategy and decision-making across Performance Auto Group. By leveraging SQL, Python, and Google Suite, this role translates complex datasets into actionable business intelligence, creating dashboards and reports that inform key stakeholders.

Working collaboratively across departments, the Data Analyst identifies performance trends, ensures data integrity, and optimizes analytical methodologies to support organizational growth.

In this role, maintaining meticulous order is essential. Beyond technical analysis, the Data Analyst must implement rigorous, structured data management practices to ensure accuracy, facilitate reproducibility, and guarantee that complex information remains accessible and reliable for the entire organization.

About Us

Performance Auto Group, is a leading automotive group, whose purpose is to be the preferred automotive group by fostering fair and respectful relationships that positively impact our team, customers, partners, and communities. Our core values as an organization are the principles we were built on that guide us today:

Great Experience: Every customer is entitled to a great experience

Integrity: We are honest and transparent in our actions

Diversity: We are stronger through the inclusion of people, brands and ideas

Innovation: We passionately embrace opportunities for new and continuous improvements

Empowerment: We encourage and support our people in taking initiative

Giving Back: We positively impact the communities in which we live, work and raise families

Here’s What You’ll Deliver

  • Lead the ongoing evolution of our measurement and analysis tools and processes;
  • Manage tracking codes and pixels, along with related technology, throughout our marketing channels;
  • Mine data from multiple systems and provide comprehensive analysis and insights;
  • Work collaboratively with team members to deliver actionable data in friendly, digestible formats, including dashboards and reports;
  • Assist with the development and execution of data-driven marketing campaigns, working with vendors and other team members to execute;
  • Actively participate in team meetings to drive improvements to campaign performance and overall website metrics;
  • Other duties as assigned.

What do you need to succeed?

  • Post-secondary degree/Diploma in Computer Science or related field required.
  • 3-5 years of work experience as a Data Analyst or in a related field is preferred.
  • Proficient with Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, and exposure to emerging tracking and data collection tools.
  • Experience with Google Data Studio or experience utilizing data visualization tools is highly desirable.
  • Must have a valid Ontario Driver's License and good driving record.
  • Proficient with SQL and Python
  • Expert command of spreadsheets and reporting tools.
  • Strong attention to detail, with excellent organization and project management skills.
  • Driven yet collaborative - you work well in an engaged, busy team environment.
  • Savvy and up-to-date with current digital marketing practices.
  • Proactive, organized, and results driven.
  • Proficient verbal and written communication skills.

What’s in it for you?

  • Opportunities through our Internal career development program

  • Discount on vehicles, service and parts for you and your family
  • Discounts to retailers and service providers across the country
  • Free and engaging employee events
  • Did we mention we love giving back to our communities?!

Title: Supply Chain Data Analyst
Description:
Convergint is looking for a team-oriented, results-driven, Supply Chain Data Analyst, to join our amazing culture. As the Supply Chain Data Analyst, you will be responsible for maintaining vendor item master catalogs and provide reporting and analytics to support the source to pay process. This role involves tasks like data entry, updates, and resolving discrepancies, with a strong focus on data integrity and efficiency in operations. This role has an emphasis on support of the supply chain and sales teams, and motivation in building effective business relationships with vendors and customers alike.

Who You Are

You have a passion for providing world-class service to customers, colleagues and communities. You are a person of integrity with a commitment to growth, accountability and delivering results. You want to join an organization with a positive culture that embraces inclusion and allows everyone to be the best version of themselves at work and home.

Who We Are

With 20-years of proven growth and exceptional performance, our mission is to be our customer’s best service provider. We realize the importance of diversity in achieving that goal. Our company was built upon a solid foundation of 10 Values and Beliefs which drive our unmatched culture, making us the #1 global, serviced-based systems integrator in the industry. We take great pride in protecting the lives and assets of our customers and their communities with the solutions we provide.

What you’ll do with “Our Training and Your Experience”


Creating, updating, and managing item records within relevant systems.
Ensure the accuracy, completeness, and consistency of item data, including descriptions, classifications, units of measure, vendor information, pricing, and other relevant details.
Conduct regular audits to identify and correct errors, duplicates, or outdated records.
Manage the lifecycle of item records, including creation, updates, and decommissioning.
Support for item master data management, including troubleshooting issues and assisting with system enhancements.
Interact with suppliers, manufacturers, and distributors on an on-going basis, which may include negotiating prices, discounts, credit terms, and logistics.
Participating in the development and implementation of best practices for item master data management.
Work with Supply Chain leadership to develop and implement best practices, demand planning, and other optimizations in order to help grow the business and to ensure process transparency, fairness, impartiality, and confidentiality.
Collaborate with global counterparts to align with process standardization and consistency in reporting across all regions.
Provide reporting and data analytics to Supply Chain leadership to show trends, address discrepancies, and generate accurate forecasting.
Develop constructive and cooperative working relationships with internal stakeholders and vendors alike and maintain them over time.
Executes all work in accordance with Provincial/State, local regulations, general best practices as well as company health and safety policies and procedures.
Acts as “our customer’s best service provider” at all times thereby ensuring Convergint Technologies is the customer’s first choice for service.
Communicates with and works with the other team members consistently to improve overall operations of the Convergint Technology Center, and company.
Keeps up to date and informed on all company policies.
Presents a professional image of Convergint Technologies at all times in appearance and behavior.
Performs other duties and responsibilities as requested or required.


What You’ll Need


Strong attention to details.
Strong verbal, written and interpersonal communication skills and ability to deal with a wide cross section of people both internal and external to the business.
Excellent Excel, Word, and Outlook skills.
Excellent proficiency in financial software specifically related to purchase order entry, accounts payable, and inventory management.
Strong negotiation skills.
Strong financial knowledge and skills—ability to understand basic financials such as accounts receivable/payable, purchase orders, work orders, etc.
Excellent organizational skills and the ability to handle multiple projects tasks simultaneously.
Excellent attention to detail.
Strong flexibility to adapt to changing priorities, work processes, department structure, environment and technology in a dynamic work environment.
Self-driven and motivated to meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Strong analytical skills are necessary to resolve problems and look for solutions.
Strong ability to demonstrate independence, resourcefulness and tenacity.
Shows initiative – regularly engages in proactive behavior and looks for opportunities.


Requirements


Diploma and/or certificate in Business Administration or Purchasing required.
SCMP designation highly desirable.
Minimum of five years of procurement experience including elements of the supply chain
preferred.
Minimum of five years of negotiation and vendor management experience preferred.
Minimum of five years of customer service experience is required.
Experience in Ivalua, Salesforce, and NetSuite highly desirable.
Relevant industry experience in electrical, security, building automation, or fire is required


Company Benefits And Perks

Convergint fosters a supportive, accessible, and inclusive environment in which individuals of different backgrounds and identities are able to realize their maximum potential within the company. We offer a variety of programs and exceptional benefits to all of our colleagues:


Company Holidays and Paid Time Off
Immediate comprehensive benefit plan (medical, dental, etc.)
Employer contribution to RRSP
Work environment committed to safety.
Colleague Assistance Program
Tuition reimbursement
Competitive salary and compensation plan
Corporate Social Responsibility Day
And much more…


Convergint is committed to a culture of Inclusion and Diversity and is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Visit our Convergint careers site to learn more about the company and the exciting opportunities available.

Title: Data Analyst - Coquitlam
Description:
Data Analyst

Are you a Data Analyst with a passion problem solving? Factors Group are a local and fast-growing manufacturing and health and wellness company where your work will have a direct and meaningful impact on business growth. As part of a collaborative and continuous improvement environment, you’ll have the opportunity to both help us as we migrate to Fabric, and work on projects you’re passionate about, all while building your skills and advancing your career.

Permanent, Full-time

Monday – Friday, 8:00am – 4:30pm

Hybrid – 2000 Brigantine Drive, Coquitlam, BC

$75,000 – $90,000 per annum

In this role, you will:


Analyze and interpret data from multiple business systems to support informed decision-making.
Design and maintain dashboards and reports using Power BI, Tableau, and Excel.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to gather requirements and improve reporting solutions.
Identify and resolve data discrepancies to ensure data integrity and consistency across systems.
Support data governance initiatives and contribute to ongoing improvements in data quality and accessibility.


You have:


A Bachelor’s degree or diploma in Computer Science, Business Systems Analysis, or a related field.
3+ years of experience in data analysis, including data modeling and analytics.
Strong proficiency in Power BI, Tableau, Excel, and Power Query (DAX/LOD).
Working knowledge of t-SQL for data querying and manipulation (Python is an asset).
Experience working with diverse data sources, including databases, cloud platforms, and APIs.


You get:


Great benefits package (healthcare, health & personal spending accounts, wellness days, Employee & Family Assistance Program)
Company-provided RRSP
$300 per year to spend on company products
Employee discount pricing
Employee appreciation events & rewards
Free onsite parking
Bus pass reimbursement
Exclusive discounts with various vendors


Why us?


We’re much bigger than you think – 30+ natural health brands loved across North America.
Proudly Canadian-owned & operated
We are committed to sustainability across people, planet, and profit, embedding responsible practices into how we operate and grow as a business.


Learn more about us here: https://ca.naturalfactors.com/pages/our-nature

FGOffice

This position is open to all qualified applicants who are legally entitled to work in the country in which this job is located. Only electronic applications can be accepted. This position will close when a competitive candidate pool has been identified. We thank you all for your applications and a member of our team will contact you soon. All the best!

All Internal applicants will be given priority for consideration for the first five business days that this position is posted. After that five days, Internal applicants will be considered along with all other applicants; however, are guaranteed at minimum a call from the Talent Acquisition team.

Creates, publishes, and monitors Engineering reports through PowerBi, ensuring that it is modeled by a common data structure that satisfies the functional requirements of the area, and supports overall business objectives. Supports the optimization of business systems internal to Engineering and as affected, cross functionally through the process of continuous improvement.

Creates, maintains and publishes Engineering PowerBi business reports

Incorporate automation into Engineering business systems data collection for reporting purposes
Creates and maintains process documents pertaining to the creation and maintenance of PowerBi reports, SharePoint sites, Visual Studio programs, etc
Other duties as assigned

Experience in the design/development of SharePoint sites, PowerBi reports, Visual Studio, Visual Basic, .NET, Power Query, Python
