Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
About Block Labs

Block Labs is a premier technology studio operating at the bleeding edge of Web3, Artificial Intelligence, and iGaming. We don't just ship features; we engineer high-scale, production-grade platforms that power the next generation of digital products.

We are a collective of senior engineers, product strategists, and builders who refuse to compromise on architecture. Whether we are designing autonomous multi-agent AI systems, building decentralized financial infrastructure, or architecting high-frequency iGaming platforms, our standard is excellence.

We move fast, but we build for the long term. If you are looking to work alongside a team that values deep technical expertise, thoughtful system design, and product ownership, Block Labs is where you belong.

About The Role

We’re looking for a data-obsessed BI Analyst to turn blockchain and product data into crisp insights that drive growth, retention, and risk controls across our crypto-enabled iGaming products. You’ll own the end-to-end analytics stack for wallet funnels, wagering behavior, lifecycle marketing, and compliance—using SQL, DBT, Python, Amplitude and Tableau as your primary tools. In the future we planning transition from Tableau to other BI tools alternatives like Metabase or Apache Superset or others.

Key Responsibilities


Drive the business forward through proactive analytics - don't just fulfill dashboard requests; spot anomalies, frame the right question yourself, and bring actionable recommendations to Product / CRM / Risk before they have to ask..
Build, maintain, and iterate dashboards surfacing KPIs (GGR/NGR, ARPU, LTV, churn, deposit→wager conversion, fraud loss rate).
Write production-grade SQL (CTEs, window functions, incremental models) to model clean datasets from Clickhouse and on-chain tables.
Build and maintain AI-agent workflows that automate recurring analytics - anomaly investigation, root-cause loops etc.
Use Python (pandas, seaborn, sklearn, scipy) for analysis, data quality checks, lightweight ETL/backfills, API pulls (e.g., blockchain providers), and automation.
Work with on-chain data: parse EVM logs, token transfers (ERC-20/721), join labeled address datasets, analyze exchange/bridge flows, gas/fee dynamics, and wallet clustering.
Partner with Product, Marketing/CRM, Risk/Compliance, and Engineering to define event schemas and tracking plans; ensure data quality, lineage, and documentation.
Design and analyze experiments, cohort analyses, and attribution within iGaming and regional compliance constraints.
Investigate anomalies (bonus abuse, botting, arbitrage, suspicious wallet rings) and recommend mitigations.
Automate recurring reporting and alerting for KPI movements.


Must-haves


2+ years in BI or Data Analytics; crypto or iGaming exposure prefered.
Ambitions about building great analytics infrastructure.
Good SQL: complex joins, window functions, query optimization, and dimensional modeling.
Solid AI-collaboration craft — prompt & context engineering, spec-driven task framing, and eval-driven iteration. Comfortable decomposing business questions into precise, agent-executable steps and designing the right tools for the agent to call.
BI Versatility: Expert knowledge of Tableau (LODs, parameters, performance tuning) combined with a strong interest or experience in modern, open-source BI tools like Metabase or Apache Superset.
Proficient Python for analytics & data engineering: pandas, modular scripts/notebooks, API integrations, scheduling small jobs, and writing maintainable code.
Solid statistics and probability fundamentals for experimentation - hypothesis testing, sample size and power, confidence intervals, and the discipline to distinguish real signal from noise.
Practical understanding of crypto wallets and transaction flows.


Nice-to-haves


dbt, Great Expectations/Monte Carlo for data quality; orchestration with Airflow/Prefect.
Built or contributed to production AI agents for analytics — ReAct loops, RAG over a knowledge base , MCP servers for data sources, multi-agent orchestration. Knows how to curate domain knowledge for agents so they stop hallucinating.
Ability to produce on-chain analytics (Dune/Flipside/Goldsky/Nansen tables or self-indexed data; contract ABIs; entity labeling).

What kind of culture can I expect?

HTML:
About Block Labs

Block Labs is a premier technology studio operating at the bleeding edge of Web3, Artificial Intelligence, and iGaming. We don't just ship features; we engineer high-scale, production-grade platforms that power the next generation of digital products.

We are a collective of senior engineers, product strategists, and builders who refuse to compromise on architecture. Whether we are designing autonomous multi-agent AI systems, building decentralized financial infrastructure, or architecting high-frequency iGaming platforms, our standard is excellence.

We move fast, but we build for the long term. If you are looking to work alongside a team that values deep technical expertise, thoughtful system design, and product ownership, Block Labs is where you belong.

About The Role

We’re looking for a data-obsessed BI Analyst to turn blockchain and product data into crisp insights that drive growth, retention, and risk controls across our crypto-enabled iGaming products. You’ll own the end-to-end analytics stack for wallet funnels, wagering behavior, lifecycle marketing, and compliance—using SQL, DBT, Python, Amplitude and Tableau as your primary tools. In the future we planning transition from Tableau to other BI tools alternatives like Metabase or Apache Superset or others.

Key Responsibilities

  • Drive the business forward through proactive analytics - don't just fulfill dashboard requests; spot anomalies, frame the right question yourself, and bring actionable recommendations to Product / CRM / Risk before they have to ask..
  • Build, maintain, and iterate dashboards surfacing KPIs (GGR/NGR, ARPU, LTV, churn, deposit→wager conversion, fraud loss rate).
  • Write production-grade SQL (CTEs, window functions, incremental models) to model clean datasets from Clickhouse and on-chain tables.
  • Build and maintain AI-agent workflows that automate recurring analytics - anomaly investigation, root-cause loops etc.
  • Use Python (pandas, seaborn, sklearn, scipy) for analysis, data quality checks, lightweight ETL/backfills, API pulls (e.g., blockchain providers), and automation.
  • Work with on-chain data: parse EVM logs, token transfers (ERC-20/721), join labeled address datasets, analyze exchange/bridge flows, gas/fee dynamics, and wallet clustering.
  • Partner with Product, Marketing/CRM, Risk/Compliance, and Engineering to define event schemas and tracking plans; ensure data quality, lineage, and documentation.
  • Design and analyze experiments, cohort analyses, and attribution within iGaming and regional compliance constraints.
  • Investigate anomalies (bonus abuse, botting, arbitrage, suspicious wallet rings) and recommend mitigations.
  • Automate recurring reporting and alerting for KPI movements.

Must-haves

  • 2+ years in BI or Data Analytics; crypto or iGaming exposure prefered.
  • Ambitions about building great analytics infrastructure.
  • Good SQL: complex joins, window functions, query optimization, and dimensional modeling.
  • Solid AI-collaboration craft — prompt & context engineering, spec-driven task framing, and eval-driven iteration. Comfortable decomposing business questions into precise, agent-executable steps and designing the right tools for the agent to call.
  • BI Versatility: Expert knowledge of Tableau (LODs, parameters, performance tuning) combined with a strong interest or experience in modern, open-source BI tools like Metabase or Apache Superset.
  • Proficient Python for analytics & data engineering: pandas, modular scripts/notebooks, API integrations, scheduling small jobs, and writing maintainable code.
  • Solid statistics and probability fundamentals for experimentation - hypothesis testing, sample size and power, confidence intervals, and the discipline to distinguish real signal from noise.
  • Practical understanding of crypto wallets and transaction flows.

Nice-to-haves

  • dbt, Great Expectations/Monte Carlo for data quality; orchestration with Airflow/Prefect.
  • Built or contributed to production AI agents for analytics — ReAct loops, RAG over a knowledge base , MCP servers for data sources, multi-agent orchestration. Knows how to curate domain knowledge for agents so they stop hallucinating.
  • Ability to produce on-chain analytics (Dune/Flipside/Goldsky/Nansen tables or self-indexed data; contract ABIs; entity labeling).
  • Warehouse ecosystems knowledge Clickhouse, AWS, S3 buckets.
  • Data governance & privacy (GDPR, data minimization, retention policies).
  • Understanding of iGaming economics & KPIs: deposits, wagers, hold, GGR/NGR, RTP, bonus efficiency, RG metrics, and common fraud/AML patterns.
  • Experience with product analytics services like Amplitude, Mixpanel, Google Analytics.

What kind of culture can I expect?

Mature, mission-driven, and low-ego. We value clarity over noise, outcomes over theatrics, and pace without chaos. If you’re one of the smartest minds in your craft and want to build with other experts, you’ll feel at home here.

Title: Data Analyst (Payments)
Description:
As a Payments Data Analyst, you will help optimize the performance of our global payment infrastructure by turning transaction data into actionable insights. You will work closely with Payments, Product, Finance, Risk, and Data teams to identify opportunities for improving payment success rates, reducing costs, and delivering a better customer payment experience.

This role combines analytical thinking with a strong understanding of payment flows. You will investigate payment performance, identify trends and anomalies, evaluate experiments, and help drive data-informed decisions across the payments domain.

Responsibilities


Monitor and optimise payments performance, including approval rates, decline patterns, 3DS flows, timeouts, retries and routing rules.
Analyse and reduce fraud-related declines, collaborate on tuning fraud rules and improving risk thresholds.
Perform deep-dive analyses to discover patterns, trends and new insights across card schemes, PSPs, BINs, countries and methods.
Drive actionable outcomes: update routing logic, cascading strategy, risk rules, issuer-specific heuristics and operational configurations.
Own continuous monitoring: daily health checks, anomaly detection and alerts on key metrics.
Build dashboards and monitoring tools in Metabase / Amplitude / Tableau
Prepare clear and actionable analytics reports for internal teams and leadership.
Evaluate impact of new features, routing changes, ML versions, product experiments and provide data-backed recommendations.
Work closely with Product, Data, ML, Payments Operations and Fraud teams to design and implement improvements end-to-end.


You will thrive if you have…


Strong advanced SQL (NoSQL, window functions), ability to build metrics properly
Understanding of PSP behaviour, decline codes, 3DS, issuer logic, routing, tokens, cascading
Understanding of fraud basics (RDR, chargebacks, rule tuning, risk signals)
Ability to translate data into clear actions
Comfortable in a fast-moving, operational environment
Ownership mindset, strong attention to detail
Fluency in Python Data Science stack (Jupyter Notebook, Pandas)


Nice-to-haves


Knowledge of Russian
Experience with card schemes / PSPs and issuer-specific patterns
Hands-on work with 3DS flows, network tokens, or routing optimisation
Background in fraud prevention (rules, signals, chargebacks)
Work experience in fintech / high-load transactional products


Conditions

We know that great talent deserves great conditions, so here's what you can expect when joining us:


EU-based employment contract.
42 remote work days
21 vacation days/10 days paid sick leave
Transparent performance reviews twice a year, with bonus opportunities and salary adjustments.
Private medical insurance for you and your family, a corporate mobile plan (unlimited in Cyprus with roaming included), and interest-free support for car purchases.
Mindfulness & well-being support, including psychological assistance with 50% coverage.
50% coverage of school and kindergarten fees for your children.
Fully covered sports benefits, and also access to in-house electric scooters and bike rentals, and cycling purchase compensation.
Investment in your growth: paid language courses and access to suited-for-you development programs, including conferences, training programs, and coaching to support your professional journey.
A culture of recognition: a peer reward program to celebrate your contributions.
A fully equipped office in Limassol, with everything you need for deep work and collaboration.
Free breakfast options at the office, Wolt Allowance, and an in-house coffee bar with high quality drinks and a health bar stocked with nutritious snacks.
A strong engineering culture: international teams, corporate events, team buildings, and hackathons—because great work happens in great communities.


Recruitment Process


HR Interview (40mins)
Technical interview with the hiring team (1hr)
Experience shown on site: 2+
vacancy_type: Payment Operations

Title: Reporting Engineer
Description:
We are looking for a Reporting Engineer to join our Data Warehouse team. In this role, you will develop and maintain regulatory and in-house reporting solutions that ensure compliance, data accuracy, and reliable insights for both internal stakeholders and B2B clients.

You will work closely with software developers and the DWH team to maintain and improve our reporting ecosystem, troubleshoot existing queries, and ensure reporting evolves alongside product and data changes. This role plays an important part in maintaining data integrity, supporting regulatory requirements, and continuously improving reporting performance in a fast-paced environment.




Core responsibilities

Develop high-performing SQL queries and reporting solutions on large datasets.
Build and maintain regulatory, in-house, and B2B client reporting.
Troubleshoot and optimize existing reports, improving performance and data integrity.
Ensure data consistency by analyzing dependencies between reports.
Maintain and improve documentation for reporting logic, KPIs, and data transformations.
Identify and help reduce technical debt in reporting solutions.
Support the DWH team in maintaining and improving the reporting ecosystem.
Participate in agile ceremonies such as sprint planning and retrospectives.
Collaborate with both technical teams and business stakeholders as needed.
Identify and contribute to process and reporting improvements.




Experience and key competencies

Strong experience writing efficient SQL queries on large datasets.
Good understanding of relational databases (PostgreSQL) and database architecture.
Experience in reporting, data analysis, or data engineering roles.
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work independently.
Ability to adapt to changing priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Excellent spoken and written English.
Python or JavaScript/TypeScript knowledge and experience with tools like Prefect or Telerik is a plus.




Coolbet operates in a highly regulated environment. This role may require a gaming license in any jurisdiction in which the company operates, and this position may require the ability to secure and maintain a license in all relevant jurisdictions. A gaming license application utilizes personal data, a criminal and credit check, and fingerprints. Coolbet will cover all costs and provide access to an Identity Protection program.




Our benefits, Your happiness

Enjoy 28 days of vacation with the option to use your balance on business days only, effectively giving you up to 10 extra days off. Combined with flexible working hours, you can make the most of your time off.
Prioritize your well-being with monthly sports/health compensation, full sick leave coverage for the first 8 days, our in-house gym, and a chill games room to unwind.
Invest in your professional growth with an annual training budget that can be used for courses, certifications, workshops, and learning opportunities relevant to your role.
Our office is a cozy (and dog-friendly) haven right in the heart of the city. Enjoy complimentary fruits and beverages and free parking. It's the perfect place to be productive and feel right at home.
Be part of the most legendary events and make amazing connections with colleagues from across the entire company.
Thrive in a diverse, multicultural environment where innovation and new ideas are welcomed, independence is encouraged, success is celebrated, and you'll have the freedom to make a real impact alongside talented people from across the globe.
..plus, if you're not already a pro, we'll teach you how to play poker!




Recruitment flow

Interview - Technical Interview - Police record, and background check - Offer
Start date: ASAP
Open to candidates based in Estonia
Applications are open until we find the one - take the leap and apply now!




We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, ancestry, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, age, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status.




Compensation

At Coolbet, we carefully consider a wide range of factors when determining your salary, including market benchmarks, specific job family, background, location, skills, and experience. The typical range for this role is 3000€-3800€ gross per month. This reflects total compensation.




Location

Tallinn, Estonia




Coolbet is an award-winning gaming company delivering both B2C and B2B platforms across international markets. We offer sportsbook, casino, and poker experiences to players in 8 countries across 3 continents and provide advanced B2B solutions to operators in the US. Part of the Sega Sammy Creation gaming segment, our core team of 500+ experts collaborates across borders to create world-class experiences for both players and operators. With a strong presence at our Tallinn HQ, home to a diverse team representing over 50 nationalities, we bring different perspectives together to shape the future of gaming through continuous product development, a collaborative culture, and a strong commitment to responsible play. Find out more about Coolbet!

Title: Data Acquisition Specialist
Description:
Our Mission:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.

Our Vision:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.

The Role

As our Data Acquisition Specialist, you sit at the point where our customers’ factories meet our product roadmap. You go deep into electronics manufacturing processes, capture how they really work, and turn that complex reality into structured data, analytics, and reporting that the product team uses to decide which robotics capabilities to build next.

You are the technical face of Sunrise in these conversations: trusted by customer engineers to understand their process, and trusted internally to translate it into evidence the product team can act on. This is a balanced, hands-on role, part time in the field with customers, part time building the data foundation.

What You’ll Do

Capture and translate customer processes:


Work directly with customer engineering and operations teams to observe, document, and map their manufacturing processes (for example PCBA, assembly, inspection, test, and material handling).
Elicit and write up process requirements, constraints, cycle times, tolerances, and failure modes in a clear, structured form.
Translate domain language in both directions between customers and our product and engineering teams.


Structure data and build the analytics foundation:


Define what data we should capture across customer engagements, and design simple, durable structures to hold it.
Build and maintain the datasets, queries, dashboards, and reports the product team relies on, using strong SQL and Python and a BI tooling.
Keep the data clean, consistent, and trustworthy across many processes and customers. You own this lightweight infrastructure end to end.


Turn data into product insight:


Analyse process and capability data to surface patterns, reuse opportunities, and gaps that should shape the roadmap.
Produce the recurring reporting and one off analyses the product team use to prioritise.
Recommend where robotics capabilities will unlock the most value, backed by evidence.


Be a technical face of the company externally:


Represent Sunrise credibly with customer technical stakeholders, both on-site and remote.
Build trust by genuinely understanding both the manufacturing process and what modern robotics software and machine learning can and cannot do for it.
Feed customer reality back into product decisions, and clear technical narratives back out to customers.


What You’ll Bring


Hands-on analytical and data skills. SQL and Python, comfort structuring messy data into usable datasets, and building dashboards and reports in a BI tool. You don’t need to be a data engineer, but you should be able to stand up lightweight data infrastructure and own it.
Deep familiarity with manufacturing processes, from either a process or industrial engineering background in any manufacturing environment, or from deploying robotics and automation into manufacturing. You can walk a factory floor and understand what you’re seeing.
Requirements capture. The ability to elicit, structure, and clearly document complex technical requirements.
Customer-facing presence. Credible, trusted, and clear with technical stakeholders; comfortable being the external technical representative of the company.
Working understanding of modern robotics and machine learning. Enough to reason about feasibility and value. You don’t need to be a practitioner, but you need to understand what it takes.
Excellent written communication. Able to turn observation and data into crisp, decision-ready narratives.


Nice to have


Direct experience in electronics manufacturing (PCBA, box build, test, or EMS / contract manufacturing environments).
Experience working alongside or within a product team.
Familiarity with data pipeline or warehouse tooling, even if it isn’t your primary muscle.
Exposure to robotics, computer vision, or machine-learning-driven automation projects.


Location and travel


Remote-first, CET +/- 3Hrs
You’ll travel to customer manufacturing sites as projects require (minimum 50% travel), with the flexibility to be on the floor when it matters most.

Title: Reporting Engineer
Description:
Founded in 1999, the company has a premium listing on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange and is focused on regulated and regulating markets across its B2B business. By leveraging its proprietary technology, Playtech delivers innovative products and services to ensure a safe, engaging and entertaining gaming experience.



As the gaming industry's leading technology company, it combines business intelligence-driven software, services, content, and platform technology to drive excellence and innovation across the sector. Read more about who we are and what we do here: www.playtechpeople.com and www.playtech.com



Here at Playtech, we genuinely believe that people are our biggest asset. Diverse thoughts, experiences, and individual characteristics enrich our work environment and lead to better business decisions. Embracing differences and maintaining transparency in our processes is the core of Playtech's overall commitment to responsible business practices.



Ready to level up your career?



Playtech’s IMS team is on the lookout for a diligent, proactive, and detail‑oriented Reporting Engineer with strong analytical and system thinking skills





Job Description



Your influential mission. You will…



Design and develop high‑performing SQL queries and reporting solutions that handle large and complex datasets
Troubleshoot and optimize existing reports, ensuring faster performance, improved scalability, and stronger data integrity
Collaborate with cross‑functional teams to understand reporting needs and translate them into efficient technical solutions
Guarantee data consistency by analyzing dependencies between reports and proactively identifying potential issues
Continuously improve reporting processes, introducing automation and best practices to enhance efficiency


Qualifications



Components for success. You…



Have extensive knowledge of writing efficient SQL queries, spotting problematic areas, and optimizing them for performance
Possess strong experience working with large datasets and designing queries that balance speed and accuracy
Understand relational databases and database architecture at a deep level.
Demonstrate analytical thinking, attention to detail, effective time management, and strong collaboration skills
Are proactive and systematic in your approach, always seeking ways to improve processes and outcomes

You'll get extra points for…



Hands‑on experience with JavaScript, Python, or Java, especially in integrating reporting solutions with broader systems
Familiarity with data visualization tools or libraries that enhance reporting clarity
Ability to automate repetitive reporting tasks and streamline workflows

Thrive in a culture that values…



Collaboration and teamwork across diverse functions, where knowledge sharing is encouraged
Proactivity and ownership in solving complex problems, with freedom to propose new ideas
Systematic approaches that ensure scalability, reliability, and long‑term sustainability of solutions
Continuous improvement, curiosity, and a passion for learning new technologies
A supportive environment that balances high performance with personal growth


Additional Information



IMS UNIT



At the heart of our product ecosystem lies the Information Management Solution (IMS) - a powerful, fully‑integrated suite of tools designed to empower operators in every aspect of their business. Title: Web Analyst
Description:
We're looking for a Web Analyst with strong GA4, GTM, and SQL expertise who can turn data into actionable insights. You'll build dashboards, analyze performance, and tell compelling data stories that help teams make smarter decisions. If you're independent, collaborative, and enjoy working in an inclusive environment, we'd love to hear from you.




In this role, you are going to:

Establish, maintain, review, and continuously optimize Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and Google Tag Manager (GTM) configurations to ensure absolute data accuracy and integrity
Analyze and optimize dataLayer coverage and structure to ensure robust, scalable data collection
Create and implement measurement strategies perfectly aligned with business goals, defining custom dimensions, metrics, and advanced event tracking
Utilize GA4 with all its functionalities to perform deep-dive analyses, uncover trends, and build customized reports and dashboards that turn complex data into actionable business insights
Apply familiarity with modern marketing and tracking practices to safely implement, troubleshoot, and thoroughly test tracking scripts
Partner with marketing, product, and development teams to promote data-driven decision-making and ensure tracking specs are met
Collaborate with cross-functional teams on data analysis and segmentation; support A/B testing initiatives where relevant
Contribute to broader business insights analysis, working alongside the analytics team to connect web data to wider business performance questions
Keep up-to-date with the latest privacy regulations, GA4 features, and analytics best practices




To be successful in this role, you need to have:

Demonstrated experience setting up, maintaining, reviewing, and optimizing complex Google Tag Manager and GA4 environments
Strong understanding of dataLayer architecture, including the ability to analyze coverage gaps and guide developers on optimization
Strong data analysis skills, with the ability to leverage the full suite of GA4 functionalities to answer complex business questions
Hands-on experience implementing various marketing and tracking scripts, with a disciplined approach to testing and QA
Strong SQL skills for querying and analyzing raw GA4 export data
Excellent communication skills and proficiency in spoken and written English - capable of translating intricate tracking setups and raw data into clear, stakeholder-friendly insight




Preferred skills and experience:

Familiarity with iGaming metrics and KPIs
Experience working with Google BigQuery
Knowledge of Google Data Studio / Looker Studio for creating automated, dynamic dashboards
Familiarity with Google Search Console (GSC) to connect search performance with on-site user behavior
Sufficient knowledge of A/B testing and CRO methodologies to support other roles with advanced data validation and analysis
Solid understanding of web development technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) and how they impact tracking and DOM scraping
Google Analytics or Google Tag Manager certifications




We will show our gratitude by:

Offering working tools of employees´ own choice - Company makes sure everyone feels comfortable in the office and has all the necessary tools one might need
Creating an exciting and fun international English-speaking environment
Working in a modern office in Ülemiste City
Organizing different company and team events
Offering extra sports compensation via Stebby + private health insurance
Creating an opportunity to learn and develop
Offering a competitive salary based on experience and a benefits package




If this seems like a challenge you are looking for, apply and let’s have a chat!




Sounds great but what do we do?

We are a growing company focused on the use of data to benefit e-commerce operations. Our Data Scientists provide decision-making assistance and models to improve operational efficiency and customer experience. The Analytics team provides insights on specific topics. Our Business Intelligence team makes information accessible. Together with the right technology we deliver the foundation for highly automated and effective CRM solutions.

Learn more about NabuMinds: https://nabuminds.com




As we strive to achieve our mission we reaffirm our commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI). We are proud to employ people of all backgrounds who possess the talent, energy, and focus to accelerate our vision forward and to tell our story. Each employee is encouraged to bring their whole self to work, knowing that NabuMinds values individuality as much as we value collaboration.

Title: Business Analyst - Masterdata Governance
Description:
Job description:



Business Analyst – Masterdata Governance

Hybrid working

 

 

Tirlán

Tirlán, is a world-class food and nutrition co-operative, with a diverse portfolio of quality ingredients, leading consumer and agri brands.

Tirlán owns leading brands such as Avonmore, Kilmeaden Cheese, Millac, Solmiko, Truly Grass Fed, Premier Milk, Wexford, mymilkman.ie and GAIN Animal Nutrition.

We nurture a talented team across Ireland, the US, MEA and China, delivering annual revenues of circa €3 billion, and circa 2,000 people to manage our network of 11 processing plants and 52 agri branches. Our purpose is to nourish the world, while safeguarding our environment for future generations.

 

Role Profile

We have an opportunity for a Business Analyst to support implementation of a Master Data Governance strategy, with a strong focus on SAP master data analysis, data quality control, governance assurance, and process improvement. This is a highly analytical, control-focused role working across business and IT functions to strengthen the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of master data.

 

Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Quality

Analyse SAP master data to identify data quality issues, including missing values, inconsistencies, duplicates, outliers and invalid records
Perform root-cause analysis to understand if issues are caused by system configuration, process gaps, control weakness, user behaviour or data migration activity
Support data cleansing and remediation activity, validating outcomes through profiling, reconciliation and post-remediation checks

Data Controls & Governance

Analyse large datasets from SAP and integrated systems to identify trends, exceptions, control gaps, and data integrity risks
Design and maintain data quality controls, exception checks, and validation routines to support proactive master data governance
Support master data cleansing, remediation, and ongoing control monitoring across key master data objects

Analytics & Control Monitoring

Use SAP Analytics Cloud, BI, Excel, and other analytical tools to monitor data quality, control effectiveness, and remediation progress
Provide insight-led analysis on recurring issues, control failures, process inefficiencies, and opportunities to improve master data governance

 

Requirements

Bachelor’s Degree (Business Information Systems, Accountancy or IT) and/or relevant experience and training
Minimum 3–5 years’ experience working in SAP or enterprise data environments.
Proven experience analysing SAP data, interpreting table-level information, and identifying data quality or control issues
Strong capability in data analysis, profiling, cleansing, root-cause investigation, and governance control techniques
Advanced Excel and data analysis capability, with the ability to manage and interpret large, complex datasets.
Experience developing data quality monitoring, exception analysis, or control assurance solutions
Exposure to SAP testing, data migration, or transformation programmes
Strong analytical and problem-solving ability
High attention to detail and data accuracy
Experience managing large, complex datasets
Ability to engage effectively with technical and business stakeholders

 

We offer

Hybrid working (flex Fridays)
Free parking incl. EV charging – where applicable
On-site gym free to use for employees
Parenting room
Work/life balance and collaborative work culture
Career progression opportunities

 

About us

Through our expertise in research and development, customer insights, food safety and state-of-the-art manufacturing we bring innovative food and nutrition solutions to a global market.  Our new and vibrant headquarters in Kilkenny City operates as a collaboration hub where multi-functional teams come together in a hybrid work environment.

 

Commitment to Diversity & Inclusion
We believe in the value of difference. We know that different perspectives, life experience and backgrounds can drive innovation and deliver growth.

We welcome people from all walks of life without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, or disability status. 

 

Join our Talent Pool

Considering a new career in Tirlán but this isn’t the role for you? Join our talent community and sign up for job notifications at www.tirlan.com/careers

 

Tirlán is a Values Based Organisation

Title: Manufacturing Data Product Analyst
Description:
At Deep DSG, we help global manufacturers unlock the full value of their operational data through modern industrial architecture, connected systems, and advanced analytics.

As we continue to expand our Data Enablement capabilities, we are seeking a Manufacturing Data Product Analyst to support the development and delivery of industrial data solutions that drive smarter, more connected manufacturing operations.

This role will focus on the design, configuration, and implementation of industrial data products, data pipelines, and integration solutions. Working closely with Data Enablement Leads, OT engineers, IT teams, and manufacturing stakeholders, you will help transform operational requirements into scalable data solutions that improve visibility, decision-making, and enterprise connectivity.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone with a strong technical foundation in manufacturing systems, industrial data platforms, and system integration who is looking to further develop their expertise in modern industrial data architectures, including Unified Namespace (UNS), DataOps, MQTT, and enterprise data platforms.

Requirements


Support the design, development, and deployment of Unified Namespace (UNS), Industrial DataOps, and industrial data solutions.
Assist in translating manufacturing and business requirements into scalable data models and integration architectures.
Develop, configure, and maintain industrial data products that support manufacturing, quality, maintenance, and business intelligence initiatives.
Design and support data pipelines that connect plant-floor systems with enterprise applications and analytics platforms.
Implement data transformation, contextualization, validation, and governance processes.
Configure and support industrial data brokers, messaging infrastructure, historians, and integration platforms.
Collaborate with Data Enablement Leads, manufacturing teams, OT engineers, IT personnel, and implementation partners on project delivery.
Contribute to the development and adoption of technical standards, best practices, and data governance frameworks.
Support testing, deployment, troubleshooting, and continuous improvement activities across Data Enablement initiatives.
Stay informed on emerging technologies and industry best practices within industrial data management and smart manufacturing.
Experience working with manufacturing systems, industrial automation, or industrial data solutions.
Knowledge of industrial data architectures, data modelling, and system integration principles.
Familiarity with Unified Namespace (UNS), Industrial DataOps, MQTT, or similar industrial data frameworks.
Experience developing or supporting data pipelines, data integration, or industrial software solutions.
Exposure to platforms such as HighByte Intelligence Hub, HiveMQ, AVEVA PI System (OSIsoft PI), or comparable technologies.
Understanding of manufacturing operations and OT/IT environments.
Familiarity with industrial middleware, historians, and enterprise integration platforms.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to work through technical challenges independently and as part of a team.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
Degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Information Technology, Data Engineering, or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good.

Your impact

This is a great time to join Jacobs’ Dublin team as we scale our analytics capability within transportation. You’d be working on BusConnects, a major infrastructure programme that will directly improve daily commutes and make the city more sustainable. It’s a real greenfield opportunity as the team is still maturing, so you’ll help shape processes, influence how analytics is done, and have a tangible impact from day one. There’s strong growth in the pipeline, with leadership heavily investing in this space.

With a chance to make a real difference to real peoples day-to-day lives, why wouldn't you want to make an impact?

Day to Day


Frame ambiguous business problems into structured data analysis questions and testable hypotheses
Apply core Analytics techniques including descriptive, inferential, and predictive analysis
Perform statistical analysis using regression, hypothesis testing, sampling, and confidence intervals
Conduct exploratory data analysis to identify patterns, trends, and anomalies in complex datasets
Build reproducible analytical workflows using Python and notebook environments
Use AI tools to enhance data analysis, code generation, and insight discovery with careful validation
Query, validate, and manipulate data using SQL across multiple data sources
Create clear data visualisations and insight-led narratives tailored to different audiences
Communicate analytical findings in a way that supports decision-making without overstating certainty
Collaborate with stakeholders to refine data requirements and ensure outputs are relevant and accurate
Assess data quality, bias, and limitations, and clearly articulate what the data can and cannot support
Work within data governance, privacy, and ethical frameworks at all times



Technical Stack


Python for data analysis including pandas, numpy, scipy, statsmodels
SQL for data extraction, transformation, and validation
Jupyter or similar notebook tools for reproducible data analysis
Azure-based data platforms including Fabric, Synapse, and Databricks
Data pipeline tooling such as Azure Data Factory
AI and copilot tools to support and accelerate analytical workflows
Data visualisation using matplotlib, seaborn, Plotly or similar
Version control and CI/CD practices using Git



What’s In It For You


Apply your Data Analyst skills to deliver insight on major infrastructure and transformation programmes
Accelerate your career as a Data Analyst in a high-growth, high-visibility environment
Work alongside experienced analysts, data professionals, and domain experts
Develop both your technical data skills and your stakeholder-facing consulting capability
Be part of a collaborative culture where data-driven thinking is valued and encouraged
Gain exposure to diverse datasets, sectors, and analytical challenges
Learn to apply data analysis in complex, real-world environments with measurable impact
Take ownership of your work and grow your capability in a supportive, forward-thinking team



Benefits


Competitive salary aligned to experience
33 days paid holiday per annum with the option to purchase more
Stock purchase options
Inclusive employee networks and diverse community
Family planning support including fertility services
Enhanced parental leave and support programmes
Income protection for long-term security
40 hour working week with flexible working patterns
Wellbeing hub with access to apps such as Calm and Daily Burn
Professional development, paid memberships, and CPD opportunities
Charity match funding, volunteering time, and donations



#Digital&Data

Here's what you'll need

Key Skills & Experience Required


Strong experience with
SQL
Python
Microsoft Fabric, Synapse, and Databricks
Exposure to AI and advanced analytics
Familiarity with Generative AI (AI augmentation concepts)
Experience using analytical tools that support AI-driven insights
Communication & Storytelling
Strong ability to communicate insights clearly
Capable of translating data into meaningful business stories
Data Management & Governance
Solid understanding of data quality and assurance principles
Knowledge of data completeness and data lineage
Engineering Best Practices
Experience with version control (e.g. Git)
Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and deployment practices
Cloud Environment
Primary focus on Microsoft Azure
Experience with AWS or Google BigQuery is transferable
Leadership & Growth
Will be expected to support and mentor others

Joining Jacobs not only connects you locally but globally. Our values stand on a foundation of safety, integrity, inclusion and belonging. We put people at the heart of our business, and we truly believe that by supporting one another through our culture of caring, we all succeed. We value positive mental health and a sense of belonging for all employees.

With safety and flexibility always top of mind, we’ve gone beyond traditional ways of working so you have the support, means and space to maximize your potential. You’ll uncover flexible working arrangements, benefits, and opportunities, from well-being benefits to our global giving and volunteering program, to exploring new and inventive ways to help our clients make the world a better place. No matter what drives you, you’ll discover how you can cultivate, nurture, and achieve your goals – all at a single global company. Find out more about life at Jacobs.

We aim to embed inclusion and belonging in everything we do. We know that if we are inclusive, we’re more connected and creative. We accept people for who they are, and we champion the richness of different perspectives, lived experiences and backgrounds in the workplace, as a source of learning and innovation. We are committed to building vibrant communities within Jacobs, including through our Jacobs Employee Networks, Communities of Practice and our Find Your Community initiatives, allowing every employee to find connection, purpose, and belonging. Find out more about our Jacobs Employee Networks here.

Jacobs partners with VERCIDA to help us attract and retain talent from a wide range of backgrounds. For greater online accessibility please visit https//www.vercida.com/uk/employers/jacobs to view and access our roles.

As a disability confident employer, we will interview disabled candidates who best meet the criteria. We welcome applications from candidates who are seeking flexible working and from those who may not meet all the listed requirements for a role.

We value collaboration and believe that in-person interactions are crucial for both our culture and client delivery. We empower employees with our hybrid working policy, allowing them to split their work week between Jacobs offices/projects and remote locations enabling them to deliver their best work.

Your application experience is important to us, and we’re keen to adapt to make every interaction even better. If you require further support or reasonable adjustments with regards to the recruitment process (for example, you require the application form in a different format), please contact the team via Careers Support.

It’s a real greenfield opportunity as the team is still maturing, so you’ll help shape processes, influence how analytics is done, and have a tangible impact from day one. There’s strong growth in the pipeline, with leadership heavily investing in this space.

With a chance to make a real difference to real peoples day-to-day lives, why wouldn't you want to make an impact?

Day to Day

  • Frame ambiguous business problems into structured data analysis questions and testable hypotheses
  • Apply core Analytics techniques including descriptive, inferential, and predictive analysis
  • Perform statistical analysis using regression, hypothesis testing, sampling, and confidence intervals
  • Conduct exploratory data analysis to identify patterns, trends, and anomalies in complex datasets
  • Build reproducible analytical workflows using Python and notebook environments
  • Use AI tools to enhance data analysis, code generation, and insight discovery with careful validation
  • Query, validate, and manipulate data using SQL across multiple data sources
  • Create clear data visualisations and insight-led narratives tailored to different audiences
  • Communicate analytical findings in a way that supports decision-making without overstating certainty
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to refine data requirements and ensure outputs are relevant and accurate
  • Assess data quality, bias, and limitations, and clearly articulate what the data can and cannot support
  • Work within data governance, privacy, and ethical frameworks at all times


Technical Stack

  • Python for data analysis including pandas, numpy, scipy, statsmodels
  • SQL for data extraction, transformation, and validation
  • Jupyter or similar notebook tools for reproducible data analysis
  • Azure-based data platforms including Fabric, Synapse, and Databricks
  • Data pipeline tooling such as Azure Data Factory
  • AI and copilot tools to support and accelerate analytical workflows
  • Data visualisation using matplotlib, seaborn, Plotly or similar
  • Version control and CI/CD practices using Git


What’s In It For You

  • Apply your Data Analyst skills to deliver insight on major infrastructure and transformation programmes
  • Accelerate your career as a Data Analyst in a high-growth, high-visibility environment
  • Work alongside experienced analysts, data professionals, and domain experts
  • Develop both your technical data skills and your stakeholder-facing consulting capability
  • Be part of a collaborative culture where data-driven thinking is valued and encouraged
  • Gain exposure to diverse datasets, sectors, and analytical challenges
  • Learn to apply data analysis in complex, real-world environments with measurable impact
  • Take ownership of your work and grow your capability in a supportive, forward-thinking team


Benefits

  • Competitive salary aligned to experience
  • 33 days paid holiday per annum with the option to purchase more
  • Stock purchase options
  • Inclusive employee networks and diverse community
  • Family planning support including fertility services
  • Enhanced parental leave and support programmes
  • Income protection for long-term security
  • 40 hour working week with flexible working patterns
  • Wellbeing hub with access to apps such as Calm and Daily Burn
  • Professional development, paid memberships, and CPD opportunities
  • Charity match funding, volunteering time, and donations


#Digital&Data

Here's what you'll need

Key Skills & Experience Required

  • Strong experience with
    • SQL
    • Python
    • Microsoft Fabric, Synapse, and Databricks
  • Exposure to AI and advanced analytics
    • Familiarity with Generative AI (AI augmentation concepts)
    • Experience using analytical tools that support AI-driven insights
  • Communication & Storytelling
    • Strong ability to communicate insights clearly
    • Capable of translating data into meaningful business stories
  • Data Management & Governance
    • Solid understanding of data quality and assurance principles
    • Knowledge of data completeness and data lineage
  • Engineering Best Practices
    • Experience with version control (e.g. Git)
    • Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and deployment practices
  • Cloud Environment
    • Primary focus on Microsoft Azure
    • Experience with AWS or Google BigQuery is transferable
  • Leadership & Growth
    • Will be expected to support and mentor others
Joining Jacobs not only connects you locally but globally. Our values stand on a foundation of safety, integrity, inclusion and belonging. We put people at the heart of our business, and we truly believe that by supporting one another through our culture of caring, we all succeed. We value positive mental health and a sense of belonging for all employees.

With safety and flexibility always top of mind, we’ve gone beyond traditional ways of working so you have the support, means and space to maximize your potential. You’ll uncover flexible working arrangements, benefits, and opportunities, from well-being benefits to our global giving and volunteering program, to exploring new and inventive ways to help our clients make the world a better place. No matter what drives you, you’ll discover how you can cultivate, nurture, and achieve your goals – all at a single global company. Find out more about life at Jacobs.

We aim to embed inclusion and belonging in everything we do. We know that if we are inclusive, we’re more connected and creative. We accept people for who they are, and we champion the richness of different perspectives, lived experiences and backgrounds in the workplace, as a source of learning and innovation. We are committed to building vibrant communities within Jacobs, including through our Jacobs Employee Networks, Communities of Practice and our Find Your Community initiatives, allowing every employee to find connection, purpose, and belonging. Find out more about our Jacobs Employee Networks here.

Jacobs partners with VERCIDA to help us attract and retain talent from a wide range of backgrounds. For greater online accessibility please visit https//www.vercida.com/uk/employers/jacobs to view and access our roles.

As a disability confident employer, we will interview disabled candidates who best meet the criteria. We welcome applications from candidates who are seeking flexible working and from those who may not meet all the listed requirements for a role.

We value collaboration and believe that in-person interactions are crucial for both our culture and client delivery. We empower employees with our hybrid working policy, allowing them to split their work week between Jacobs offices/projects and remote locations enabling them to deliver their best work.

Your application experience is important to us, and we’re keen to adapt to make every interaction even better. If you require further support or reasonable adjustments with regards to the recruitment process (for example, you require the application form in a different format), please contact the team via Careers Support.

Title: Data Analyst (Dealing / Trading Analytics)
Description:
We are a leading trading platform that is ambitiously expanding to the four corners of the globe. Our top-rated products have won prestigious industry awards for their cutting-edge technology and seamless client experience. We deliver only the best, so we are always in search of the best people to join our ever-growing talented team.

We are hiring a Data Analyst to partner closely with our Dealing team to improve trading and operational decision-making through data. This person will build and maintain analytics and reporting capabilities, translate business needs into well-scoped data solutions, and deliver actionable insights to support product evaluation and day-to-day operations. Strong SQL and Python skills are required, along with proven experience in trading/fintech/financial markets.

Responsibilities:


Partner day-to-day with the Dealing team to support trading and operational workflows with data and insights
Build and enhance analytics and reporting capabilities to support product evaluation, trading performance, and operational monitoring
Gather business requirements and apply market/trading context to scope data requests, define metrics, and recommend practical solutions
Analyze large, disconnected datasets to synthesize insights and highlight risks/opportunities
Collaborate with business leaders and cross-functional stakeholders to enable market-facing solutions powered by data
Communicate findings clearly (written and verbal) and deliver solutions reliably and on time
Ensure data quality by validating inputs, monitoring key metrics, and improving definitions/documentation where needed



Requirements:


Strong SQL and database experience (querying, joining, modeling/structuring data for analysis)
Advanced working knowledge and experience with Python
Basic statistical knowledge and ability to apply it appropriately in analysis and reporting
Demonstrated experience manipulating, processing, and extracting value from large, messy, and/or disconnected datasets
Strong understanding of trading/dealing concepts and market microstructure (execution, pricing, liquidity, slippage, exposure, hedging basics)
Familiarity with anti-fraud / trading abuse patterns and investigation workflows
Experience working with API connections (REST/WebSocket)
Experience with machine learning / deep learning / data science methods (nice to have)



What you will get in return:


Competitive Salary: We believe great work deserves great pay! Your skills and talents will be rewarded with a salary that makes you feel valued and motivated
Work-Life Harmony: Join a company that genuinely cares about you - because your life outside of work matters just as much as your time on the clock
Generous Time Off: Need a breather? Our annual leave policy lets you recharge and enjoy life outside of work without a worry
Employee Referral Program: Love working here? Share the love! Bring your talented friends on board and get rewarded for growing our awesome team
Comprehensive Health & Pension Benefits: From medical insurance to pension plans, we’ve got your back. Plus, location-specific benefits and perks!
Workation Wonderland: Live your digital nomad dreams with 30 extra days to work remotely from anywhere in the world (some restrictions apply). Adventure awaits!
Volunteer Days: Make a difference! Take two additional paid days each year to support causes you care about and give back to the community



Be a key player at the forefront of the digital assets movement, propelling your career to new heights! Join a dynamic and rapidly expanding company that values and rewards talent, initiative, and creativity. Work alongside one of the most brilliant teams in the industry.

Title: Analytics Engineer, Marketplace Strategy & Planning
Description:
Brief Info About Vinted

Our mission is to make second-hand the first choice, and we're looking for people who want to help us get there. Every day, we work together to help our members buy and sell pre-loved clothing and lifestyle items, giving each piece a second life – or even a third.

The Vinted Group is made up of three business units that support this mission:

Vinted Marketplace is Europe’s leading platform for second-hand fashion and a go-to destination for all kinds of pre-loved items, with a growing range of categories. Our platform connects millions of members across 20+ markets, helping great items find a new life.

Vinted Go enhances the shipping experience with a vast network of over 500,000 pick-up and drop-off points, partnering with more than 60 carriers across Europe, with added services like item verification for peace of mind on high-value pieces.

Vinted Pay is the newest part of the Vinted Group, dedicated to bringing secure, reliable payments to buyers and sellers across Europe. Seamlessly integrated into the Vinted app, it helps keep every transaction safe, efficient, and easy for our members.

Founded in 2008 in Lithuania, Vinted began as a way for friends to find new homes for clothes they no longer needed. In 2019, we became Lithuania's first unicorn! Today, our headquarters remain in Vilnius, and we've grown with offices across Europe, supported by a team of over 2,000 people.

Information about the position

As an Analytics Engineer in the Marketplace Strategy & Planning team, you will build analytical data models, dashboards, and other data products that provide insight into the performance of Vinted’s Marketplace and help shape our strategy.


About the team: Marketplace Strategy & Planning domain identifies growth opportunities for Vinted’s Marketplace in different markets, categories and other business opportunities. As the data team inside Strategy & Planning we quantify and measure our Marketplace strategy, including setting OKRs, defining metrics, tracking markets and competition, and forecasting of our performance. We work on highly visible strategic projects, with a wide range of stakeholders and data sources, and deliver the data products to support strategic decision making.
About the role: This is an end-to-end analytical role, where you will architect, build and maintain complete data products. You will work closely with two other Analytics Engineers in the Marketplace Strategy & Planning domain and be part of Vinted’s broader 40+ person Analytics Engineering Guild, benefiting from shared best practices, tooling, and regular knowledge sharing sessions.


In this position, you’ll


Architect and build scalable and reliable data models and pipelines in dbt, BigQuery, and Looker that turn internal and external source data into datasets and dashboards used for reporting, forecasting, and analyses.
Implement data quality checks, tests, and controls to improve the reliability and trustworthiness of our data.
Translate stakeholder needs into technical delivery by gathering requirements, aligning metric definitions, and turning them into well-documented models and metrics.
Create and enable data products that help stakeholders understand marketplace and competitor performance and make informed decisions.


About You


2+ years of working experience (or alternative activities resulting in a similar level of proficiency) as an Analytics Engineer or a closely related role (e.g. BI Engineer, Data Engineer or Data Analyst) with ownership of production pipelines
Strong technical skills, comfortable in our analytics engineering tech stack, which includes SQL/Google BigQuery, dbt, Python, Looker/LookML, Git.
Experience with data modeling and interested in developing and maintaining data products at scale, while balancing scalability, efficiency and reliability.
Excellent attention to detail. You care about data quality, edge cases, and consistency across pipelines, metrics and dashboards. You value documentation and clean, tidy code
Proactive and independent: you look for ways to iterate on and improve the data products you and your team own, and improve stakeholder experience.
You communicate clearly and proactively, and can be a reliable partner for business and technical stakeholders both within the domain and the broader organisation. You are comfortable in ambiguous and complex situations.


Studies show that women and members of underrepresented communities often apply for jobs only if they meet 100% of the qualifications. If you don’t recognise yourself in all of the above, you might still be an excellent candidate for this position. If this role excites you, we encourage you to apply.

Work perks


The opportunity to benefit from our share options programme
25 working days of holiday
Access to all the tools & tech needed for work
Home office support: we provide IT workstation equipment and a personal budget of up to €540 for home workplace furniture
Private health insurance
Digital mental and emotional health support and Employee Assistant Program (EAP)
Frequent team-building events
A personal monthly budget for shopping on Vinted
The opportunity to spend up to 90 days per year - 21 of which can be spent working outside of the EU - on workation
A dog-friendly office
In Vilnius office: Gym & in-house meals at friendly prices
In Kaunas office: a monthly lunch allowance, and a once-a-week provided in-house lunch and breakfast


Working at Vinted

Individual Learning Budget

We invest in your professional growth! As part of our commitment to continuous learning, we offer an annual learning budget to support your personal and career development through courses, certifications, workshops and more.

Hybrid Work

We’ve adopted a hybrid workplace model where 2 days in the office are recommended but not enforced. It’s up to you and your team to decide on the exact days you’ll spend working together in person.

Equal Opportunity

The Vinted Group is committed to building an inclusive workplace where people from all walks of life feel a sense of belonging. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and life experiences. At Vinted, all applicants are treated fairly without regard to their race, age, religion or belief, sex, national origin, citizenship, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic.

The gross monthly salary range for this position is:

€3.433—€4.650 EUR

Title: Supply Chain Master Data Analyst
Description:
The Company

Dexcom Corporation (NASDAQ DXCM) is a pioneer and global leader in continuous glucose monitoring (CGM). Dexcom began as a small company with a big dream: To forever change how diabetes is managed. To unlock information and insights that drive better health outcomes. Here we are 25 years later, having pioneered an industry. And we're just getting started. We are broadening our vision beyond diabetes to empower people to take control of health. That means personalized, actionable insights aimed at solving important health challenges. To continue what we've started: Improving human health.

We are driven by thousands of ambitious, passionate people worldwide who are willing to fight like warriors to earn the trust of our customers by listening, serving with integrity, thinking big, and being dependable. We've already changed millions of lives and we're ready to change millions more. Our future ambition is to become a leading consumer health technology company while continuing to develop solutions for serious health conditions. We'll get there by constantly reinventing unique biosensing-technology experiences. Though we've come a long way from our small company days, our dreams are bigger than ever. The opportunity to improve health on a global scale stands before us.

Meet The Team

New ideas have a way of becoming not only great products but also best in class practices that can significantly enhance the customer experience. At Dexcom you can make a difference in the lives of millions across the world!

Reporting to the Sr Supply Chain Manager, this role contributes to the coordination of Engineering Change Orders (ECOs) and supports product lifecycle activities, ensuring that system data is accurate and updated in a timely manner. You will help enable effective planning, scheduling, warehouse and execution processes aligned with Dexcom’s operational standards.

Where You Come In


You will collect, validate, and update master data required for Purchasing (Buy Items), Production Planning (Make Items), Warehousing and Inventory processes
You will ensure data accuracy and completeness within ERP systems
You will support coordination and implementation of Engineering Change Orders across make and buy items, assist with updating system data in line with approved changes, Track ECO progress and escalate issues where required
You will assist in managing Phase-in / phase-out of parts, Product revision changes
You will ensure updates are reflected accurately in systems to support production and procurement readiness
You will provide accurate data inputs to support MRP and planning activities
You will support resolution of data-related issues impacting Supply Chain operations
You will identify and support improvements in data accuracy and processes
You will follow established data governance and change control procedures


What Makes You Successful


You have had exposure to master data, planning and purchasing processes
You have familiarity with item master data structures
You are an advanced user of Microsoft Excel, Copilot, Outlook, Teams and Smartsheets is preferable
You have experience in the medical device or CPG industry
You have experience with Oracle EBS, Oracle Fusion, PLM and Anaplan systems networks is advantageous


Experience And Education Requirements


Typically requires a Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Operations Management, or a related field and a minimum of 2-5 years of related experience.


What You’ll Get


A front row seat to life changing CGM technology.
A full and comprehensive benefits program.
Growth opportunities on a global scale.
Access to career development through in-house learning programs and/or qualified tuition reimbursement.
An exciting and innovative, industry-leading organization committed to our employees, customers, and the communities we serve.


Travel Required


0-5%


To all Staffing and Recruiting Agencies: Our Careers Site is only for individuals seeking a job at Dexcom. Only authorized staffing and recruiting agencies may use this site or to submit profiles, applications or resumes on specific requisitions. Dexcom does not accept unsolicited resumes or applications from agencies. Please do not forward resumes to the Talent Acquisition team, Dexcom employees or any other company location. Dexcom is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes/applications.

Title: Senior IT Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Senior Data Analyst

The Role

As a Senior Data Analyst, you will work closely with business and technical teams to drive the creation of scalable and documented data design solutions. They drive adoption of enterprise and industry standard modeling methodologies. They partner with project teams to deliver practical & resilient data model designs that meet business needs and are capable of easily adapting to future business requirements.

The Expertise and Skills You Bring

Requirements And Analysis


Engage with business and technology partners to understand the business requirements, analyze data gaps, and recommend appropriate solutions.
Assess objectives, use cases, data requirements and specifications documents created by other analysts
Analyze and resolve major data issues that arise during the course of projects or in production environments


Data Design


You are an experienced enterprise data modeler who can define conceptual and logical data models, with strong definitions, to capture organizational data needs across multiple subject areas
Create physical design from logical data models for different types of systems such as operational systems, data warehouses, data lakes and others
Research to identify effective data designs, new tools and methodologies for data analysis
Document and present database solutions to product partners for approval.
Promote common data API adoption and reuse.
Map Technical terms to Business terms in data catalog tool.


Technical Leadership


Ensure technology decisions align with enterprise standards and strategy.

Title: BI Analyst
Description:
Position: BI Analyst (Tableau & Revenue Operations)

Location: On site (Larnaca or Limassol)

Department: Data & Analytics




About Us

Our company is a leading provider of cloud contact center solutions that enable businesses of all sizes to deliver exceptional customer experiences. Our innovative platform offers a full range of features and capabilities, including intelligent routing, IVR, call recording, analytics, and more. We are growing rapidly and expanding our global footprint, and we are looking for a talented Sales professionals to help us expand our client base.




Role Overview

We are looking for a proactive BI Analyst to become the core owner of our Tableau ecosystem. Our organization is heavily sales-driven, meaning your work with data will directly impact our revenue growth. You will be responsible for building high-quality interactive dashboards, managing our Tableau Server, and working closely with the Revenue team to track the sales pipeline and conversion rates.




The ideal candidate is someone who is technically skilled in Tableau and SQL but also has the "business curiosity" to understand how our operations works and where we can improve.




Key Responsibilities

1. Tableau Development & Admin

Dashboarding: Create functional, interactive, and visually polished dashboards in Tableau that stakeholders actually enjoy using.
Server Maintenance: Manage Tableau Server (handling user access, permissions, and organizing the project structure).
Data Freshness: Manage and monitor Tableau Extracts and subscriptions to ensure data is always up-to-date and accurate.

2. Sales & Revenue Support

Funnel Analysis: Build and maintain reports on sales pipeline, conversion stages, and lead velocity.
Ad-hoc Reporting: Provide quick, accurate data insights using Excel or Tableau to support urgent business decisions.
Proactivity: Don’t just build what is asked — analyze the data and suggest where the business might be missing opportunities.

3. Collaboration & Processes

Requirements Gathering: Communicate with business users to understand their needs and turn them into clear technical requirements.
Data Pipeline: Coordinate with data engineers to explain what datasets you need to build the best possible dashboards.




Requirements

Experience: 2–4 years of experience in BI or Data Analytics.
Tableau Stack: Proficiency in Tableau Desktop (LODs, Parameters, Sets) and experience with Tableau Server administration.
SQL: Solid ability to write queries to extract and transform data.
Excel: Advanced user (Pivot tables, complex formulas, data cleaning).
Business Mindset: Understanding of basic sales metrics (Pipeline, Conversion, Win Rate, Churn, MRR).
Language: Professional working proficiency in English.




Nice to have:

Python: Basic scripting for data tasks.
Statistics: Understanding of core statistical concepts.
Vibe Stack: Cursor for development, n8n for workflow automation, and an understanding of how to prompt/deploy AI agents for data tasks.




Key Competencies

Attention to detail: You double-check your numbers before showing them to the manager.
Organization: You can manage multiple requests without losing track of priorities.
Communication: You can explain "what the data says" to someone who isn't a data person.




What We Offer:

Play a crucial role in growing our product.
Attractive compensation and benefits.
Top-notch work gear.
Embrace a flexible hybrid work model.
Free lunch and healthy snacks every day.
Medical insurance.
A team of talented and fun colleagues from different countries.




At Voiso, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to fostering an inclusive environment for all team members.
