Title: VYRESNIOJI DUOMENŲ ANALITIKĖ (-AS)
HTML:
Ar nori tapti „Ignitis Group“ dalimi?

Šiuo metu ieškome naujos (-o) komandos narės (-io) duomenų analitikos komandoje. Šioje pozicijoje būsi atsakinga (-as) už analizę, elektros energijos prekybai vykdyti. Manai, mums pakeliui? Tuomet laukiame tavęs savo komandoje!

Take YOUR part in #EnergySmart!

Prisidėsi prie žalios ir saugios energetikos ekosistemos kūrimo:


  • Kuriant analitinius įrankius duomenų gavimui, analizei, procesų optimizavimui, procesų stebėsenai ir automatizacijai;
  • Analizuojant duomenis, ieškant dėsningumų ir teikiant įžvalgas verslo sprendimams;
  • Kuriant, testuojant ir tobulinant prognozavimo bei analizės modelius;
  • Kuriant įrankius kitiems įmonės specialistams, savarankiškoms ataskaitoms;
  • Vykdant didmeninės rinkos modeliavimą;
  • Inicijuojant ir diegiant naujus analitikos sprendimus;
  • Tobulinant ir administruojant automatizuotas prekybos sistemas;
  • Ieškant naujų analitikos, automatizavimo ir dirbtinio intelekto sprendimų.


Šiai pozicijai svarbu:


  • Aukštasis išsilavinimas (ekonometrijos, statistikos, matematikos ar kitų panašių tiksliųjų mokslo srityse);
  • Ne mažesnė nei 2 metų patirtis, kuriant analitinius sprendimus duomenų analizės ar modeliavimo srityje;
  • Geri programavimo įgūdžiai su „Python“, „VBA“ bei „SQL“ programavimo kalbomis;
  • Analitinis mąstymas, duomenų raštingumas, geri problemų sprendimo įgūdžiai;
  • Gebėjimas analizuoti didelius duomenų kiekius ir sudėtingas problemas paversti aiškiais sprendimais.


Privalumas būtų:


  • Ankstesnė patirtis elektros, kitų žaliavų, akcijų ar valiutų biržoje;
  • Su energetika susijusių duomenų dalinimosi platformų išmanymas;
  • Elektros rinkos veikimo principų išmanymas;
  • Patirtis kuriant prognozavimo modelius ar taikant dirbtinio intelekto sprendimus.


Mainais už tvaresnės ateities kūrimą, mes pažadame:


  • Papildomų naudų paketą pasirenkant: pensijų kaupimą, sveikatos draudimą ar kitą naudą iš pasirenkamų naudų sąrašo „MELP“ programėlėje;
  • Papildomas apmokamas dienas: sveikatinimuisi, trumpalaikės ligos atveju, savanorystei, už sukauptą darbo stažą;
  • Nelaimingų atsitikimų draudimą;
  • Hibridinį darbo modelį (3+2) ir lankstų darbo laiko grafiką;
  • Vidines karjeros galimybes;
  • Plačias mokymosi, kvalifikacijos kėlimo programas;
  • Dėmesį fizinei ir emocinei sveikatai: gerovės mentorių iniciatyvas ir psichologinę pagalbą, tinklinio ir krepšinio komandas ir t.t;
  • Galimybę prisidėti prie energetikos transformacijos, siekiant sukurti 100% žalią ir saugią energijos ekosistemą dabarties ir ateities kartoms.


Kviečiame prisijungti prie augančios tarptautinės komandos. Ją sudaro išmaniajai energetikai atsidavę specialistai, kuriuos vienija bendras tikslas – sukurti 100% saugią ir žalią energetikos ekosistemą dabarties ir ateities kartoms. O būdas tai pasiekti – tikrai ne laukti tinkamo laiko. Mes tikime, kad energija veikti įsikrauname patys. Suvienyti prasmės. Geriausi ten, kur svarbu. Prisiimame iššūkius. Drąsūs atrasti ir keistis. Tai mūsų vertybinės nuostatos, drauge jos kuria energiją, kuri yra visko, ką mes darome, varomoji jėga. Energiją, kuri virsta mūsų pasirinkimais. Jei ir tu vadovaujiesi šiomis vertybinėmis nuostatomis – lauksime savo komandoje!

Siekdami strateginių tikslų padidinti lyčių balansą „Ignitis grupėje“, pareigų pavadinimus darbo skelbimuose nurodome pirmiausia moteriškos giminės galūne.

„Ignitis grupė“ yra lygias galimybes užtikrinanti darbovietė, kurioje laukiami visi kandidatai, nediskriminuojant jų dėl rasės, religijos, lyties, seksualinės orientacijos ar lyties tapatybės, amžiaus, negalios ar dėl kitų su darbo vykdymu tiesiogiai nesusijusių požymių.

Pareigybių grupės pastovios atlyginimo dalies intervalas: yra nuo 3430 iki 5150 EUR (neatskaičius mokesčių). Konkretus darbo užmokestis siūlomas atsižvelgiant į darbo patirtį ir kompetenciją. Prie darbo užmokesčio gali būti mokama kintamoji atlygio dalis.

Apie mus:

Norėtum prisidėti prie projektų, keičiančių elektros energijos gamybos ir vartojimo ateitį Baltijos šalyse ir už jų ribų? „Ignitis grupė“ – atsinaujinančiai energijai prioritetą teikianti integruota energijos paslaugų bendrovė, apjungianti daugiau nei 4300 skirtingų sričių ekspertų kasdienėje veikloje besivadovaujančių vertybėmis ir vienijamų bendro tikslo sukurti 100% žalią ir saugią energetikos ekosistemą dabarties ir ateities kartoms.

Vystome mažai anglies dioksido į aplinką išskiriantį gamybos portfelį, didžiausią dėmesį sutelkdami į jūrinio vėjo elektrinių, sausumos vėjo hibridinių elektrinių, elektros kaupimo, konvertavimo (angl. Power-to-X) bei saugojimo technologijas. Pasitelkdami integruoto verslo modelio privalumus, įgaliname žaliosios ir lanksčiosios gamybos plėtrą ir planuojame pasiekti 4–5 GW instaliuotos galios iki 2030 m.

www.ignitisgrupe.lt

Title: Commercial Data Analyst
Description:
Epic is one of the largest telecommunications companies in Cyprus and a member of a dynamic European family of telecommunications companies. With ability, innovation and agility at our core, we are offering a state-of-the-art network recognized by multiple awards in Cyprus.




At Epic we take care of our people and we enhance a people centric and learning culture for your professional growth. We are always looking for ambitious employees to be a part of our award-winning organisation!

Our team is growing so join us to take your career to the next level and become a part of our team of more than 350 professionals.




The role:

As a Commercial Data Analyst, you'll turn data into actionable insights that shape commercial and marketing decisions. You'll work closely with cross functional teams to analyze customer behavior, identify opportunities, and deliver targeted marketing campaigns that drive customer engagement, increase value, and improve retention. Your insights will directly influence commercial strategies and contribute to the success of the business.




Your Responsibilities:

Analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights that inform business decisions
Development, implementation and maintenance of weekly/monthly KPI’s for commercial segments stakeholders (Postpaid, Prepaid, Fixed).
Preparation of data & analysis of all marketing and sales campaigns: Cross Sell, Up Sell, Churn Prevention
Collaborate with broader Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Operations teams to develop and implement strategic initiatives that drive revenue/LTV growth and market expansion
Identify and implement process improvements to enhance data accuracy, reporting efficiency, and overall analytical capabilities.
Provide commercial insight on the month end results, trends and follow up actions. Clear and concise articulation of standard KPIs to segment stakeholders.
Acting as an internal consultant, using analytical skills to answer critical business questions.







Our proposition

Competitive remuneration package
Annual bonus scheme
13th salary
Attractive benefits with comprehensive medical and life insurances and provident fund
Company telecom products with free mobile and internet lines and exclusive discounts for our services with maximum monthly total value of 130 euros
Opportunity for a sustainable career and continuously building skills and expertise in a dynamic technology sector
Hybrid workplace model







Your Profile

2+ years of professional experience as an analyst/senior analyst, preferably in contract-based industry, market or consumer research with a particular focus on business data analysis, insights generation and planning
BSc in Computer Science (Data Science), Business Intelligence, Economics or related field
Postgraduate degree in any data science related field will be considered as strong advantage
Proficient SQL skills and data manipulation with Python (preferred) or R
Experience with any analytical/visualization tool (Power BI, Tableau)
Comfortable utilizing AI tools to improve efficiencies and integrate their usage in daily work
Understanding of data warehouses and ETL principles
Experience with an ETL platform such as Airflow, Informatica etc.
Willingness to learn new skills on the job
Fluent in English, with strong verbal and written communication skills




Interested to join a team of passionate professionals? Apply now! Send your CV to career@epic.com.cy stating the job title in the subject line.

Title: Data Steward
Description:
Join our team as a Data Steward!

Yes, you can! That's our attitude towards our work, ambitions, ideas, and relationships. We at Luminor believe that anyone has the potential to achieve big things and encourage everyone to achieve them, professionally and personally.

Whoever you are and whatever your role is, with Luminor, you will get a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to build a local banking champion.

Data Stewardship team works on pan-Baltic level and is important part of Data Management initiatives at Luminor. We strive to improve, standardize and align data across all three Baltic states. Traveling in the Baltics is expected.

How You Will Make an Impact:



Develop and maintain Data Catalog activities, thus assuring that Data Glossary and Data Dictionary items are up to date, not duplicated, stitched to technical metadata and data ownership is assigned correctly etc.
Develop and maintain Data Quality activities, i.e., perform Root Cause Analysis (RCA), initiate data quality assessments, data quality improvement projects, create DQ rules, run data quality monitoring, collaborate with other teams across Luminor to fix identified data discrepancies etc.
Develop Data Lineage activities, i.e. provide required input to the Data Lineage, verify data flows, use the Data Lineage to make RCA, cooperate with Information Technology (IT) units to assure that all technical metadata are up to date etc.
Be responsible for Data Domain, Data as Product or Data Solution / Application
Serve as first point of contact in case of any question related to your area of expertise coming both form Business or IT
Participate in IT and business projects, Value Streams, ensuring data requirements are clearly defined, data quality standards are upheld, and data governance principles are embedded throughout the project lifecycle
Support business area and Business Analyst in analyzing incoming data requirements
Implement Data Management related processes, standards and procedures



What Makes You a Great Fit:



Educational background in Economics, Business Management, Finance, IT or any related field
Proven work experience as Data Steward, Business Analyst, Reporting Analyst, IT System Analyst or similar
At least 3 years’ professional experience in the area of data governance and data management
At least 1 year experience with banking or other finance industry, good knowledge of the Bank products, processes and IT systems
Very Good SQL knowledge, ability to work with large data sets to find quality issues, detect data structures etc.
Very good understanding of data warehousing and reporting concepts and ability to apply and use in practice data profiling techniques
Very good understanding of IT database structures/principles
Very good knowledge of MS Office and Project management tools (e.g., Atlassian JIRA, Confluence)
Ability to be a “bridge” between IT and business
Problem solver mindset and attention to detail-oriented personality in combination of possessing excellent communication skills, both written and oral
Initiative and high-level of responsibility
Experience in working with DWH, Tableau/PowerBI, Cloud Data Warehouse e.g. Snowflake, any ETL or Data Governance tool / solution will be considered as strong advantage
Data Management or Data Stewardship certification would be considered as an advantage e.g. DAMA
Fluent English is required in spoken and written communication due to working on Pan-Baltic level



Why You Will Love Working Here:



Gross salary. 2,750-4,000 EUR/month, which is to be determined depending on your level of experience and competencies
Flexibility. Flexible working hours, Hybrid work, and the possibility to work from anywhere in the EU, Iceland, Switzerland, and the UK (in total 90 days per year)
International teams. Teams that go outside Pan-Baltic borders, where people value challenging work together with good humor and having fun
Culture. You’ll be joining a team where things move fast, ideas flow freely, and change is part of the everyday. It’s a dynamic environment that keeps you learning, growing, and never bored
More vacation. Additional weeks of vacation are available to all employees who have been in the company for 1 year or more
Volunteer time off. We care about giving back to society, therefore, you will get additional days off for volunteering purposes
Paid leave. We are proud of our employees who are participating in military training. Therefore, Luminor offers 30 fully paid calendar days for military training every year
Health benefits. A competitive benefits package in addition to your salary that includes health insurance after the first 3 months pass in all three Baltic states, as well as Health days in case of your absence due to sickness without a doctor's note needed
Wellbeing. Access to tools and resources that help you feel good and be productive at work and in life
Professional growth. Internal and external training programs, LinkedIn learning, workshops, conferences, online training, etc
Special Offer for Luminor products & services. Enjoy special offers & pricing for products and services provided by Luminor



The application deadline is the 31st of July. We are looking for a candidate primarily already located within the Baltics. Please apply with your CV in English and PDF format!

Luminor is the leading independent bank in the Baltics and the third-largest provider of financial services in our region. We serve the financial needs of individuals, families, and companies. Just like our home markets of Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania we are young, dynamic, and forward looking.

Luminor has a strong and capable team that is committed to achieving our strategic objectives. We are dedicated to supporting sustainable growth in the Baltic region by providing a long-term commitment to businesses and individuals.

Title: People Data & Automation Specialist
Description:
Omniva connects people, businesses, and communities across the Baltics and beyond. We’ve grown from Estonia’s trusted postal service into a modern, technology-driven logistics company supporting the growth of e-commerce and global trade.

Every day, we help our customers send, receive, and grow — with care, precision, and reliability. Our people are at the heart of everything we do: caring, open, and driven to deliver experiences that feel personal and effortless.

At Omniva, every delivery carries possibility — and every team member helps make that happen.

About The Role

We’re looking for a People Data & Automation Specialist to join our Baltic People & Culture team and help shape how data and technology drive our people’s decisions.

This is a high-impact, future-facing role at the intersection of people analytics, automation, and digital enablement. You will build the foundations of reliable People data, transform it into actionable insights, and design smart, scalable solutions that reduce manual work and improve how our People team operates. You’ll play a key role in moving us from reactive reporting to proactive, data-driven decision-making, while embedding automation and AI into everyday People processes.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building, simplifying, and improving systems, and who wants to make People data genuinely useful and operationally impactful.

What You’ll Do


Build and maintain a reliable People data ecosystem across the Baltics; define key metrics and standardize reporting
Create and manage dashboards (e.g. Power BI) and deliver actionable insights to support business decisions
Implement low-code/no-code, AI, and automation solutions to reduce manual work and improve accuracy
Optimize and automate employee lifecycle processes (hiring, onboarding, exits, reporting)
Develop simple internal tools and scalable solutions to improve team efficiency
Collaborate with leadership, People Partners, and engineering teams while continuously replacing manual tasks with smarter ways of working


What You Bring


Experience in People/HR Analytics or data analysis, with understanding of HR data and employee lifecycle processes
Strong Excel skills (data structuring, validation, modelling)
Hands-on experience with Power BI or similar tools
Experience with automation, workflows, or process optimisation; familiarity with HR systems (HRIS, ATS, payroll) is a plus
Interest in or experience with low-code/no-code tools, scripting, or AI-assisted development
Comfortable designing and building solutions from scratch


Mindset & Ways of Working


Ownership: You take responsibility and follow through
Self-direction: You manage priorities independently and act proactively
Continuous improvement: You constantly look for better, simpler ways of working
Clarity in communication: You turn complex data into understandable insights
Collaboration: You build strong relationships and work effectively across teams
Reliability: You deliver consistently and can be trusted to get things done
Curiosity: You stay open to learning and exploring new tools and approaches
Adaptability: You’re comfortable in evolving environments where not everything is predefined


Why you’ll love working here

At Omniva, we live our values every day.

We care — about our people, our customers, and the world we connect.

We are open — to new ideas, innovation, and learning from each other.

We are trustworthy — we do what we promise and take pride in getting it right.

And we enjoy our work — celebrating progress, teamwork, and shared achievements.

You’ll also benefit from:


A meaningful role in a growing, technology-driven logistics company
Hybrid work model with office location Pallasti 28, Tallinn with office and flexible environment
Health insurance or sports compensation
Five additional vacation days after two years
Opportunities for personal and professional growth


Compensation & Growth

For this position, the gross monthly salary range is EUR 3000 – 3800 gross, depending on your experience and qualifications.

At Omniva, we believe in fair and transparent compensation that reflects your contribution and supports your growth.

✉️ How To Apply

Ready to make your mark?

Apply now and help shape the future of logistics and cross-border commerce with Omniva.

We are only considering candidates currently located in Estonia.


Please note: Applications must be submitted in English, which is Omniva’s global working language.

Title: Technical AIP Data Analyst
Description:
Founded in 2011, Altenar Technology Limited is a leading igaming software provider inspired by the vision of our customers. As our platform has scaled we have begun to generate high volumes of transactional data which can be potentially mined to generate additional insights. The Altenar Intelligence Platform (AIP) is the long term vision for unlocking this potential.




We are seeking a Technical AIP Data Analyst to join the Technology team to work closely with various Product and Business stakeholders to focus on using data pipelines and various analytical techniques to analyse business procedures. The role requires outstanding communication skills, proven ability to build and manage medium-scale modelling projects, define scope of existing infrastructure, identify data requirements, build methodology and tools that are well researched, undertake feasibility studies, and support with documentation. The candidate will be expected to demonstrate familiarity with concepts such as ETL, machine learning and statistics.




The successful applicant should illustrate comfort working in a startup environment, possessing high intellectual curiosity with ability to quickly learn new concepts/frameworks, algorithms and technology that powers Alternar business objectives. We seek someone with a high degree of ownership that can effectively examine flows of data, researching and evaluating to consistently deliver superior quality results on-time. The successful candidate should be able to rapidly prototype analytical models with the goal of generating strategic business insights as per the needs of the AIP roadmap.




Finally, the applicant is expected to display a high degree of independence working across multiple programs and initiatives to un-earth opportunities. The role demands strong data organisation, data structure, and data hierarchy knowledge, and may be suited to incumbents with extensive Data Analysis and/or computing programming experience.




The activities of a Technical AIP Data Analyst include but are not limited to:




Continuously updating core knowledge and skills in SQL and Python or R as applied to mathematical analysis
Liaises with internal stakeholders including the Solution Architect and Business Analysts, and directly with key Product and Business owners on topics for exploration and their description
Liaises with internal stakeholders including Data Engineers, Infrastructure Engineers and Data Services Engineers on data collection, movement and storage strategies
Analyse and Build analytical applications in a structured manner that can be tested and deployed based on stakeholder requirements
Undertake feasibility studies and various types of analytics including descriptive, diagnostic, predictive or prescriptive analytics.
Communicate the impact of technologies by delivering views, dashboards and report configurations using BI tools as required by internal stakeholders
Supporting software architecture processes with data handling and visualisation specifications
Participation in the Architecture Review process (including by providing advice and assistance) to ensure new initiatives or major changes to the Altenar product portfolio have data flow considerations represented at the inception phase
Examine existing business models and flows of data and design functional specifications by participating in the research, test, design of new data services tools for online or warehoused data
Work in a fast-paced, dynamic, multinational, multicultural environment
Creating and maintaining documentation for the solutions provided




Qualifications

Experience working with python and pandas
Strong SQL querying skills
Knowledge of noSQL and ETL operations considered a plus
Strong analytical and forecasting skills using offline methods such as spreadsheets
Strong math and statistics background (relevant Degree education required)
Proven experience with one or more BI tools (Metabase, Apache Superset, Redash, PowerBI, Tableau, Qlikview)
Ability to quickly write ad-hoc scripts to analyze small to medium datasets.
Strong research and validation skills
Motivated and self-driven to work in an environment with minimal supervision
Strong presentation skills
Proficient in spoken and written English
Experience in Technical Data Analytics, Data Science (Computing), and/or Product Management roles
Graduate-level education in Computer Science, Engineering, Math, Statistics, Economics, or other relevant field




Preferred Experience

Experience with Spark core functionality such as Spark SQL, Spark Structured Streaming is very desirable
Some experience in using Git version control in the context of documentation/source code management
Knowledge of Linux CLI tools to perform "quick and dirty" command line analysis to serve as first exploratory review of data
Some knowledge of MLaaS such as Amazon Machine Learning services, Azure Machine Learning, Google AI Platform, and IBM Watson Machine Learning
Experience in building, testing and validating machine learning algorithms
Prior direct or indirect involvement in data warehouse design, tool selection and support
Experience with Data Lab concepts with Hue, Zeppelin, Jupyterhub
Experience with Hadoop/HDFS - CLI, HDFS access from scripts
Knowledge of C# or Java is considered an asset

The successful candidate should be able to rapidly prototype analytical models with the goal of generating strategic business insights as per the needs of the AIP roadmap.

Finally, the applicant is expected to display a high degree of independence working across multiple programs and initiatives to un-earth opportunities. The role demands strong data organisation, data structure, and data hierarchy knowledge, and may be suited to incumbents with extensive Data Analysis and/or computing programming experience.

The activities of a Technical AIP Data Analyst include but are not limited to:

  • Continuously updating core knowledge and skills in SQL and Python or R as applied to mathematical analysis
  • Liaises with internal stakeholders including the Solution Architect and Business Analysts, and directly with key Product and Business owners on topics for exploration and their description
  • Liaises with internal stakeholders including Data Engineers, Infrastructure Engineers and Data Services Engineers on data collection, movement and storage strategies
  • Analyse and Build analytical applications in a structured manner that can be tested and deployed based on stakeholder requirements
  • Undertake feasibility studies and various types of analytics including descriptive, diagnostic, predictive or prescriptive analytics.
  • Communicate the impact of technologies by delivering views, dashboards and report configurations using BI tools as required by internal stakeholders
  • Supporting software architecture processes with data handling and visualisation specifications
  • Participation in the Architecture Review process (including by providing advice and assistance) to ensure new initiatives or major changes to the Altenar product portfolio have data flow considerations represented at the inception phase
  • Examine existing business models and flows of data and design functional specifications by participating in the research, test, design of new data services tools for online or warehoused data
  • Work in a fast-paced, dynamic, multinational, multicultural environment
  • Creating and maintaining documentation for the solutions provided

Qualifications

  • Experience working with python and pandas
  • Strong SQL querying skills
  • Knowledge of noSQL and ETL operations considered a plus
  • Strong analytical and forecasting skills using offline methods such as spreadsheets
  • Strong math and statistics background (relevant Degree education required)
  • Proven experience with one or more BI tools (Metabase, Apache Superset, Redash, PowerBI, Tableau, Qlikview)
  • Ability to quickly write ad-hoc scripts to analyze small to medium datasets.
  • Strong research and validation skills
  • Motivated and self-driven to work in an environment with minimal supervision
  • Strong presentation skills
  • Proficient in spoken and written English
  • Experience in Technical Data Analytics, Data Science (Computing), and/or Product Management roles
  • Graduate-level education in Computer Science, Engineering, Math, Statistics, Economics, or other relevant field

Preferred Experience

  • Experience with Spark core functionality such as Spark SQL, Spark Structured Streaming is very desirable
  • Some experience in using Git version control in the context of documentation/source code management
  • Knowledge of Linux CLI tools to perform "quick and dirty" command line analysis to serve as first exploratory review of data
  • Some knowledge of MLaaS such as Amazon Machine Learning services, Azure Machine Learning, Google AI Platform, and IBM Watson Machine Learning
  • Experience in building, testing and validating machine learning algorithms
  • Prior direct or indirect involvement in data warehouse design, tool selection and support
  • Experience with Data Lab concepts with Hue, Zeppelin, Jupyterhub
  • Experience with Hadoop/HDFS - CLI, HDFS access from scripts
  • Knowledge of C# or Java is considered an asset

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
BettingJobs is working with a very well established land-based resorts company who also operate a successful online casino brand. They are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join their growing team in Malta.







About The Role




The Data Analyst is accountable for:




Deliver high-quality reporting and insights across online gaming products and platforms in regulated markets
Design and implement automated reporting, dashboards, and scalable data solutions to improve efficiency and reliability
Analyse complex datasets to identify trends, risks, and opportunities that inform product, commercial, and operational decisions
Monitor and improve data quality, working across internal teams and external vendors to ensure accuracy, integrity, and compliance







About You




You are a commercially minded and detail‑driven Data Analyst with strong experience working in complex, data‑rich digital environments. You thrive on turning large, messy datasets into clear, actionable insights and take pride in building reliable, scalable reporting solutions.




You are a self‑starter who enjoys working across systems, stakeholders, and vendors, and you bring a high level of rigour to data accuracy, documentation, and governance. You are confident engaging with both technical and non‑technical audiences, and you’re comfortable operating in regulated industries where compliance and precision are critical.




You bring proven experience in building dashboards, automating reporting, and analysing trends to support business decisions, ideally within online gaming or a similar regulated digital environment.




What’s in it for you?




Work on high impact digital initiatives shaping the company’s online future
A supportive, flexible, and inclusive workplace where you can thrive
Opportunities for development and career growth
Great company benefits

Title: Digital Analyst - Product
Description:
Patrianna is a fast-scaling product development company headquartered in Gibraltar, with a dynamic, global team powering our growth. We operate at the intersection of technology and entertainment, building innovative solutions that shape the future of social gaming and deliver outstanding experiences to millions of players worldwide.

We're driven by speed, ambition, and bold ideas. At our core, we're product creators and problem-solvers who thrive in a high-performance environment. We're looking for exceptional talent—smart, adaptable, and motivated individuals eager to make an impact, scale business functions at pace, and continuously improve.

The Role

Transform data into actionable insights that drive product decisions and enhance player experiences across our gaming platforms.

What You Will Be Doing


Guide product team members through complex data landscapes, providing valuable insights that support critical decision-making
Conduct deep-dive analyses of user behavior, app performance, and product features to boost player acquisition, engagement, and retention
Ensure top-tier behavioral data collection by collaborating with analytics developers on tracking implementation and issue resolution
Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and alerting systems for real-time monitoring of key conversion metrics
Present insights clearly and effectively to stakeholders across the organization
Debug data issues and optimize tracking mechanisms to maintain data integrity


What We Are Looking For


Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail
Critical thinking mindset with proactive approach and high reliability
Solid SQL skills for querying and manipulating databases
Experience creating detailed reports and dashboards using tools like Looker or Tableau
Familiarity with digital analytics platforms such as Snowplow and GA4, plus tracking mechanisms and data layers
Experience with data modeling in Dataform and session replay/autotracking platforms is a bonus


Why you will love it

You'll be at the heart of product innovation, where your insights directly shape gaming experiences for millions of players. In our high-growth environment, you'll have the freedom to take ownership of impactful projects while collaborating with brilliant minds who value your expertise. Every analysis you deliver will drive real business decisions and help us stay ahead in the competitive gaming landscape.

Equal Opportunities

We hire based on skills, drive, and ideas—nothing else. Patrianna is a fast-scaling product development company headquartered in Gibraltar, with a dynamic, global team powering our growth. We operate at the intersection of technology and entertainment, building innovative solutions that shape the future of social gaming and deliver outstanding experiences to millions of players worldwide.

We're driven by speed, ambition, and bold ideas. At our core, we're product creators and problem-solvers who thrive in a high-performance environment. We're looking for exceptional talent—smart, adaptable, and motivated individuals eager to make an impact, scale business functions at pace, and continuously improve.

The Role

Transform data into actionable insights that drive product decisions and enhance player experiences across our gaming platforms.

What You Will Be Doing

  • Guide product team members through complex data landscapes, providing valuable insights that support critical decision-making
  • Conduct deep-dive analyses of user behavior, app performance, and product features to boost player acquisition, engagement, and retention
  • Ensure top-tier behavioral data collection by collaborating with analytics developers on tracking implementation and issue resolution
  • Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and alerting systems for real-time monitoring of key conversion metrics
  • Present insights clearly and effectively to stakeholders across the organization
  • Debug data issues and optimize tracking mechanisms to maintain data integrity

What We Are Looking For

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail
  • Critical thinking mindset with proactive approach and high reliability
  • Solid SQL skills for querying and manipulating databases
  • Experience creating detailed reports and dashboards using tools like Looker or Tableau
  • Familiarity with digital analytics platforms such as Snowplow and GA4, plus tracking mechanisms and data layers
  • Experience with data modeling in Dataform and session replay/autotracking platforms is a bonus

Why you will love it

You'll be at the heart of product innovation, where your insights directly shape gaming experiences for millions of players. In our high-growth environment, you'll have the freedom to take ownership of impactful projects while collaborating with brilliant minds who value your expertise. Every analysis you deliver will drive real business decisions and help us stay ahead in the competitive gaming landscape.

Equal Opportunities

We hire based on skills, drive, and ideas—nothing else. Your background, gender, age, race, ethnicity, disability, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, or educational path will never be a barrier to joining us. We also welcome candidates from non-traditional career journeys and value diverse perspectives that challenge conventional thinking. Diversity fuels our innovation, collaboration, and growth, and we're committed to creating an environment where everyone can contribute their best work and thrive.

Key operational KPIs.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Are you ready to join our digital revolution journey?

At Aleph , we’re not just part of the digital advertising landscape—we’re shaping its future. Representing the world’s leading platforms, including TikTok, Amazon, Google, and nearly 55 others, we operate in 130+ markets across new and existing geographies.

Our mission is to empower advertisers and brands to unlock the full potential of these platforms' advertising capabilities. By fostering long-lasting partnerships, we create limitless opportunities for people and businesses to advertise effectively at both local and global levels.

With a presence spanning continents, Aleph offers you the chance to be part of a fast-growing, innovative team where your work makes a direct impact. If you’re ambitious, forward-thinking, and eager to thrive in a dynamic, global environment, Aleph is the perfect place to build your career.

Role Overview

We are looking for a highly analytical Data Analyst to join our Business Intelligence team. You will act as a critical "backend engine," building the data foundations and analytical models that enable data-driven decisions for both our supply (platforms) and demand (sales) sides.

You will dive deep into complex datasets, build automated reporting pipelines, and uncover actionable insights that drive our strategy with global platforms like TikTok, Microsoft, Google, X, Spotify, and others.

What You 'll Do



Extract and analyze complex data.
Query, clean, and transform large datasets from multiple sources to uncover trends, performance metrics, and market insights that support strategic decision-making across the organization.
Build and maintain BI dashboards.
Design, develop, and maintain automated dashboards and reports using Looker Studio to provide real-time visibility into business performance for executive and partner discussions. Support strategic projects.
Conduct deep-dive analyses for platform RFP responses, Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs), and strategic initiatives that shape Aleph’s partnerships and revenue strategy. Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders.
Work closely with strategists, sales teams, and regional leadership to define data requirements, understand their business challenges, and deliver quantitative solutions.
Translate data into actionable recommendations.
Identify performance anomalies, revenue opportunities, and market risks by packaging raw data into structured, easy-to-consume analytical models and insights.
Ensure data integrity and governance.
Establish clear data validation frameworks, optimize queries, and maintain high standards for data accuracy and documentation internally.



Who You Are



Highly analytical and problem-solving oriented.
You have a strong quantitative background and enjoy tackling complex business questions through rigorous data analysis.
Technically proficient in data tools.
You possess strong SQL skills for data extraction and manipulation, and you are highly proficient in at least one major BI visualization tool (e.g., Power BI, Looker Studio). Advanced Excel/Google Sheets skills are a must; experience with Python a strong plus. Business-minded.You don’t just pull numbers; you understand the business context behind the data and can connect your analysis directly to revenue growth, efficiency, and key opportunities.
A clear communicator. You can translate complex technical findings and data models into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders and leadership
Structured and detail-oriented. You are meticulous about data accuracy, organize your code and queries logically, and ensure all deliverables meet a high-quality standard before they reach stakeholders.
Interested in digital media or advertising.
Experience in digital advertising analytics, media agencies, ad-tech, or similar environments is a strong plus, though not strictly required.



What You'll Love About Us



We care about your individuality by giving you freedom to grow and create within the company, regardless of your position.
Work in a dynamic team in a fast-paced industry of an international company with multi-cultural teams around the world .
Learn from the best: Our state of the art workshops guarantee the latest insights into digital advertising.
In line with the EU Pay Transparency Directive, we are committed to fair and open compensation practices. For this role, the minimum gross annual base salary is 35,000 EUR.



In addition to base salary, this role includes a variable component, paid annually. The final offer will reflect your experience, skills, and the scope of the role.

#ALEPH

Why Join Us?

Aleph is a place of many cultures, perspectives, and talents. We support each other, creating an environment of giving and receiving. We value partnership and communication because we believe it takes a group of people to achieve great things. We are energized by our ever-changing industry, our curiosity keeps us learning and seeking out new opportunities.

We stay flexible and adaptable, and believe in moving with the speed of change. We encourage everyone to strive for more, providing our internal talent with growth opportunities and the ability to learn together.

#ALEPH

We use Lever, our recruitment management platform, which includes AI-powered features that may assist our hiring team in reviewing applications, analyzing CVs, and identifying relevant qualifications. These tools support - but do not replace - human judgement. All hiring decisions are made by people. For more information about how your personal data is processed, including your rights in relation to AI-assisted processing, please see our Candidate Privacy Policy or contact us at [email protected] .

Title: Marketing Analyst
Description:
We’re looking for a highly analytical and commercially minded Marketing Analyst to join our BI & Analytics team. In this role, you’ll be a key partner to the Marketing Department, working closely with the CMO and members of the Acquisition team to transform raw data into strategic intelligence that drives business performance. This is not a surface-level reporting role. You’ll dive deep into marketing performance, iGaming product performance, and player behaviour to uncover the “why” behind the numbers. Your insights and recommendations will directly influence how we optimise marketing investment, improve player engagement, and grow market share.




What are we looking for now?

A highly analytical and commercially minded Marketing Analyst to join our BI & Analytics team. In this role, you will be a crucial partner to the Marketing Department, collaborating closely with the CMO and members of the Acquisition team, transforming raw data into the strategic intelligence that drives our business forward.
You will move beyond surface-level reporting to conduct deep, methodologically sound analyses of our marketing campaigns, iGaming products, and player behaviour.
Your primary mission is to challenge assumptions, uncover the "why" behind the numbers, and provide clear, actionable recommendations that optimise our marketing investments, enhance player engagement, and grow our market share.
This is a high-impact role for a data-driven storyteller who is passionate about the iGaming industry and thrives on turning complex questions into profitable business decisions.




What You’ll do:

Design, execute, and deliver post-mortems for marketing and acquisition campaigns, going beyond top-line results to measure true incremental lift, ROI, and profitability, accounting for confounding variables and establishing clear attribution.
Apply a rigorous analytical framework to evaluate the performance of marketing activities, measuring the impact of these "tests" using statistical principles to assess effectiveness and inform future strategy.
Analyse player behaviour by segmenting the user base across various dimensions (e.g., New vs. Existing, Player Value Tiers, Game/Provider Preference) to better understand player loyalty and lifetime value.
Translate complex findings into compelling narratives and actionable recommendations for stakeholders in Marketing and Leadership; these insights will directly influence decisions on marketing spend, product strategy, and promotional calendars.
Develop and maintain powerful, intuitive dashboards and reports in Looker to provide stakeholders with self-serve access to performance metrics.




What You'll Bring:

You will have proven previous experience working as a business data analyst in the domain of online acquisition marketing or a closely related field. Online casino or iGaming industry experience is welcome, but not strictly required.
You hold a bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Economics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Marketing or related field.
You have a strong analytical mindset, backed up by relevant skills for the ability to collect, organise, analyse, and disseminate significant amounts of information with attention to detail and accuracy.
You are comfortable working with SQL and writing complex queries to retrieve, combine, transform and model data from different sources.
You have hands-on experience in developing reports and dashboards in data visualisation tools such as Looker, Power BI or similar. Looker and LookML experience is preferred, but not essential.




What We Offer: 

Competitive salary
Health Insurance & Fitness Benefit
Free parking
Weekly team meals
Team-building events and parties
Learning & Development opportunities
A great working environment

Title: Commercial Analytics Specialist
Description:
KM Malta Airlines Limited, the national airline of the Maltese Islands, is seeking to recruit a Commercial Analytics Specialist within the Commercial department.




Purpose of Position




The Commercial Analytics Specialist is a key conduit of KPI management and all commercial analytics development that relies on utilising and interpreting data to make effective, reliable business decisions based on accurate information.
Accountable for the setting and management of all metrics for good control of revenue performance and strategic compliance across commercial.
The role is to act as a hub-and-spoke model that delivers analytics and algorithms that deliver competitive advantage.




Responsibilities




Develop all aspects of corporate strategy, data, information and analytics strategy.
Actively contribute to the development of the overall commercial strategy and business plan for the airline.
Take responsibility for construct and distribution of revenue budget targets by partnering with Network Sales and Revenue Management.
Own, create, and develop all technical aspects related to data tracking, delivery, and reporting.
Ensure that regulatory compliance and data quality are maintained throughout the design and management of data and information systems, set standards to manage both physical and logical data.
Create, document and present technical designs, technical solutions to relevant stakeholders.
Ensure communication of sales actions and results within the team and to stakeholders in Sales, Revenue Management, Network Planning, Distribution, and Finance.
Develop and foster relationships with reporting and analytics communities across the organisation identifying opportunities to share best practice and develop collaborative strategies that deliver group benefits.




Experience, Qualifications and Skills




Extensive background in data mining and / or statistical analysis.
Able to understand various data structures and common methods in data transformation.
Experience with programming languages, such as Java / Saas / Python / SQL.
Familiarity in Revenue Forecasting, Revenue & Route Planning, Commercial Data Architectures, Airline Data Warehouse, Revenue Accounting, NDC, One Order.
Experience in the airline industry is desired.
Degree MQF Level 6 or higher or other professional qualification in Computer Science, Statistics, Applied Math / Data Science or related field (essential).
Computing & Analytics: Requires a basic knowledge on Cloud platforms, SQL & NoSQL, Tableau, PowerBI, MicroStrategy, Project Management, Business Analysis, Agile, Data Modeling & Warehousing, Data Mining.
Strong analytical skills, problem-solving and decision-making capability.




Jobsplus Permit: 283/2024




Closing Date: 02nd August 2026

Title: Technical AIP Data Analyst
Description:
Founded in 2011, Altenar Technology Limited is a leading igaming software provider inspired by the vision of our customers. As our platform has scaled we have begun to generate high volumes of transactional data which can be potentially mined to generate additional insights. The Altenar Intelligence Platform (AIP) is the long term vision for unlocking this potential.




We are seeking a Technical AIP Data Analyst to join the Technology team to work closely with various Product and Business stakeholders to focus on using data pipelines and various analytical techniques to analyse business procedures. The role requires outstanding communication skills, proven ability to build and manage medium-scale modelling projects, define scope of existing infrastructure, identify data requirements, build methodology and tools that are well researched, undertake feasibility studies, and support with documentation. The candidate will be expected to demonstrate familiarity with concepts such as ETL, machine learning and statistics.




The successful applicant should illustrate comfort working in a startup environment, possessing high intellectual curiosity with ability to quickly learn new concepts/frameworks, algorithms and technology that powers Alternar business objectives. We seek someone with a high degree of ownership that can effectively examine flows of data, researching and evaluating to consistently deliver superior quality results on-time. The successful candidate should be able to rapidly prototype analytical models with the goal of generating strategic business insights as per the needs of the AIP roadmap.




Finally, the applicant is expected to display a high degree of independence working across multiple programs and initiatives to un-earth opportunities. The role demands strong data organisation, data structure, and data hierarchy knowledge, and may be suited to incumbents with extensive Data Analysis and/or computing programming experience.




The activities of a Technical AIP Data Analyst include but are not limited to:




Continuously updating core knowledge and skills in SQL and Python or R as applied to mathematical analysis
Liaises with internal stakeholders including the Solution Architect and Business Analysts, and directly with key Product and Business owners on topics for exploration and their description
Liaises with internal stakeholders including Data Engineers, Infrastructure Engineers and Data Services Engineers on data collection, movement and storage strategies
Analyse and Build analytical applications in a structured manner that can be tested and deployed based on stakeholder requirements
Undertake feasibility studies and various types of analytics including descriptive, diagnostic, predictive or prescriptive analytics.
Communicate the impact of technologies by delivering views, dashboards and report configurations using BI tools as required by internal stakeholders
Supporting software architecture processes with data handling and visualisation specifications
Participation in the Architecture Review process (including by providing advice and assistance) to ensure new initiatives or major changes to the Altenar product portfolio have data flow considerations represented at the inception phase
Examine existing business models and flows of data and design functional specifications by participating in the research, test, design of new data services tools for online or warehoused data
Work in a fast-paced, dynamic, multinational, multicultural environment
Creating and maintaining documentation for the solutions provided




Qualifications

Experience working with python and pandas
Strong SQL querying skills
Knowledge of noSQL and ETL operations considered a plus
Strong analytical and forecasting skills using offline methods such as spreadsheets
Strong math and statistics background (relevant Degree education required)
Proven experience with one or more BI tools (Metabase, Apache Superset, Redash, PowerBI, Tableau, Qlikview)
Ability to quickly write ad-hoc scripts to analyze small to medium datasets.
Strong research and validation skills
Motivated and self-driven to work in an environment with minimal supervision
Strong presentation skills
Proficient in spoken and written English
Experience in Technical Data Analytics, Data Science (Computing), and/or Product Management roles
Graduate-level education in Computer Science, Engineering, Math, Statistics, Economics, or other relevant field




Preferred Experience

Experience with Spark core functionality such as Spark SQL, Spark Structured Streaming is very desirable
Some experience in using Git version control in the context of documentation/source code management
Knowledge of Linux CLI tools to perform "quick and dirty" command line analysis to serve as first exploratory review of data
Some knowledge of MLaaS such as Amazon Machine Learning services, Azure Machine Learning, Google AI Platform, and IBM Watson Machine Learning
Experience in building, testing and validating machine learning algorithms
Prior direct or indirect involvement in data warehouse design, tool selection and support
Experience with Data Lab concepts with Hue, Zeppelin, Jupyterhub
Experience with Hadoop/HDFS - CLI, HDFS access from scripts
Knowledge of C# or Java is considered an asset

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Casumo is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Join us at Casumo, where you are invited to be your authentic YOU-MO!

Company Overview

Welcome to Casumo, your passport to a world of fun, excitement, and responsible gaming. We're an international online casino company with a knack for creating unforgettable gaming experiences. Our secret sauce? A blend of innovation, security, and a dash of playful charm.

Nowadays, we're on the hunt for an analytical and collaborative Business Intelligence Analyst!

Position Overview

The Business Intelligence Analyst is responsible for developing, maintaining, and improving reporting and analytics solutions that support business decision making across the organization. The role works closely with Business Intelligence Analyst Lead, Senior Business Analysts, Data Engineers and business stakeholders to deliver accurate, scalable, and user-friendly reports. The BI Analyst translates defined business requirements into dashboards, reports and analyses.

Responsibilities


Design, develop, and maintain dashboards, reports, and self-service reporting solutions primarily using Tableau.
Develop and maintain SQL queries, reporting views, calculations, and supporting reporting logic.
Validate reporting accuracy and ensure KPI logic is implemented correctly.
Apply dimensional modelling and reporting best practices in analytics solutions
Ensure dashboards and reports are visually clear, user-friendly, and aligned with the best reporting practices.
Support the maintenance and enhancement of existing reporting solutions.
Perform ad-hoc analyses to support operational and tactical business decision-making.
Investigate data discrepancies, incidents, and reporting issues.
Analyze datasets to identify trends, anomalies, and actionable insights.
Collaborate with Business Analyst Lead and Senior Business Analysts to deliver reporting and analytics solutions based on defined business requirements.
Communicate with stakeholders regarding reporting functionality, enhancements, and issue resolution.
Provide support and guidance to end-users on reporting usage and available data sources.
Maintain documentation for reports, dashboards, and reporting logic
Identify opportunities to automate and improve recurring reporting processes.


Requirements


A bachelor’s degree in business administration, finance, computer science, data science, economics, statistics, or a related field.
2+ years of experience in data analysis, business analysis, or a related field.
Good knowledge of the iGaming industry including key operational business processes, metrics, and key performance indicators (KPIs).
Strong SQL skills including joins, aggregations, subqueries, and reporting logic development.
Experience developing dashboards and reports using Tableau.
Good understanding of data warehousing and dimensional modelling concepts.
Familiarity with ETL concepts and reporting-oriented data structures.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to interpret data and provide actionable insights.
Good understanding of business KPIs and reporting requirements.
Strong communication skills with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Experience working in SCRUM or agile environments is considered a plus.


Think we're a good match? Apply now!

The Perks

Malta Office

Being a part of the Casumo group provides an unparalleled experience. You’ll find yourself surrounded by the brightest minds within the most inspiring and collaborative office spaces! In addition to that, you’ll enjoy:


Private health insurance
Wellness incentives, including a fitness allowance and mental well-being services
Flexible national holidays: public holidays mean more time off, choose how and when to enjoy them!
2 weeks Work From Anywhere (10 days), increased to 4 weeks (20 days) after longer duration of employment within the Company: explore the world while working remotely
Gourmet lunches and healthy snacks prepared by our in-house chef
Variety of discounts from local vendors
Access to some of the greatest tools and platforms for developing your professional skills and building success within your role
A range of training courses, known as Casumo College, for continuous learning and growth
Social events for building strong relationships with colleagues from all across the organisation


Macedonia Office


Private health insurance
Wellness incentives, including a fitness allowance and mental well-being services
2 weeks Work From Anywhere (10 days), increased to 4 weeks (20 days) after longer duration of employment within the Company: explore the world while working remotely
Office lunches - three times per week
Access to some of the greatest tools and platforms for developing your professional skills and building success within your role
A range of training courses, known as Casumo College, for continuous learning and growth
Social events for building strong relationships with colleagues from all across the organisation


Zagreb Office


Private health insurance
Wellness incentives, including a fitness allowance and mental well-being services
2 weeks Work From Anywhere (10 days), increased to 4 weeks (20 days) after longer duration of employment within the Company: explore the world while working remotely
Food allowance
Access to some of the greatest tools and platforms for developing your professional skills and building success within your role
A range of training courses, known as Casumo College, for continuous learning and growth
Social events for building strong relationships with colleagues from all across the organisation


Our ABC Values

ASPIRE

At Casumo, "aspire" means pushing beyond the ordinary and transforming obstacles into stepping stones. Challenges are our breakfast of champions, and comfort zones are out of bounds. Mediocrity? Left behind. Our mantra? Dream big, aim high, and always be ready for the next adventure in innovation.

BELIEVE

Belief at Casumo isn't just a feel-good sticker; it's the glue that binds us. Turning "me" achievements into "we" victories, we're a tight-knit crew of dreamers, doers, and relentless supporters. With a high-five arsenal and a trusty cheerleading squad, we're on a mission to prove that together, we're not just strong; we're Casumo strong.

CARE

Care is our secret ingredient, the cherry on top of our game. It's not only about ensuring our players have a blast (responsibly, of course); it's about weaving a fabric of support so tight, even the toughest challenges can't tear us apart. From tailoring player experiences to being there for each other, we're all about creating memorable moments.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
BettingJobs is working with a very well established land-based resorts company who also operate a successful online casino brand. They are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join their growing team in Malta.







About The Role




The Data Analyst is accountable for:




Deliver high-quality reporting and insights across online gaming products and platforms in regulated markets
Design and implement automated reporting, dashboards, and scalable data solutions to improve efficiency and reliability
Analyse complex datasets to identify trends, risks, and opportunities that inform product, commercial, and operational decisions
Monitor and improve data quality, working across internal teams and external vendors to ensure accuracy, integrity, and compliance







About You




You are a commercially minded and detail‑driven Data Analyst with strong experience working in complex, data‑rich digital environments. You thrive on turning large, messy datasets into clear, actionable insights and take pride in building reliable, scalable reporting solutions.




You are a self‑starter who enjoys working across systems, stakeholders, and vendors, and you bring a high level of rigour to data accuracy, documentation, and governance. You are confident engaging with both technical and non‑technical audiences, and you’re comfortable operating in regulated industries where compliance and precision are critical.




You bring proven experience in building dashboards, automating reporting, and analysing trends to support business decisions, ideally within online gaming or a similar regulated digital environment.




What’s in it for you?




Work on high impact digital initiatives shaping the company’s online future
A supportive, flexible, and inclusive workplace where you can thrive
Opportunities for development and career growth
Great company benefits

Title: Digital Analyst - Product
Description:
Patrianna is a fast-scaling product development company headquartered in Gibraltar, with a dynamic, global team powering our growth. We operate at the intersection of technology and entertainment, building innovative solutions that shape the future of social gaming and deliver outstanding experiences to millions of players worldwide.

We're driven by speed, ambition, and bold ideas. At our core, we're product creators and problem-solvers who thrive in a high-performance environment. We're looking for exceptional talent—smart, adaptable, and motivated individuals eager to make an impact, scale business functions at pace, and continuously improve.

The Role

Transform data into actionable insights that drive product decisions and enhance player experiences across our gaming platforms.

What You Will Be Doing


Guide product team members through complex data landscapes, providing valuable insights that support critical decision-making
Conduct deep-dive analyses of user behavior, app performance, and product features to boost player acquisition, engagement, and retention
Ensure top-tier behavioral data collection by collaborating with analytics developers on tracking implementation and issue resolution
Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and alerting systems for real-time monitoring of key conversion metrics
Present insights clearly and effectively to stakeholders across the organization
Debug data issues and optimize tracking mechanisms to maintain data integrity


What We Are Looking For


Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail
Critical thinking mindset with proactive approach and high reliability
Solid SQL skills for querying and manipulating databases
Experience creating detailed reports and dashboards using tools like Looker or Tableau
Familiarity with digital analytics platforms such as Snowplow and GA4, plus tracking mechanisms and data layers
Experience with data modeling in Dataform and session replay/autotracking platforms is a bonus


Why you will love it

You'll be at the heart of product innovation, where your insights directly shape gaming experiences for millions of players. In our high-growth environment, you'll have the freedom to take ownership of impactful projects while collaborating with brilliant minds who value your expertise. Every analysis you deliver will drive real business decisions and help us stay ahead in the competitive gaming landscape.

Equal Opportunities

We hire based on skills, drive, and ideas—nothing else. Your background, gender, age, race, ethnicity, disability, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, or educational path will never be a barrier to joining us. We also welcome candidates from non-traditional career journeys and value diverse perspectives that challenge conventional thinking. Diversity fuels our innovation, collaboration, and growth, and we're committed to creating an environment where everyone can contribute their best work and thrive.

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Eeze is a Malta-based, technology-driven company that has been developing world-leading online gaming solutions since 1999. Underpinned by a history that directly interconnects with the rise of online gambling, the company has been a key pioneer within the international market. Over the years, Eeze has amassed a world-class team of gambling specialists who remain at the frontiers of innovation within this ever-evolving industry. With a focus on being an international B2B Slots and Live Casino provider with our Eeze brand, we have over 900 talented team members based in various locations across Europe, with our headquarters and studio in Malta - Europe’s leading iGaming hub.

Role Overview

The Business Analyst is responsible for providing data-driven insights, performance analysis, and structured reporting to support decision-making across Live Casino operations. The role focuses on analysing studio performance, identifying trends, and delivering actionable recommendations to key stakeholders, including the Director of Live Games Operations and Partner Studio Operations Manager. In addition, the Business Analyst is responsible for compiling and maintaining key operational documentation, ensuring alignment with SOPs, version control standards, and certification requirements. While supporting testing activities where required, the primary focus of the role is to act as the analytical and governance backbone of the Strategy unit.

Key Responsibilities

Performance Analysis & Reporting

Produce Monthly Studio Performance Reports Covering


Operational performance.
Incident trends.
Game Presenter-related insights (where relevant).
Studio comparisons.


Provide clear, structured recommendations based on findings.

Present Insights To


Director of Live Games Operations.
Partner Studio Operations Manager.
Internal Strategy stakeholders.


Data Analysis & Insights

Analyse Data Across


Studio performance.
Incident patterns.
Operational efficiency.
Player-related trends (where available).


Identify:


Root causes recurring issues.
Performance gaps.
Improvement opportunities.


Dashboard Development & Maintenance

Develop And Maintain Performance Dashboards To Provide Visibility On


Key operational KPIs.
Studio-level performance.
Trends over time.
Ensure dashboards are:
Accurate.
User-friendly.
Aligned with business needs.


Documentation & Certification Support

Compile And Maintain Documentation Related To


New dealer clients.
Operational flows.
Incident handling and settlement procedures.


Ensure documentation:


Follows structured version control and history tracking.
It is aligned with approved SOPs.
Supports certification and audit requirements.


Work closely with relevant stakeholders to ensure documentation is:


Accurate.
Up to date.
Consistently applied across operations.


Recommendations & Continuous Improvement:


Translate data into actionable recommendations.


Support Stakeholders In Understanding


Key risks.
Performance gaps.
Areas for optimisation.
Track impact of implemented improvements where possible.


Testing Support

Support Testing Activities When Required, Including


New dealer clients.
Platform releases.
Product updates.
Provide structured feedback based on testing outcomes.


Success Metrics (KPIs)


Quality and clarity of performance reports.
Accuracy and usability of dashboards.
Relevance and impact of recommendations.
Completeness and audit-readiness of documentation.


Essential

Stakeholder satisfaction and adoption of insights.Required Skills & Expereince


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Experience working with data, reporting, and dashboards.
Ability to translate data into clear insights.
Strong organisational and documentation skills.


Desirable:


Experience in live operations or similar environments.
Familiarity with reporting or BI tools.
Experience with SOP-driven or regulated environments.
Familiarity with AI tools for data analytics.


Key Competencies


Analytical thinking
Attention to detail.
Structured communication.
Organisation and documentation discipline.
Stakeholder management.


What’s in it for you?


Experience a dynamic and team-orientated work environment
Opportunities for personal growth and learning
An open, inclusive and supportive team where you will be valued, and your suggestions will be welcome
24 days paid holiday per year, in addition to local public holidays
Birthday Leave, a paid day off in the month of your birthday
Competitive Salary
Hybrid Working
Risk Benefits such as Private Medical Insurance and Life Assurance (2x annual salary)
Annual Wellness Allowance
Team Building Opportunities
Monthly Lunch Allowance
Parking (limited)
Local discounts and more…


Our team is committed to keeping remuneration and benefits under constant review to make sure what we offer stays relevant.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Description

We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Junior Data Analyst to join our dynamic team. This entry-level position offers an excellent opportunity for individuals who are passionate about data and eager to develop their analytical skills in a professional environment. As a Junior Data Analyst, you will collaborate with various departments to collect, process, and analyze data to support business decision-making. You will work under the guidance of senior analysts and managers to transform raw data into meaningful insights that drive strategic initiatives. The ideal candidate should have a strong foundation in data analysis techniques, be proficient with data tools, and possess a keen eye for accuracy and problem-solving. In this role, you will gain hands-on experience with data visualization, reporting, and database management, contributing directly to the company’s growth and operational efficiency. This position is perfect for someone looking to kick-start their career in data analytics and grow their expertise in a supportive and innovative workplace. Join us and be part of a team that values curiosity, collaboration, and continuous learning, all while making a tangible impact through data-driven insights.

Responsibilities


Collect, clean, and validate data from various sources to ensure accuracy and completeness.
Assist in the development and maintenance of dashboards and reports for internal stakeholders.
Conduct basic statistical analysis to identify trends, patterns, and insights.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand data requirements and provide analytical support.
Prepare visualizations and presentations to communicate findings effectively to non-technical audiences.
Support senior analysts in complex data projects and ad hoc reporting tasks.
Monitor data quality and identify areas for process improvement or data enhancement.


Requirements


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, or a related field.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or Power BI.
Basic knowledge of SQL for querying databases.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Ability to work collaboratively in a team-oriented environment.
Eagerness to learn new technologies and data analysis methodologies.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About The Role

Curely is looking for a Medical Receptionist to be the first point of contact for our patients. In this role, you’ll ensure a smooth check-in process, manage appointment scheduling, and provide administrative support to our medical team. If you have strong communication skills, a patient-friendly attitude, and a passion for healthcare, we’d love to hear from you!

What You'll Do


Greet and check in patients with professionalism and warmth.
Schedule, confirm, and manage patient appointments efficiently.
Handle phone calls, emails, and patient inquiries.
Maintain patient records and ensure all information is up to date.
Verify insurance details and process billing transactions.
Coordinate with doctors, nurses, and other staff for seamless patient care.
Uphold confidentiality and comply with HIPAA regulations.
Assist with other front desk and clerical duties as needed.


What We’re Looking For


High school diploma or equivalent (Associate’s degree preferred).
1-2 years of experience in a receptionist or administrative role (medical setting preferred).
Strong verbal and written communication skills.
Proficiency in scheduling software and Microsoft Office Suite.
Ability to multitask and work efficiently in a fast-paced environment.
Friendly, patient-focused attitude with excellent customer service skills.
Knowledge of medical terminology and insurance processes is a plus.


Bonus Skills & Experience


Bilingual in English and Spanish (or other languages) is a plus.
Experience using electronic health record (EHR) systems.
Previous work in a hospital or clinic setting.


Why Join Curely?


Competitive Salary – Fair and rewarding compensation.
Health & Dental Insurance – Comprehensive coverage for you and your family.
Paid Time Off & Holidays – Enjoy a work-life balance.
Career Growth Opportunities – Training and promotion possibilities.
Supportive Team Culture – Work in a collaborative and respectful environment.
Modern & Innovative Workplace – Utilize cutting-edge healthcare technology.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking to hire a data analyst to join our data team. You will take responsibility for managing our master data set, developing reports, and troubleshooting data issues. Data Analyst Responsibilities: Managing master data, including creation, updates, and deletion. Managing users and user roles. Provide quality assurance of imported data, working with quality assurance analysts if necessary. Commissioning and decommissioning of data sets. Processing confidential data and information according to guidelines. Helping develop reports and analyses. Managing and designing the reporting environment, including data sources, security, and metadata. Supporting the data warehouse in identifying and revising reporting requirements. Supporting initiatives for data integrity and normalization. Assessing tests and implementing new or upgraded software and assisting with strategic decisions on new systems. Generating reports from single or multiple systems. Troubleshooting the reporting database environment and reports. Evaluating changes and updates to source production systems. Training end-users on new reports and dashboards. Providing technical expertise in data storage structures, data mining, and data cleansing. Data Analyst Requirements: Bachelor s degree from an accredited university or college in computer science. Work experience as a data analyst or in a related field. Ability to work with stakeholders to assess potential risks. Ability to analyze existing tools and databases and provide software solution recommendations. Ability to translate business requirements into nontechnical, lay terms. High-level experience in methodologies and processes for managing large-scale databases. Demonstrated experience in handling large data sets and relational databases. Understanding of addressing and metadata standards. High-level written and verbal communication skills.
