Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Position Title: Data Analyst

Position Status: Full-time in-office or hybrid, Natick, MA

About Uwill

Uwill is the leading mental health and wellness solution for colleges, schools, and students. Utilizing its proprietary technology and counselor team, Uwill pioneered the first student and therapist matching platform. The solution offers an immediate appointment with a licensed counselor based on student preferences, all modalities of teletherapy, a direct crisis connection 24/7/365, prescription management, on-demand medical support, basic needs assistance, wellness programming, realtime data, and support. Uwill serves more than 4 million students at 500+ institutions worldwide.

About The Role

Uwill is seeking an experienced analyst who will enable critical product and business decisions as we continue to grow and expand our platform. This full-time position reports to the Vice President of Product Management and plays a key role in driving data-informed decisions across product, customer success, and marketing.

You will help guide the setup of the data infrastructure and focus on analyzing product performance, identifying opportunities for growth, and supporting strategic initiatives through data insights and market research. You will also help build dashboards that serve as single sources of truth across departments, enabling easy access to key metrics and consistent data storytelling for both internal and client-facing purposes. Additionally, you’ll design, run, and analyze surveys to support marketing initiatives and measure clinical outcomes.

Your work will directly impact the optimization of our platform, the success of our customers, and the overall growth of the business.

Key Skills


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a focus on actionable insights
Solid understanding of product metrics, experimentation, and statistical measurement
Ability to present complex data clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders
Detail-oriented and highly organized, with the ability to manage multiple tasks
Collaborative mindset with experience working across product, engineering, and other cross-functional teams
Proficient in data analysis tools (e.g., SQL, Excel, R) and tracking/visualization tools (e.g., Amplitude, Tableau, Looker)


Key Responsibilities


Analyze product performance and user behavior to provide insights for product decisions
Support product managers by defining and tracking KPIs and success metrics
Build and maintain dashboards and reports to track business and product performance
Partner with teams to identify growth opportunities and optimize user engagement
Design and analyze surveys to support marketing and customer success initiatives
Lead analyses of clinical outcomes and other user data to drive research initiatives and evidence-based product strategy


Required Qualifications


3+ years in product or business analytics, preferably in tech or healthcare
Proficiency in Excel, SQL and data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker)
Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., business, analytics, computer science) or equivalent experience
Strong communication and collaboration skills
Self-starter


Preferred Qualifications


Prior work in healthcare or mental health industries
Proficiency with R, Stata, or other data analysis software tools
Experience with clinical survey instruments (e.g., PHQ-9, GAD-7, or equivalent)
Familiarity with privacy and regulatory requirements (e.g., HIPAA)
Exposure to high-growth startup environments
Familiarity with AI tools and workflows


Employee Benefits


100% company-paid medical, vision, and dental coverage for the employee and family
11 paid holidays per year
Flexible paid time off
401(k) plan with company match
A team-oriented culture, supportive supervisory team, and accessible leadership team
Opportunities for advancement within our unique company


As one of the fastest-growing companies in Massachusetts, you will be challenged to build your skills and take on increasing responsibility. Our office environment is open, collaborative, fast-paced, and high-energy.

Hiring Base Salary Range: $50,000 - $100,000 (Salary to be determined by the education, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities of the applicant, internal equity, and alignment with market data)

Applicants must be authorized to work in the United States without the need for visa sponsorship now or in the future.

Uwill is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We thrive because of the diversity of our employees. At Uwill, people of different backgrounds, experiences, abilities, and perspectives are always welcome. We foster an environment of mutual respect and do not tolerate any discrimination. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, or protected veteran status.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Data Analyst

About Busek

Busek Co. Inc. is a pioneer in advanced space propulsion and technology. Located in Natick, MA, we develop innovative solutions that enable the future of space exploration. Our products and technologies include electric propulsion systems, chemical propulsion, and advanced material development. Join our team to work on cutting-edge projects that shape the space industry.

Position Overview

Busek is seeking a Data Analyst to support the analysis of test and production data for advanced space propulsion systems. This role will work closely with Engineering, Test Operations, and Manufacturing to transform large volumes of technical data into meaningful insights that improve product performance, manufacturing processes, and production readiness.

The ideal candidate is analytical, curious, and self-motivated, with the ability to work independently and quickly learn new systems and technologies. While experience with electric propulsion is beneficial, candidates with strong data analysis skills and experience supporting engineering or manufacturing organizations are encouraged to apply.

Key Responsibilities


Analyze data generated from engineering testing, production, and manufacturing activities to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities for improvement
Develop reports, dashboards, and visualizations that communicate technical findings to engineering and leadership teams
Partner with Engineering, Manufacturing, and Test Operations to support product development and production initiatives
Validate data quality and ensure the integrity of engineering and manufacturing datasets
Support root cause investigations through data analysis and statistical evaluation
Assist in developing automated reporting and data analysis tools to improve efficiency
Document analysis methods, assumptions, and results to support engineering decisions
Present findings and recommendations clearly to technical and non-technical audiences
Support continuous improvement initiatives by identifying opportunities to improve data collection, reporting, and decision-making


Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Data Analytics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Physics, Statistics, or a related technical field
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex technical data
Experience analyzing large datasets using Excel and other data analysis tools
Ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and learn new technologies with minimal supervision
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Attention to detail and a commitment to producing accurate, actionable analysis


Preferred Skills


Experience using Python, MATLAB, SQL, Power BI, Tableau, or similar analytical tools
Experience supporting engineering, manufacturing, aerospace, or other technical organizations
Familiarity with statistical analysis and data visualization techniques
Experience working with production, manufacturing, or test data
Knowledge of electric propulsion, aerospace systems, or other advanced engineering technologies is a plus but not required


Benefits


Health, dental, and vision insurance
Paid time off and holidays
Retirement savings plan (401k)
Professional development opportunities
Collaborative and innovative work environment


What We Offer


Opportunity to work on cutting-edge space propulsion technology
Exposure to advanced engineering, testing, and manufacturing operations
Opportunity to influence product development and production decisions through meaningful data analysis
Collaborative environment working alongside engineers developing next-generation space systems


U.S. Citizenship Requirement

Due to the nature of work performed under U.S. government contracts and applicable security and contractual requirements, this position is limited to U.S. citizens.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
About The Role

The role bridges the gap between raw data infrastructure and executive decision-making, translating complex data models into actionable business strategies and performance dashboards. Operating within a high-growth tech environment, this position focuses on analyzing customer acquisition metrics, unit economics, and operational efficiency.

The role works closely with data engineering and business stakeholders to design scalable metrics frameworks and maintain the integrity of business intelligence platforms. This requires deep analytical curiosity, strong technical acumen in SQL and data modeling, and the ability to articulate complex analytical findings to non-technical leaders.

Key Responsibilities


Design, build, and maintain production-grade Tableau and Looker dashboards to track company-wide KPIs and operational metrics
Write complex, optimized SQL queries across Snowflake and BigQuery data warehouses to extract, transform, and analyze large-scale business datasets
Partner with data engineering to define schema requirements, build dbt models, and ensure clean, reliable data pipelines for business reporting
Perform ad-hoc deep-dive analyses on user retention, lifetime value (LTV), customer acquisition cost (CAC), and product feature adoption
Establish statistical measurement frameworks for A/B testing, marketing attribution, and revenue forecasting models
Document data definitions, metric logic, and taxonomy standards to maintain a single source of truth across all business departments


What We Are Looking For


3–6 years of experience as a business data analyst, analytics engineer, or product analyst in a fast-paced technology company
Advanced proficiency in SQL, including window functions, CTEs, query optimization, and experience working with Snowflake, Redshift, or BigQuery
Hands-on experience with modern BI tools (Looker, Tableau, or Sigma) and data transformation frameworks like dbt
Solid understanding of SaaS business metrics, including ARR, net retention, unit economics, and cohort analysis
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science, or equivalent experience
Bonus: Experience with Python or R for statistical analysis, familiarity with Airflow, or knowledge of product analytics tools like Amplitude or Mixpanel

Title: Data Analyst Internship
Description:
About ConnectPrep: Our goal is to redefine "tutoring" - to use data, insight, and our extensive teaching experience find solutions for our students. ConnectPrep is about connecting - connecting students, parents, educators, and the community. We have bold ideas and an eye towards the future.

The Opportunity: We are offering a Data Analyst Internship for qualified candidates. This is a cross-disciplinary role, both instructional and technical. You'll engage with our team of educators to validate and iterate the models that drive our analytics. Our Data Analysts develop an understanding of our algorithms, the technologies and platforms that support our products, and our proprietary educational content. This knowledge will then be synthesized into actionable tests to build modules that will assist in identifying the academic needs of each individual student. You will engage with team members that think outside of the box, with the goal of improving education through the creation of a data infrastructure and insightful analytics.

Requirements

What we are looking for:


Core skills
You enjoy creative problem solving and working with messy, real-world data.
You have strong math/quantitative skills and excellent attention to detail.
You are curious about how data can improve learning and student outcomes.
Academic & technical background
You are studying (or have studied) Applied Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field.
You have some coding experience and a strong interest in technology and AI.
You have experience with at least one of the following:
SQL, Python data tools (Pandas, NumPy, or similar)
Building simple data pipelines or analysis scripts in Python
Excel & analysis skills
You are comfortable doing serious work in Excel, including several of the following:
Advanced formulas and functions
Tables and formatting
Conditional formatting
Advanced charting and visualizations
Pivot tables and pivot reporting
Using Excel productively for analysis, not just basic spreadsheets
Data tables, simulations, and Solver
(Bonus) VBA and macros
AI, data, and analytics work you will support
Work with AI tools and workflows to support data-driven decision-making
Perform data mining and cleaning from multiple sources (web, internal trackers, marketing platforms)
Support projects involving AI and chatbot development for ConnectPrep's programs and website
Conduct SEO and keyword research to identify growth opportunities and track performance
Analyze website analytics (traffic, engagement, conversions) and prepare simple dashboards or summary reports
Assist with data analysis for marketing campaigns, content performance, and business operations
Help test, document, and iterate on new data and AI workflows as they are developed
Nice to have
Prior internship or project experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or marketing analytics
Familiarity with web analytics and marketing tools (for example, Google Analytics, GA4, Search Console, Looker Studio, or similar platforms)
Experience visualizing data in tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Data Studio
Experience working with SEO tools or keyword platforms
Experience collaborating in remote or hybrid teams (Slack, Zoom, project management boards)
Benefits

Why join ConnectPrep?


Get in on the ground floor of an exciting new company, with the potential to help shape our direction
Be involved with a team that has bold ideas, with the goal of reinventing education across the globe
Gain valuable experience, meet technology innovators, and learn about the business side of our company


Sound Interesting?

Please submit your resume and letter of interest at careers@connectprep.com.

All applicants must be a U.S. Citizen or National of the U.S., an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence, or an alien authorized to work in the U.S. for this employer. ConnectPrep is an equal opportunity employer EEO/M/F/D/V.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Data Analyst

About Busek

Busek Co. Inc. is a pioneer in advanced space propulsion and technology. Located in Natick, MA, we develop innovative solutions that enable the future of space exploration. Our products and technologies include electric propulsion systems, chemical propulsion, and advanced material development. Join our team to work on cutting-edge projects that shape the space industry.

Position Overview

Busek is seeking a Data Analyst to support the analysis of test and production data for advanced space propulsion systems. This role will work closely with Engineering, Test Operations, and Manufacturing to transform large volumes of technical data into meaningful insights that improve product performance, manufacturing processes, and production readiness.

The ideal candidate is analytical, curious, and self-motivated, with the ability to work independently and quickly learn new systems and technologies. While experience with electric propulsion is beneficial, candidates with strong data analysis skills and experience supporting engineering or manufacturing organizations are encouraged to apply.

Key Responsibilities


Analyze data generated from engineering testing, production, and manufacturing activities to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities for improvement
Develop reports, dashboards, and visualizations that communicate technical findings to engineering and leadership teams
Partner with Engineering, Manufacturing, and Test Operations to support product development and production initiatives
Validate data quality and ensure the integrity of engineering and manufacturing datasets
Support root cause investigations through data analysis and statistical evaluation
Assist in developing automated reporting and data analysis tools to improve efficiency
Document analysis methods, assumptions, and results to support engineering decisions
Present findings and recommendations clearly to technical and non-technical audiences
Support continuous improvement initiatives by identifying opportunities to improve data collection, reporting, and decision-making


Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Data Analytics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Physics, Statistics, or a related technical field
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex technical data
Experience analyzing large datasets using Excel and other data analysis tools
Ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and learn new technologies with minimal supervision
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Attention to detail and a commitment to producing accurate, actionable analysis


Preferred Skills


Experience using Python, MATLAB, SQL, Power BI, Tableau, or similar analytical tools
Experience supporting engineering, manufacturing, aerospace, or other technical organizations
Familiarity with statistical analysis and data visualization techniques
Experience working with production, manufacturing, or test data
Knowledge of electric propulsion, aerospace systems, or other advanced engineering technologies is a plus but not required


Benefits


Health, dental, and vision insurance
Paid time off and holidays
Retirement savings plan (401k)
Professional development opportunities
Collaborative and innovative work environment


What We Offer


Opportunity to work on cutting-edge space propulsion technology
Exposure to advanced engineering, testing, and manufacturing operations
Opportunity to influence product development and production decisions through meaningful data analysis
Collaborative environment working alongside engineers developing next-generation space systems


U.S. Citizenship Requirement

Due to the nature of work performed under U.S. government contracts and applicable security and contractual requirements, this position is limited to U.S. citizens.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
About Purple Carrot

Founded in 2014, Purple Carrot delivers clean, long-lasting energy through the power of plants. As the only company in America devoted entirely to plant-based eating, we have spent over a decade perfecting the balance of protein and fiber that only plants can provide. Offering chef-crafted, dietitian-designed meals delivered right to your door, we make plant-forward eating effortless, flexible, and endlessly exciting. Our menu spans quick weeknight dishes, occasion-worthy dinners, breakfast, lunch, snacks, and ready-to-eat meals.

Purple Carrot prioritizes taste and flexibility, offering no membership fees, the freedom to skip or unskip weekly deliveries whenever you want, and a no-pressure, cancel-anytime policy. We pride ourselves on doing plants better than anyone else - and we have the receipts to prove it.

Description

We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our mission-driven team. As an integral part of the Data department, you will join forces with the Sr. Manager, Data and Analytics (to whom you will also report) in our continued effort to inspire people to eat more plants!

We are looking for candidates who are passionate about transforming and analyzing complex datasets into clear, actionable, and timely insights that drive strategic decision-making and improve business results.

As the Senior Data Analyst, you will be focused on leveraging SQL, Python, and BI tools to identify opportunities, optimize key metrics, and measure performance across the organization. This is a high-visibility role that partners directly and frequently with senior leadership, translating data into high-quality analysis to support the decisions the business acts on. You'll also work closely with marketing and other teams across the organization to help them become proficient in the data assets you produce. You'll be part of a small data analytics team, so your work is visible, and your voice matters.

If you are a detail-oriented problem-solver who thrives at the intersection of data, analysis, and strategy, we'd like to meet you.

We greatly thank all applicants; however, only those under serious consideration will be contacted.


Location


This role is based in Dedham, MA, 3 days per week in the office
Our hybrid approach includes 3 in-office days each week, giving teams the best of both flexibility and collaboration. Many team members choose to come in more often - it's a great way to stay inspired and engaged with the team



On the Plate


Query and analyze large datasets to extract and communicate meaningful insights
Build and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using SQL, Python, and BI platforms (e.g., Looker, Omni, Sigma)
Build and apply machine learning models in Python — from predictive modeling (e.g., cancel/churn prediction, demand forecasting) to applied techniques (e.g., clustering, regression) that generate deeper analytical insight
Train and support teams on the data assets you build, so they have what they need to self-serve
Own forecasting and experimentation initiatives end-to-end, from framework design through measurement and recommendation
Partner with stakeholders across the business to understand what they actually need and provide data-driven solutions
Produce clear reports, visualizations, and presentations for leadership, with the ability to influence decisions without formal authority
Monitor and report on key performance indicators and emerging trends
Assist with ad hoc projects and tasks as necessary



Main Ingredients for the Role


3+ years' experience as a Data Analyst, Business Analyst, or similar analytical role
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Economics, Mathematics, Data Science/Analytics) or equivalent experience
Able to commute to the Dedham, MA office 3 days/week
Proficient in SQL for data querying and transformation
Skilled in Python - for advanced analysis and automation, including building and running machine learning models
Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Looker, Omni, Sigma)
Strong Excel skills for analysis and reporting



Extra Special Sauce


A collaborative team player who thrives on a small analytics team
You love teaching almost as much as you love a clean dataset - nothing makes you happier than watching a teammate go from confused to confident
You've got opinions, and you're not afraid to share them - pushing back when it's the right call, not just going along to get along
You are adaptable to a fast-paced working environment with changing priorities based on the business needs
Genuine curiosity about the "why" behind the numbers, not just the "what."
Experience with dbt or modern data stack tools is a plus



Compensation

The anticipated base salary range for this role is $95,000 - $115,000. Compensation is based on experience and qualifications, and the posted range is not a guarantee of any specific offer.

Garnish

We love our staff, and show it with benefits (including but not limited to medical, dental, vision, FSA, and life insurance), matching retirement plan, generous and flexible paid time off, Purple Carrot box delivery credits, professional development, and the opportunity to work in collaboration with teammates who are passionate about food!

Final Touches

We are committed to a safe and inclusive work environment, free of discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, genetics, national origin, ancestry, age, medical condition, mental or physical disability, handicap, veteran status, or marital status.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Crown Linen Service, Inc is looking to add an ON-SITE Data Analyst to the team in Brockton, MA!

Join Crown Linen Service Inc as a Full-Time Data Analyst and immerse yourself in an environment where innovation meets excellence. Located in Brockton, MA, this onsite role offers the opportunity to tackle real-world problems and drive strategic initiatives. With a competitive pay range of $80,000 - $90,000, you will be rewarded for your analytical skills and contributions to our forward-thinking team. Experience the thrill of collaborating with passionate professionals dedicated to high performance and integrity in everything they do.

THIS IS NOT A REMOTE POSITION AND REQUIRES DAILY ON-SITE REPORTING TO OUR BROCKTON, MA LOCATION.

You will be provided with great benefits such as Medical, Dental, Vision, 401(k), Life Insurance, Health Savings Account, Flexible Spending Account, Competitive Salary, and Paid Time Off. Don’t miss the chance to shape the future of our services while advancing your career in a supportive and dynamic workplace.

Make a difference as a Data Analyst

We are seeking a Data Analyst to join our data team. We are a small team at a well-established company, giving us the opportunity to make a real and immediate impact. This role focuses on developing reports and automated dashboards to support multiple departments.

What We Offer


A nimble work environment that provides the freedom to explore our data landscape and discover new strategies
An executive team that values the insights that data can provide
A direct impact on the business via reporting, as well as data-driven strategies
A company culture that supports its workforce and is focused on operating an ethical and sustainable business.


What You Bring


Three to five (3-5) years of working experience with progressively more responsibility and advanced work product.
A curious mind - you will often be tasked with vague targets, and it is up to you how to get there.
A collaborative spirit - we touch every aspect of the business and need to be able to work seamlessly across all business functions.
A heightened business acumen - we are not just data folks. We regularly interact with all levels of the organization and must be able to relate our findings to executable business strategies.
A technically savvy approach.
Be an Excel wizard - much like an instrument, you should be able to play Excel like a fiddle
A comfortable working knowledge of how to query data sets across multiple sources with significant exposure to at least one SQL language, including Joins, CTEs, Aggregations and/or Windows Functions (our bread and butter)
The ability to transform vague business requests into concrete data driven solutions
Work experience with at least one BI tool such as PowerBI, Tableau, etc.
The ability to design, implement and maintain an ever-changing ETL landscape

As a new Data Analyst at Crown Linen Service Inc, you can expect a dynamic work environment where each day brings new challenges. Your schedule will be Monday to Friday, with standard business hours dedicated to analyzing data, identifying trends, and providing actionable insights to enhance our services. You will collaborate closely with various departments, facilitating cross-functional meetings to ensure alignment on projects and initiatives. Expect to create and maintain detailed reports, supporting strategic decision-making processes. Your role will involve problem-solving and innovation, requiring you to think critically and propose solutions that drive efficiency.

Additionally, you will participate in ongoing training and development opportunities, allowing you to continuously enhance your skills and knowledge in a high-performance culture.

Make your move

If you're ready to take your Data analyst skills to the next level and join a team that values hard work and your business analyst expertise, apply today!

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment.

Salary: $80000.00 - $90000.00 per year

Title: Master Data Analyst
Description:
Job Overview:

The Master Data Analyst coordinates and performs day to day tasks for the creation, maintenance, and data quality throughout the lifecycle of a material for all Routes to Market, Business Units, and Bottlers. Develops knowledge and understands the end-to-end processes including impacts to up and down stream processes and systems. Supports the initiatives product launch process to ensure master data successfully supports flawless execution. Is responsible for data accuracy, performing and reporting on periodic audits and data integrity checks. Provides data verification and analysis in SAP material master and applicable systems. Reviews data input and processes to ensure quality, consistency, and timeliness. Maintains and supports relationships with all business partners related to material Master Data. Collaborates with functional groups on effectiveness and efficiencies of Master Data processes, including Sales Operations, Marketing, Supply Chain, IT, etc. Leverages data tools to analyze SAP and Legacy systems data to ensure compliance with business processes and a high level of data quality. Develops and maintains up-to-date and accurate documentation and provides knowledge transfer for business processes & workflows within functional area. Improves and streamlines business processes within functional area. Participates in and/or leads projects internally to include material testing and supporting business design requirements, if applicable.

Total Rewards:


Salary range: USD $62,500.00 - USD $80,000.00
Actual placement within the compensation range may vary depending on experience, skills, and other factors
Benefits, subject to election and eligibility: Medical, Dental, Vision, Disability, Paid Time Off (including paid parental leave, vacation, and sick time), 401k with company match, Tuition Reimbursement, and Mileage Reimbursement
Annual bonus based on performance and eligibility



Requirements:

Qualification:


Bachelor’s Degree from an accredited institution preferred
3+ year professional work experience in the field of Finance or General Business
SAP experience highly preferred
Proficient in Microsoft Outlook (using advanced functions in MS Excel such as pivot tables, V look ups and some base knowledge of MS Access).
Excellent attention to detail, accuracy, advanced analytical skills, quality, and strong organizational skills
An understanding of how to manipulate large datasets, combined with strong customer service and interpersonal skills
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills, both quantitative and qualitative



Company Overview:

Keurig Dr Pepper (Nasdaq: KDP) is a leading beverage company with more than 150 owned, licensed and partner brands that meet a wide range of needs and occasions. Our North American refreshment beverage business holds leadership positions across carbonated soft drinks, water, juice and mixers with a portfolio of iconic brands such as Dr Pepper®, Canada Dry®, Mott’s®, A&W®, Peñafiel®, GHOST®, 7UP®, Snapple®, Clamato® and Core Hydration®. Our global coffee business spans more than 100 markets and includes the leading Keurig® single‑serve brewing system in the U.S. and Canada, along with powerhouse brands such as Peet’s, L’OR and Jacobs, and other regional coffee leaders. Our more than 50,000 employees aim to enhance the experience of every beverage and coffee occasion while making a positive impact for people, communities and the planet.

We strive to be an employer of choice, providing a culture and opportunities that empower our team to grow and develop. We offer robust benefits to support your health and wellness as well as your personal and financial well-being. We also provide employee programs designed to enhance your professional growth and development, while ensuring you feel valued, inspired and appreciated at work.

Whatever your area of expertise, at KDP you can be a part of a team that’s proud of its brands, partnerships, innovation and growth. Will you join us?

Keurig Dr Pepper is an equal opportunity employer and recruits qualified applicants and advances in employment its employees without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, disability or association with a person with a disability, medical condition, genetic information, ethnic or national origin, marital status, veteran status, or any other status protected by law

This technology helps us efficiently identify candidates whose qualifications align with our open roles. AI does not make hiring decisions; all decisions throughout the hiring process are made by talent acquisition team members. If you prefer not to have your application processed using artificial intelligence, you may opt out by emailing your resume and qualifications directly to kdpjobs@kdrp.com in lieu of clicking Apply. Please include the job title and location or Job ID # in the email subject line in order for your email application to be considered.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking to hire a data analyst to join our data team. You will take responsibility for managing our master data set, developing reports, and troubleshooting data issues. To do well in this role you need a very fine eye for detail, experience as a data analyst, and a deep understanding of the popular data analysis tools and databases. Data Analyst Responsibilities: Managing master data, including creation, updates, and deletion. Managing users and user roles. Provide quality assurance of imported data, working with quality assurance analysts if necessary. Commissioning and decommissioning of data sets. Processing confidential data and information according to guidelines. Helping develop reports and analyses. Managing and designing the reporting environment, including data sources, security, and metadata. Supporting the data warehouse in identifying and revising reporting requirements. Supporting initiatives for data integrity and normalization. Assessing tests and implementing new or upgraded software and assisting with strategic decisions on new systems. Generating reports from single or multiple systems. Troubleshooting the reporting database environment and reports. Evaluating changes and updates to source production systems. Training end-users on new reports and dashboards. Providing technical expertise in data storage structures, data mining, and data cleansing. Data Analyst Requirements: Bachelor s degree from an accredited university or college in computer science. Work experience as a data analyst or in a related field. Ability to work with stakeholders to assess potential risks. Ability to analyze existing tools and databases and provide software solution recommendations. Ability to translate business requirements into nontechnical, lay terms. High-level experience in methodologies and processes for managing large-scale databases. Demonstrated experience in handling large data sets and relational databases. Understanding of addressing and metadata standards. High-level written and verbal communication skills.

Title: Senior Quantitative Analyst
Description:
Lead Quantitative Technology Manager

Hedge Fund Technology | Boston




VARO Partners has been retained by a leading global investment manager to identify senior quantitative technology leaders to help shape the next generation of front-office investment platforms across Securities and Risk Analytics.




These are highly visible positions sitting at the intersection of quantitative research, investment technology and engineering. Rather than building models yourself, you'll leverage your previous quantitative experience to lead the design, delivery and evolution of sophisticated investment systems used by portfolio managers, quantitative researchers and investment teams. Benefitting from significant ownership, close partnership with investment teams and the opportunity to take on leadership responsibilities immediately or over time.




The Opportunity

This is not a traditional Business Analyst position. We're looking for individuals who began their careers building quantitative models, pricing libraries, risk platforms or investment analytics, but who now enjoy solving larger technology and organizational challenges.




You'll act as the technical bridge between front-office investment professionals, quantitative researchers and software engineering teams, ensuring complex quantitative ideas are successfully translated into scalable production systems. Working across the full investment technology lifecycle, you'll influence architecture, platform design, prioritization and implementation while helping shape the future direction of investment analytics.




Responsibilities

Partner directly with portfolio managers, quantitative researchers and senior investment stakeholders to understand complex business requirements.
Translate quantitative models and investment workflows into scalable technology solutions.
Lead the functional design and implementation of front-office analytics platforms.
Drive delivery of large-scale initiatives spanning risk analytics, securities analytics, portfolio management and investment data.
Collaborate closely with engineering teams to define architecture, APIs, data models and system design.
Ensure solutions are scalable, performant, auditable and suitable for production use.
Lead requirements gathering, functional specifications, testing strategies and production rollout.
Provide subject matter expertise throughout the software development lifecycle.
Mentor junior team members and contribute to the growth of the function.
Help define the future direction of quantitative technology within the organization.




We're particularly interested in people who have experience building or overseeing:

Risk Analytics platforms
Securities Analytics platforms
Pricing libraries
P&L systems
Portfolio Analytics
Market Risk systems
Fixed Income analytics
Derivatives pricing
Quantitative data platforms
Investment infrastructure




What We're Looking For

The successful individual will combine deep quantitative knowledge with exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills. You'll be equally comfortable discussing stochastic models with quantitative researchers, reviewing technology architecture with engineering teams and working directly with investment professionals to define future platform capabilities.




Experience managing people is welcomed but not essential. Both positions (Securities and Risk) offer a clear pathway into technical leadership for candidates looking to move beyond individual contributor roles.




Why Join?

Opportunity to shape industry-leading investment technology.
Direct interaction with portfolio managers and quantitative researchers.
Highly collaborative environment with significant influence across front-office technology.
Clear progression into technical and organizational leadership.
Exposure to modern data platforms, AI-enabled tooling and next-generation quantitative infrastructure.




j.gates@varopartners.com

Title: SIOP Data Analyst - Direct Hire
Description:
Senior Data Analyst

Department: Office of the COO

Reports To: SIOP Manager

Location: Full‑time Onsite — MetroWest Boston Area

Schedule: 5 days onsite (future potential for 4 onsite / 1 remote)

Target Salary Range: $80,000 – $120,000




About the Company (Disguised)

Our client is a leading provider of advanced manufacturing and automation solutions, operating multiple technology centers across North America. The organization specializes in custom machining systems, automation integration, and turnkey production solutions, supporting a wide range of industrial and high‑precision manufacturing customers.

Position Summary

The Senior Data Analyst is a key analytical partner within the Office of the COO, responsible for transforming complex operational, sales, and service data into strategic insights that drive business performance. This role leads the development of enterprise dashboards, reporting frameworks, and data models that support SIOP, sales operations, and aftermarket service functions.

The analyst collaborates closely with cross‑functional leaders to deliver data‑driven recommendations that enhance revenue growth, operational efficiency, and continuous improvement initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the collection, integration, and analysis of data from CRM, ERP, and enterprise systems to support sales performance, aftermarket services, and SIOP planning cycles.
Design and maintain advanced dashboards and KPI reports across equipment sales, service revenue, parts consumption, and forecasting accuracy.
Serve as the primary analytics partner for SIOP, providing demand/supply analysis, forecast variance reporting, and scenario modeling for executive decision‑making.
Develop standardized aftermarket analytics, including service contract utilization, customer lifecycle metrics, and parts‑usage trends.
Perform complex statistical analyses to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for operational and revenue optimization.
Ensure data integrity and governance through rigorous validation, documentation, and quality‑assurance practices.
Collaborate with Sales, Operations, Finance, and Aftermarket teams to translate business needs into scalable, automated reporting solutions.
Lead ad‑hoc analytical projects and present insights and recommendations to senior leadership.

Required Qualifications

Education: Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Computer Science, Business Administration, or related field (or equivalent experience).
Experience: 2–7 years in data analysis, business intelligence, or analytics—preferably in manufacturing, distribution, or industrial sales environments.

Technical Skills

Advanced proficiency with Excel, Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualization tools.
Strong SQL skills for complex data extraction and transformation.
Experience with Python, R, or similar analytical programming languages.
Familiarity with ERP/CRM platforms such as Infor CSI, Salesforce, ServiceNow, SAP, or similar systems.
Understanding of SIOP/S&OP planning tools and processes.

Soft Skills

Ability to synthesize complex data into clear, actionable insights for technical and non‑technical audiences.
Strong critical‑thinking and problem‑solving abilities.
Excellent organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment.
Proven cross‑functional collaboration and stakeholder‑management capabilities.

Supervisory Responsibilities

No direct reports; mentorship of junior analysts may be expected.

Travel Requirements

Minimal; occasional travel to regional facilities.

Physical Requirements

Extended periods of computer‑based work.
Ability to lift up to 20 lbs as needed.

Title: Remote Business Intelligence & Data Analyst
Description:
We are seeking a Business Intelligence & Data Analyst with 3-5 years of experience to lead our Power BI environment and enhance data-driven decision-making across the organization.

Key Responsibilities Include


Owning end-to-end Power BI development, including semantic models, DAX, and dashboards for multiple teams.
Maintaining and improving data pipelines within Microsoft Fabric to ensure reliable data flow.
Building scalable data models using star schema and fact/dimension design principles.
Delivering automated reports and providing insights into trader behavior, risk signals, and operational metrics.
Partnering with cross-functional teams to translate business questions into analytical frameworks and actionable insights.


Qualifications And Skills


3-5 years in business intelligence or data analytics.
Strong SQL, Power BI (semantic models, DAX), and Python/PySpark skills.
Experience with Microsoft Fabric or similar platforms like Azure Synapse or Databricks.
Knowledge of data modeling, API integration, and financial time-series data is a plus.


Ideal Candidate Traits


Ability to translate complex data into clear narratives for non-technical stakeholders.
Autonomous work ethic, ownership, and initiative in a fast-paced environment.
Critical thinking, creativity, and adaptability to evolving priorities.

  • Delivering automated reports and providing insights into trader behavior, risk signals, and operational metrics.
  • Partnering with cross-functional teams to translate business questions into analytical frameworks and actionable insights.

Qualifications And Skills

  • 3-5 years in business intelligence or data analytics.
  • Strong SQL, Power BI (semantic models, DAX), and Python/PySpark skills.
  • Experience with Microsoft Fabric or similar platforms like Azure Synapse or Databricks.
  • Knowledge of data modeling, API integration, and financial time-series data is a plus.

Ideal Candidate Traits

  • Ability to translate complex data into clear narratives for non-technical stakeholders.
  • Autonomous work ethic, ownership, and initiative in a fast-paced environment.
  • Critical thinking, creativity, and adaptability to evolving priorities.

Title: Senior, Data Analytics & BI, Global, Converse
Description:
The annual base salary for this position ranges from $107,700.00 in our lowest geographic market to $212,600.00 in our highest geographic market. Actual salary will vary based on a candidate's location, qualifications, skills and experience.

Who You’ll Work With

Information about benefits can be found here.

The Data, Analytics & Intelligence team sits at the center of Converse’s data and insight ecosystem, enabling Planning, Merchandising, Finance, and enterprise partners to make smarter, faster decisions through trusted data, advanced analytics, and scalable Business Intelligence capabilities. This role reports into the Data, Analytics & Intelligence organization and collaborates cross-functionally with business leaders, analysts, data engineers, and technology partners to modernize the data foundation and elevate analytics across Converse.

Who We Are Looking For

Converse is looking for a data and analytics professional who combines deep technical expertise with strong business acumen and a passion for turning complex data into meaningful action. The ideal candidate brings experience applying advanced analytical methods, statistical modeling, forecasting, optimization, and machine learning to solve challenging business problems. They are comfortable working with large, imperfect datasets and can translate ambiguous questions into scalable analytical products that drive business outcomes.

This person thrives at the intersection of technology, analytics, and strategy. They possess strong Python and SQL capabilities, understand modern cloud-based analytics environments, and can communicate technical concepts clearly to non-technical audiences. They are naturally curious, highly collaborative, and motivated by building repeatable solutions that improve decision-making, accelerate insights, and unlock value across the enterprise. Experience in retail, consumer products, planning, merchandising, or finance environments is preferred.


5+ years of experience in advanced analytics, data science, business analytics, Business Intelligence, or related technical roles.
Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, Business Analytics, or related field. Will accept any suitable combination of education, experience and training.
Advanced proficiency in SQL, Python, statistical modeling, forecasting, optimization, and machine learning techniques.
Experience with Databricks, Snowflake, modern cloud analytics platforms, and visualization tools such as Sigma, Tableau, or similar solutions.
Practical experience applying Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Generative Artificial Intelligence, Large Language Model-based workflows, agentic systems, or Retrieval-Augmented Generation capabilities.


What You’ll Work On

You will build advanced analytics capabilities that help Converse leaders understand performance, anticipate opportunities and risks, and make more confident business decisions. Through data science, forecasting, and analytical product development, you will transform complex data into scalable solutions that accelerate insight and action across the enterprise.


Develop, maintain, and evolve data science and advanced analytics capabilities that support Planning, Merchandising, Finance, and enterprise business partners.
Access, transform, and analyze data using SQL, Python, and modern analytics platforms to uncover actionable business insights.
Design and deploy forecasting, predictive modeling, optimization, and machine learning solutions that improve planning accuracy and business performance.
Build reusable analytical workflows, models, and data products that increase automation, consistency, scalability, and speed to insight.
Partner closely with business stakeholders to translate analytical findings into clear recommendations that drive growth, profitability, inventory productivity, and consumer outcomes.
Collaborate with data engineering, analytics, and technology teams to improve data quality, governance, accessibility, and usability across the modern data ecosystem.
Advance data literacy and strengthen the data science community by sharing knowledge, promoting best practices, and exploring emerging Artificial Intelligence capabilities that responsibly scale decision support and analytical impact.


We offer a number of accommodations to complete our interview process including screen readers, sign language interpreters, accessible and single location for in-person interviews, closed captioning, and other reasonable modifications as needed. If you discover, as you navigate our application process, that you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, please complete the Candidate Accommodation Request Form.

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

Title: Intern, Business Intelligence & Operations - Fall Semester 2026
Description:
Boston Celtics Job Posting

Job Title: Business Intelligence & Operations Intern

Department: Business Intelligence & Operations

Supervisor: VP, Business Strategy and Operations

Job Classification: Non-Exempt

Job Type: Part-Time, 29 Hours per Week (September - December)

Summary

The mission of the Boston Celtics internship program is to educate its participants in all aspects of operating a professional sports organization. Through observation and practical experience, interns learn how to function in a professional environment that requires a high energy level, the ability to perform under pressure and the utilization of essential time management skills. Through these developments, the Boston Celtics strive to successfully prepare their interns to compete for positions in the professional sports industry.

Responsibilities


Assist in the development of SQL scripts used to build Tableau server data sources and validate data accuracy
Build and validate data visualizations in Tableau, summarizing data in a way that is straightforward and informative
Assist with data accuracy audits in CRM system, including day-to-day maintenance of data
Update and analyze leads in email marketing database
Assist in tagging and categorization of social media posts
Assist Ticket Operations in a variety of Archtics related ticketing projects such as pricing structure, data maintenance, financial monitoring, and third-party integrations
Process and report on ticket donations for Shamrock Dreams program to Community Engagement
Field inbound fan questions related to tickets and troubleshoot technical issues
Support various fan engagement programs and solving in-person ticketing issues at the TD Garden Box Office during home games
Research and present best practices relating to business analytics and operations in the sports industry
Other collaborative duties as assigned by the Director of Business Intelligence


Qualifications


Strong critical thinking, a growth mindset, an deductive problem-solving
Detail-oriented and thoughtful approaches to ensure accuracy and understanding of projects
Interest in developing ideas and projects from the ground up and being a self-starter
Enthusiasm to understand data, database management, and reporting and analytics
Ability to communicate and work with multiple departments and senior management


Learning Opportunities


How to build data visualizations in Tableau, including how to tell stories and how to use data to drive insights to key stakeholders
Writing effective and efficient SQL scripts used to pull, validate, and manipulate data
A macro-level understanding of the role data plays in an organization, including how data flows out of and into systems used for ticketing, CRM, and email marketing
The ins and outs of a CRM system that manages hundreds of thousands of customer records
Foundation training and regular use of Ticketmaster’s Archtics ticketing system, the most commonly used ticketing system in professional sports


Requirements


Must be a junior or senior undergraduate student or graduate student
Academic Credit
Ability to work scheduled home games and events


The expected range of annual salary/hourly rate and potential commission, if applicable, is included below. Where an employee or prospective employee is paid within this/these range(s) will depend on, among other factors, actual ranges for current/former employees in the subject position, market and industry considerations, budgetary considerations, tenure, performance, and standing with the company (applicable to current employees), as well as the employee’s/applicant’s background, pertinent experience, and qualifications.

Hourly Rate Range: $15.00

Banner Seventeen, LLC is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. All employees and applicants for employment are afforded equal opportunity in every area of hiring and employment without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, pregnancy (and pregnancy related conditions), gender identity or expression, citizenship status, national origin, ancestry, sexual orientation, age, physical or mental disability, genetic disposition or carrier status, marital status, familial status, status as a victim of domestic violence, military or veteran status, or any other category protected under applicable federal, state, or local law.

About Company

Throughout the Boston Celtics’ storied history, they have long stood for equality and respect, including drafting the first African American player, hiring the first African American coach, and playing the first all-black starting five. The Celtics have won a record 18 NBA Championships spanning six decades, including eight in a row from 1959-1966, and winning their most recent in 2024. In addition, 51 former Celtics players, management, coaches, or staff have been inducted into the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame. The team has been operating under local ownership since 2002.

Title: Data & Product Analyst (Benchmarking), CoStar Debt Solutions
Description:
Job Description

Data & Product Analyst (Benchmarking)

Location: Boston, in-office

About The Role

CoStar Debt Solutions is building the next generation of CRE analytics software — a platform where data, benchmarking intelligence, and client workflows come together in a single experience to support how lenders originate, evaluate, and manage risk.

A central part of this vision is our Benchmarking product, which enables lenders to compare loan performance, pricing, and risk characteristics against a trusted market dataset. This product is rapidly evolving, with significant investment in expanding coverage, improving methodologies, and delivering more actionable insights directly into client workflows.

We’re hiring a Data & Product Analyst to help drive the continued growth of this Benchmarking product — both as a data asset and as a highly client-facing solution.

This role combines data analysis, product development, and direct client engagement, with a strong emphasis on supporting client adoption and enabling the sales process. You will work closely with clients and internal sales teams to demonstrate product value, answer analytical questions, and ensure clients can effectively leverage benchmarking insights in their workflows.

In year one, this role will focus on analyzing benchmark data, supporting product enhancements, and actively participating in client meetings, product demos, and sales support activities. Over time, the role expands into shaping benchmark methodologies, influencing product strategy, and helping scale how benchmarking insights are delivered across the platform.

This is a hybrid role at the intersection of data analysis, product development, and client/sales engagement — ideal for someone who can translate complex datasets into clear insights and communicate them effectively to both internal and external audiences.

What You’ll Own


The analysis of complex datasets that power the Benchmarking product — identifying trends, inconsistencies, and opportunities to improve data coverage and quality
The evolution of benchmark methodologies, outputs, and usability in partnership with Product Managers and cross-functional teams
Direct engagement with clients, including leading and supporting client conversations to understand benchmarking use cases, answer analytical questions, and gather actionable feedback
Active support of sales and go-to-market efforts, including participating in product demonstrations, responding to client inquiries, and helping articulate the value of benchmarking insights
Ongoing client support and enablement, ensuring users can interpret and apply benchmark data effectively in their workflows
Collaboration with engineering teams to ensure accurate delivery of benchmark data, calculations, and features
Monitoring product usage and client feedback to identify areas for improvement and drive continuous enhancements
Data validation, quality checks, and troubleshooting to maintain the reliability and integrity of benchmark datasets
Staying current on industry trends and competitive benchmarking solutions to inform product direction


What We Expect You To Bring


Bachelor’s degree from an accredited, not-for-profit, in person, university or college. Ideally in Data Analytics, Data Science, Business Analytics, Finance, Economics, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field
2+ years of experience working with data analytics, financial data, or data-driven products
Strong hands-on experience working with large datasets and performing detailed analysis
Strong communication skills, with the ability to engage effectively with both technical stakeholders and clients
Proven ability to support client-facing interactions, including presenting, explaining analytical concepts, and responding to client questions in real time
Experience with analytics or benchmarking products, or similar data-driven solutions
Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and problem-solving skills
A track record of commitment to prior employers


Nice To Have


Exposure to SQL, Python, or similar languages
Experience with benchmarking, peer comparison, or performance analytics solutions
Experience supporting client-facing products or working in a customer-oriented environment
Familiarity with financial services, lending, or real estate data
Experience with data visualization tools
Understanding of APIs and data integration concepts


The Growth Path

The first year in this role is focused on deeply understanding the benchmark dataset, client needs, and current product capabilities — contributing directly to data analysis, active client engagement, sales support, and incremental product improvements. You will play a key role in client conversations, product demonstrations, and helping drive adoption of benchmarking insights in real-world workflows.

Beyond that, the role expands into a more strategic position:


Shaping how benchmark methodologies evolve as the dataset scales
Driving enhancements to how insights are surfaced within client workflows
Partnering with sales and client teams to improve how benchmarking is positioned, adopted, and delivered
Influencing product direction based on client usage patterns, feedback, and market trends


This role is a strong path into broader product ownership, client-facing product strategy, or advanced analytics leadership, particularly for someone interested in building and scaling data-driven products that directly support client outcomes and business growth.

What’s Unique About This Role

Most data analyst roles focus on generating insights. This role goes further — connecting those insights directly to product decisions and client outcomes.

You’ll Not Only Analyze Data, But Also


Work directly with clients and sales teams to translate insights into adoption and business value
Play a key role in client-facing discussions that shape both product direction and commercial success
Help bridge the gap between data, product, and revenue-generating activities


What’s in it for you?

When you join CoStar Group, you’ll experience a collaborative and innovative culture working alongside the best and brightest to empower our people and customers to succeed. We offer you generous compensation and performance-based incentives. CoStar Group also invests in your professional and academic growth with internal training, and tuition reimbursement.

Our Benefits Package Includes (but Is Not Limited To)


Comprehensive healthcare coverage: Medical / Vision / Dental / Prescription Drug
Life, legal, and supplementary insurance
Virtual and in person mental health counseling services for individuals and family
Commuter and parking benefits
401(K) retirement plan with matching contributions
Employee stock purchase plan
Paid time off
Tuition reimbursement
On-site fitness center and/or reimbursed fitness center membership costs (location dependent)
Access to CoStar Group’s Employee Resource Groups
Complimentary gourmet coffee, tea, hot chocolate, fresh fruit, and other healthy snacks


This position offers an annual base salary range of $99,000 - $133,000, based on relevant skills and experience and includes a generous benefits plan

We welcome all qualified candidates who are currently eligible to work full-time in the United States to apply. However, please note that CoStar Group is not able to provide visa sponsorship for this position.

CoStar Group is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer; we maintain a drug-free workplace and perform pre-employment substance abuse testing

Title: Data Analyst II
Description:
Site: The General Hospital Corporation

Mass General Brigham relies on a wide range of professionals, including doctors, nurses, business people, tech experts, researchers, and systems analysts to advance our mission. As a not-for-profit, we support patient care, research, teaching, and community service, striving to provide exceptional care. We believe that high-performing teams drive groundbreaking medical discoveries and invite all applicants to join us and experience what it means to be part of Mass General Brigham.

Summary

Job Summary

Responsible for extracting knowledge and insights from data in order to investigate business/operational problems through a range of data preparation, modeling, analysis, and/or visualization techniques.

Does this position require Patient Care?

No

Essential Functions


Collects, analyzes, and reports data to support decisions on day-to-day operations, strategic planning, and/or specific business performance issues.
Collates, models, interprets, and analyzes data.
Explains variances and trends in data.
Identifies and documents enhancements to modeling techniques.
Completes thorough quality assurance procedures, ensuring accuracy, reliability, trustworthiness, and validity of work.
Performs data validation of source-to-target data for data visuals and dashboards.


Qualifications

Education

Bachelor's Degree Analysis required or Bachelor's Degree Related Field of Study required

Can this role accept experience in lieu of a degree?

Yes

Licenses and Credentials

Experience

Experience working with data, preferably healthcare data 2-3 years required

Knowledge, Skills And Abilities


Healthcare knowledge, particularly as it pertains to electronic medical record data, is preferred but not required.
Working knowledge of relational databases, SQL, data visualization, and Business Intelligence tools such as Tableau.
Knowledge and application of statistical analyses, including variance analysis and statistical significance, are preferred.
Project management skills and/or experience are a plus.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite, including Word, Excel and PowerPoint.


Additional Job Details (if Applicable)


When applying, please include a cover letter and transcript (unofficial is ok) along with your resume.**


For more information regarding our group, please visit http://www.massgeneral.org/mpec/.

The Medical Practice Evaluation Center (MPEC) is seeking a Database Design and Management Coordinator to work with an internationally recognized multidisciplinary team from Massachusetts General Hospital dedicated to improving clinical outcomes, increasing value in health policy, and informing global public health through innovative research, collaboration, and education. The hire will work with a dynamic, results-oriented, and diverse group of researchers from Massachusetts General Hospital, Harvard Medical School, and collaborators at multiple international institutions.

The Database Design And Management Coordinator Will Work With Faculty And Project Managers In The Epidemiology And Implementation Program At MPEC. The Epidemiology And Implementation Program Works To Develop, Implement, And Evaluate Interventions To Improve Engagement In And Quality Of Medical Care In The United States And International Settings. Example Projects Include


Determining the effects of HIV on aging and dementia in Uganda through a prospective longitudinal cohort study with comprehensive clinical, cognitive, and radiologic assessments
Elucidating the contributions of immunosuppression, vaccination, and therapies on the epidemiology and transmission of viral respiratory infections
Investigating the role of maternal HIV infection and co-infection with early childhood infections
Studying service delivery mechanisms for STI screening and prevention at the MGH Sexual Health Clinic in partnership with community organizations in the Boston area
Working with database managers in multiple global environments on database development, capacity building, and training


The candidate should be highly motivated with experience in database design and data management as well as clinical and public health research. The position offers a stimulating, collaborative, and multidisciplinary environment and the opportunity to contribute to NIH-funded research. The Medical Practice Evaluation Center (MPEC) is seeking a Database Design and Management Coordinator to work with an internationally recognized multidisciplinary team from Massachusetts General Hospital dedicated to improving clinical outcomes, increasing value in health policy, and informing global public health through innovative research, collaboration, and education. The hire will work with a dynamic, results-oriented, and diverse group of researchers from Massachusetts General Hospital, Harvard Medical School, and collaborators at multiple international institutions.

The Database Design And Management Coordinator Will Work With Faculty And Project Managers In The Epidemiology And Implementation Program At MPEC. The Epidemiology And Implementation Program Works To Develop, Implement, And Evaluate Interventions To Improve Engagement In And Quality Of Medical Care In The United States And International Settings.   • Determining the effects of HIV on aging and dementia in Uganda through a prospective longitudinal cohort study with comprehensive clinical, cognitive, and radiologic assessments

Title: Analyst Reporting & Analysis
Description:
Category/Area of Expertise: Data-Science & Analytics

Job Requisition: 535634

Address: USA-MA-Quincy-1385 Hancock Street

Store Code: ADUSA Dist L&D-Executive (5156965)

ADUSA Distribution is the distribution company of Ahold Delhaize USA, providing distribution services to one of the largest grocery retail supply chains in the nation. ADUSA Distribution fosters a culture that inspires and empowers our associates to always deliver exceptional service and value. Our team is supporting the supply chain network evolution to an integrated self-distribution model of the future, serving leading omnichannel grocery brands - Food Lion, Giant Food, The GIANT Company, Hannaford and Stop & Shop. To learn more about our company and the other supply chain companies of Ahold Delhaize USA, visit www.adusasc.com.

What You'll Do (Role Overview)

As an Analyst III, Reporting and Analysis, you'll play a critical role in shaping how data drives decisions across our Distribution & Transportation network.

In this role, you'll go beyond building reports you'll unlock insights, influence strategy, and help leaders understand what's happening across the business and what to do next.

You'll work with large, complex data sets, translate them into meaningful stories, and provide clear recommendations that help drive smarter, faster decisions.

Why This Role Matters

Data is at the heart of how we operate and this role helps ensure we're using it to its fullest potential.

As a trusted partner to leadership, you will:


Turn data into actionable insights that drive performance
Support key initiatives across Distribution, Transportation, and Supply Chain
Help improve the tools, reporting, and analytics that guide our business forward


Your work will directly impact operational efficiency, strategic priorities, and overall business performance.

What You'll Be Responsible For

Turn Data into Insights


Analyze complex data sets to uncover trends, risks, and opportunities
Connect insights to broader Distribution & Transportation and Supply Chain strategies
Provide recommendations that help leaders make confident, data-driven decisions


Build & Enhance Reporting


Design, create, and maintain reporting that is accurate, scalable, and user-friendly
Deliver both recurring and ad hoc analysis based on evolving business needs
Ensure stakeholders understand and can effectively use reporting tools and outputs


Partner Across the Business


Collaborate closely with Distribution & Transportation leadership, Supply Chain teams, IT, and Data Governance
Gather and refine reporting requirements to deliver meaningful, impactful solutions
Serve as a key bridge between data and the business ensuring alignment and clarity


Solve Complex Problems


Perform advanced analysis to uncover deeper insights
Use business modeling to evaluate potential outcomes and guide decision-making
Proactively identify opportunities to improve processes, performance, and efficiency


Tell the Story Behind the Data


Transform large, complex data sets into clear, compelling narratives
Develop presentation-ready materials for leadership reviews and strategic discussions
Help leaders understand not just the data but the story, impact, and next steps


Drive Innovation & Continuous Improvement


Stay current on analytics tools and emerging best practices
Expand capabilities in reporting, visualization, and modeling
Support a culture of data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement


Be a Key Contributor


Provide subject matter expertise on analytics tools and reporting capabilities
Support key initiatives, projects, and cross-functional teams
Help champion change by using data to guide growth and transformation


What You Bring

We're looking for someone who is curious, analytical, and confident working with both data and people someone who enjoys solving problems and influencing outcomes.

Key Skills & Strengths


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with experience working with large data sets
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (Power Query, Power Pivots, etc.)
Experience with data visualization and analytics tools (Power BI, Alteryx, etc.)
Experience creating calculated expressions (DAX, Calculate fields, Excel Formulas, etc.)
Ability to query using SQL in Databricks
Knowledge of Power Automate and Power Apps
Ability to translate complex analysis into clear, actionable recommendations
Strong storytelling and presentation skills
Knowledge of supply chain, inventory, forecasting, or distribution operations (preferred)
Strong organizational and time management abilities
Ability to collaborate, influence, and build relationships across teams
Creative, innovative thinker with a proactive mindset
Ability to navigate change and drive continuous improvement


Education & Experience


Bachelor's degree in business, economics, engineering, math, statistics, or a related field
Analytics certifications are a plus
3-5 years of experience in analytics, reporting, or a data-driven environment


What Makes This Opportunity Exciting


You'll work directly with leadership and influence real business decisions
You'll turn data into insights that drive measurable impact across the network
You'll gain exposure to multiple areas of the business and expand your influence
You'll help shape how analytics supports the future of Distribution & Transportation
You'll be part of a team that values innovation, collaboration, and continuous improvement


Let's Build What's Next Together

If you're passionate about using data to tell stories, solve problems, and drive meaningful change, this is your opportunity to make a lasting impact.

Salary


$75,040-112,560, depending on experience.


We are an organization that values diversity and encourages all qualified individuals to apply without regard to sex, race, color, ancestry, national origin, citizenship status, religion, age, marital status (including civil unions), military service, veteran status, pregnancy (including childbirth and related medical conditions), genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, legally recognized disability, domestic violence victim status or any other characteristic protected by law.

Our organization is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities, including applicants. If you have a disability and require assistance in the application process, please email recruiting@adusasc.com
