Title: Modeling Analyst II
Description:
Join the Market Leader in Electric Power Data and Analytics Solutions

The electrical grid is the largest and most complicated machine ever built. Yes Energy’s industry-leading electric power trading analytics software provides real-time visibility into the massive amount of data generated by the North American electrical grid daily. Our unique and innovative view of the data informs real-time trading decisions and mid-to-long-term investment decisions that keep utility prices low, support the energy transition, and keep the grid running. It’s both challenging work and work with a purpose.

Be a part of our successful, growing business during international transformation.

Position Summary

As a Modeling Analyst II, you will be the subject matter expert on fundamental market price forecasting within our EnCompass product line. This role plays a critical part in ensuring that EnCompass meets the needs of our customers by providing advanced customer support to current and future customers, supporting product development and documentation, and testing and updating market databases for North America. The ideal candidate will have deep expertise in power system modeling including production costing, market price formation, and economic transmission analysis.

This position requires close collaboration with Product Management, Engineering, and Sales to ensure the success of our customers, while also providing customer-facing expertise including day to day customer support, onboarding and training.

Position Details


Salary range: $110,000 - $140,000 plus bonus
Full-time
Hybrid or Remote
Reporting to: Senior Director, Customer Success
Travel requirement: Up to 30%


Primary Responsibilities


Ensure customers know how to use our products to their fullest potential by providing exceptional customer service, answering questions, troubleshooting issues, and providing technical support.
Engage existing and new customers in online and in-person training and onboarding - utilizing presentation skills, industry knowledge, and Yes Energy’s product expertise to maximize our customers’ product use experience.
Update and create product documentation, tutorials, and related materials.
Work closely with Product Management and Engineering to facilitate communication about product issues, user experience and to implement and test new EnCompass features.
Assist Power Infrastructure and partner companies in providing robust EnCompass databases for the North American power markets.
Provide subject matter expertise to Sales and Marketing to develop campaigns and participate in industry events.


Minimum Qualifications


Minimum of three years of experience in power system modeling, including market price forecasting, production costing, economic transmission analysis, and/or resource planning.
Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Mathematics, Economics, or a related analytical field.
Experience using Excel or BI tools to analyze data.
Excellent communication skills.
Ability to troubleshoot complex modeling errors and guide customers through resolution.
Ability to travel to customer sites to provide in person training, up to 2 times per month.


Preferred Qualifications


Direct experience with EnCompass, PLEXOS, Aurora, or PROMOD models.
Familiarity with FERC regulations, ISO/RTO operations, and resource adequacy constructs.
Prior experience in a client-facing or consultative role, particularly within the energy sector.


At Yes Energy, we value connecting directly with candidates. We kindly ask that third-party recruiters and agencies not submit resumes, as we are not open to external recruiting partnerships.

Overview

ABOUT YES ENERGY

Yes Energy delivers real-time market data and electric power trading decision solutions. Over 1,000 market participants use Yes Energy solutions daily. The business is a leader in all aspects of information content collection and management, developing and delivering data and market analytics solutions. Since its inception in 2008, Yes Energy has become a trusted and respected supplier of innovative and reliable solutions focused on the needs of power market analysts, traders, and trade managers. Yes Energy has a team of over 350 amazing professionals in Boulder, CO (HQ); Boston, MA; Chicago, IL; Glendora, CA; Richmond, VA; London, United Kingdom; Auckland, New Zealand, Tokyo, Japan; and Bucharest, Romania.

Culture

Yes Energy has been named one of the Best Places to Work in Colorado, and we have the culture to prove it. At Yes Energy, we care about saying “Yes” to customers. We like to listen, learn, and develop our solutions in line with their needs. We think about customers as business partners, and when we help them be more successful … we are more successful, too.

Around the office, our culture is driven by some pretty fundamental values that we’re proud of:


We love innovation and solving tough challenges;
We are “high standards people” who combine passion and pride with hard work and rewards of all kinds-- in an ethic that is consistent across the company;
We’re team-focused with a flat hierarchy-- we work in small teams on well-defined projects that directly impact the success of the business;
We play to the strengths and experience of each person while each of us also works along a continuum of roles adjacent to our focus area. This presents the challenge of maintaining a broad set of skills as well as an opportunity to learn and contribute in many ways;
We are constantly growing. Professional development happens every day and every year.


Compensation And Benefits

We offer highly competitive salaries and real bonuses that are achievable and that you can impact. Our benefits package is also very competitive, including medical insurance, a 401 (k) Plan with matching, flexible vacation, and flexible work schedules. Yes Energy encourages and funds investment in both formal and informal professional development.

At Yes Energy, we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you’re excited about this role but your experience doesn’t perfectly align with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.

In accordance with Colorado law, the range provided is Yes Energy’s reasonable estimate of the base compensation for this role. The actual amount may be higher or lower based on non-discriminatory factors such as location, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities.

Yes Energy provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or genetics. In addition to federal law requirements, Yes Energy complies with applicable state and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment in every location where the company has facilities. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training.

Title: Virtual Assistant
Description:
Company Description 247Digitize is a US-based outsourcing and technology partner that helps organizations streamline non-core business processes so internal teams can focus on productivity and growth. With offices in India and Colombia, the company operates through an international team of specialists experienced in optimizing and executing diverse operational workflows. 247Digitize supports clients across industries with services such as data management, bookkeeping, and legal support. Team members benefit from exposure to global clients, structured processes, and a culture focused on efficiency, quality, and continuous improvement.
Role Description The Virtual Assistant role at 247Digitize is a full-time remote position responsible for supporting clients with a range of administrative and operational tasks. Day-to-day responsibilities may include managing calendars and schedules, organizing emails and documentation, preparing and updating spreadsheets or reports, and assisting with data entry and data management activities. The role may also involve basic bookkeeping support, coordinating meetings, maintaining task lists, and following established procedures to ensure accuracy and timely delivery. The Virtual Assistant collaborates with internal teams and client contacts, communicates clearly and professionally, and uses digital tools and platforms to manage workload and meet service-level expectations.
Qualifications

Candidates should possess strong administrative and organizational skills, including calendar management, email handling, document organization, and task prioritization.
Candidates should possess data-related skills such as accurate data entry, spreadsheet proficiency (e.g., Excel or Google Sheets), and basic data management and record-keeping abilities.
Candidates should possess basic finance and bookkeeping skills, such as handling invoices, tracking expenses, and supporting routine financial documentation under guidance.
Candidates should possess clear written and verbal communication skills, client-service orientation, and the ability to communicate across international teams and time zones.
Candidates should possess proficiency with common office and collaboration tools (e.g., Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, online communication platforms) and the ability to learn new systems quickly.
Candidates should possess strong attention to detail, reliability, time management skills, and the ability to work independently in a remote environment.
Relevant experience in virtual assistance, administrative support, outsourcing, or business process operations is beneficial; prior exposure to data management, bookkeeping, or legal support environments is a plus.
A high school diploma or equivalent is required; a college degree or relevant certification in business administration, accounting, or related fields is preferred.

  • Candidates should possess proficiency with common office and collaboration tools (e.g., Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, online communication platforms) and the ability to learn new systems quickly.
  • Candidates should possess strong attention to detail, reliability, time management skills, and the ability to work independently in a remote environment.
  • Relevant experience in virtual assistance, administrative support, outsourcing, or business process operations is beneficial; prior exposure to data management, bookkeeping, or legal support environments is a plus.
  • A high school diploma or equivalent is required; a college degree or relevant certification in business administration, accounting, or related fields is preferred.

Title: Data Analyst (Remote)
Description:
Role: Data Analyst (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Job Type: Contract
Payout: $80–$120 USD per hour




Role Overview:

We are hiring expert evaluators in data analysis / quantitative readouts to review and assess AI-generated work products (documents, spreadsheets, and slide decks) for accuracy, rigor, and domain quality. This is a remote, hourly contract engagement requiring deep subject-matter expertise.




Key Responsibilities:

• Evaluate AI-generated artifacts against domain-specific quality rubrics.

• Identify factual, aesthetic, and presentation errors in documents, spreadsheets, and slide decks.

• Apply professional standards to assess rigor and relevance of quantitative outputs.

• Provide clear, structured written feedback to improve AI-generated content.

• Ensure outputs meet professional presentation and accuracy benchmarks.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• 5+ years of relevant professional experience in data analysis or quantitative readouts.

• Native or professional fluency in English.

• Highly proficient in Microsoft Office and Google Workspace, especially slides (Google Slides / PowerPoint).

• Experience evaluating technical or data-driven documents for correctness and clarity.

• Ability to assess quantitative outputs with attention to detail and domain context.




More About the Opportunity:

This role offers a unique opportunity to contribute to the quality assurance of AI-generated outputs for a global leader in technology and data services. Your evaluations will directly inform improvements in automated content generation.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.

Title: Virtual Assistant
Description:
About The Company

Decentralized Masters is a pioneering leader in DeFi education with a global footprint. Established just two years ago, the company has rapidly expanded from a small team of co-founders to over 150 dedicated professionals committed to transforming the DeFi landscape. Recognized as one of the fastest-growing enterprises in the sector, Decentralized Masters is on a trajectory to become a unicorn company by 2030. Operating on a bootstrapped model, the company is achieving remarkable financial milestones, with an anticipated revenue of $50 million this year. The organization’s core mission is to empower investors and enthusiasts with comprehensive, accessible DeFi education, fostering a community that is well-equipped for long-term success in the evolving Web3 ecosystem.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly organized and proactive Virtual Assistant to join our dynamic team. This role offers an exciting opportunity to support our operational functions, enhance customer engagement, and contribute to the growth of our DeFi educational platform. The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing communications, assisting with customer queries, supporting onboarding processes, and coordinating various administrative tasks. This position is fully remote and requires someone who is detail-oriented, possesses excellent communication skills, and is passionate about the Web3 space. As a vital member of our team, you will help ensure smooth daily operations, facilitate webinars and live sessions, and support project management activities related to content and member management.

Qualifications


Resume submitted in English
Native English speaker or professional level proficiency in English
Must be located in South America
Proven experience as a Virtual Assistant or in a relevant administrative/support role
Customer service experience required
Familiarity with DeFi or cryptocurrency is an advantage
Excellent skills in word processing and spreadsheet management (Google Suite, MS Excel)
Previous experience working with Circle platform is a plus
Knowledge of online calendar management (Google Calendar)
Strong communication skills via phone, email, and instant messaging
Exceptional time management and organizational skills
High school diploma; additional qualifications as an Administrator or Executive Assistant are desirable


Responsibilities


Manage and respond to emails and other communication channels efficiently
Handle escalated customer queries with professionalism and promptness
Assist with refund requests, documentation, and related processes
Draft and prepare reports, presentations, and other documentation as needed
Maintain clear and consistent communication with clients and team members
Perform general administrative duties to support daily operations
Support customer onboarding processes to ensure a smooth experience
Organize webinars, coordinate Zoom calls, and assist during live sessions
Support project managers with content uploads, access management, and member gating on Circle
Update and maintain Excel dashboards and tracking sheets for operational insights


Benefits


Competitive salary package
Full-time employment with stability and growth opportunities
Unlimited paid time off to promote work-life balance
Participation in team off-site events and retreats
Dynamic, young culture with team members across more than 30 countries
Fully remote work environment, enabling flexibility to work from anywhere in the world
Collaborative and supportive team environment fostering professional development


Equal Opportunity

Decentralized Masters is committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, age, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic. We believe diverse perspectives and backgrounds strengthen our team and are dedicated to fostering a workplace where everyone can thrive.

Title: Temporary Data Request Analyst
Description:
Uncommon Schools is a nonprofit network of high-performing public charter schools dedicated to providing an exceptional K-12 education in economically disadvantaged communities. Operating in Boston, Camden, New York City, Newark, and Rochester, we are committed to closing the college completion gap and preparing every student for college and beyond. Our students consistently exceed national benchmarks, reflecting our dedication to their success. We invest deeply in our educators, offering weekly coaching, feedback, and professional development to ensure excellence in teaching and leadership. Join us to work hard, do good, and make a lasting difference!





Job Description



You’ll play a key role in ensuring the right data reaches the right people at the right time across Uncommon. In this role, you will manage and fulfill data requests, support critical data processes, and help teams make informed decisions that directly impact students and schools. You’ll collaborate with colleagues across Development, Talent, and academic teams, turning data into clear, usable insights. Your work will support the reporting and analysis that helps advance our mission of preparing students to graduate from college and achieve their dreams.



This is a temporary position starting as soon as possible, with an expected end date of June 30, 2027.



Data Processing & Reporting



Prepare, clean, and organize data for reporting and analysis across multiple systems
Configure, test, and execute data processes, including survey and assessment-related data
Build and refine reports that provide key information to stakeholders
Monitor data quality and help troubleshoot issues to support accurate reporting

Data Requests & Stakeholder Support



Fulfill and track data requests from teams across the organization for internal and external use
Partner with teams that submit recurring requests to anticipate needs and plan ahead
Respond to questions and support colleagues in accessing and understanding data
Assist with analyses requested by leadership and Home Office teams

Process Improvement & Documentation



Document processes, reports, and workflows to support consistency and quality
Contribute to and maintain a knowledge base of key datasets and reporting practices
Identify opportunities to improve processes and tools in collaboration with the Data & IT team

Team & Project Support



Support project planning to ensure timely and accurate delivery of data work
Assist with team coordination, including organizing documentation and scheduling
Continue developing data skills to contribute to broader reporting and analysis work


Qualifications



Required



Bachelor’s degree
1 to 2 years of professional experience, including experience working with data for reporting or analysis
Proficiency in SQL
Experience cleaning and preparing data using spreadsheets or a coding language
Strong attention to detail and ability to ensure data accuracy
Clear written and verbal communication skills
Ability to manage multiple priorities and follow through on deadlines

Preferred



Experience with Python or R
Familiarity with Tableau or similar tools
Experience using a ticketing or support system (e.g., Zendesk)
Coursework or experience in statistics
Experience working with education-related data

Additional Information



This role primarily involves working at a desk for extended periods

Not sure you meet every qualification? We encourage you to apply—we’re excited to learn what you bring.





Additional Information



Our people are what make us Uncommon. We offer competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits that support the personal health, wellness, and finances of our staff and their families. Our compensation philosophy values equity & fairness and attracting & retaining top talent.



Compensation



The full compensation range for this role is $67,300-$79,200. Typically, new hires who fully meet all qualifications listed in the job description will start at $71,300. Starting compensation below this reflects candidates with less experience or qualifications. Offers near the top end of the range are reserved for candidates who significantly exceed the qualifications, bringing extensive experience and specialized expertise beyond what is listed.



Compensation determinations consider relevant professional experience and tenure with Uncommon Schools. Additionally, because we value internal growth and tenure, current staff members are not subject to salary caps within this range.



Work Environment:

Uncommon is an in-person organization, with a commitment of at least three days each week at our Central Office in downtown Manhattan (100 Church Street).



Position Type:

This is a temporary role with an anticipated end date of June 30, 2027.





Benefit Highlights:



19 days of paid time off
3 weeks of paid Winter and Summer org-wide holidays
Comprehensive Health, Dental, and Vision insurance plans
403(b) retirement savings program + employer match
Paid leave of absence options (parental, medical, disability, etc.)
Mental health and counseling support + wellness benefits
Pre-tax flexible spending, dependent care, and health saving accounts



*A detailed list of all benefits is located HERE.





At Uncommon, we believe that a supportive and welcoming environment helps our students, staff, and community thrive. We are dedicated to building a team that brings a wide range of perspectives and experiences, reflective of the communities we serve---with over 70% of our staff identifying as people of color. Our organization values respect and open dialogue on important topics that shape our school culture.



As an equal opportunity employer, Uncommon provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws. We also ensure that individuals with disabilities receive all privileges and benefits associated with employment and are provided reasonable accommodations for the interview process and to perform core job functions. If you would like to request an accommodation, please email recruitment@uncommonschools.org.

Title: Remote File Clerk
Description:
1. About Our Client:

This organization provides innovative hydration solutions aimed at health-conscious consumers. It focuses on quality, sustainability, and supporting active lifestyles.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Remote File Clerk role involves managing and maintaining digital filing systems to ensure company documents are organized, secure, and accessible. This position supports operational efficiency and assists the broader team by maintaining accurate records and providing administrative support.




3. Responsibilities:

• Organize and maintain digital files and company documents within the document management system.

• Ensure accurate and timely categorization and labeling of electronic records.

• Retrieve and distribute files and information to staff upon request.

• Adhere to policies for data confidentiality, record-keeping, and security.

• Perform regular audits of the filing system to maintain accuracy and integrity.

• Assist with archiving old records and managing document lifecycles according to guidelines.

• Provide general administrative support as required.




4. Requirements:

• Experience in roles such as File Clerk, Data Entry Clerk, or Administrative Assistant.

• Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and familiarity with digital filing software like SharePoint or Google Drive.

• Strong organizational and time-management skills.

• Attention to detail and high accuracy.

• Ability to work independently in a remote environment.

• Strong written and verbal communication.

• Professional and discreet handling of confidential information.

• Minimum of a high school diploma or equivalent.




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Competitive hourly rate of $30 - $35 USD per hour.




Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
About Loop

Loop is industrializing services through AI, starting with one of the world's most complex industries: the global supply chain. We're an AI-native company built to solve the systemic data problem that's held logistics back for decades. Our Logistics Data Platform turns fragmented, unstructured data into a trusted foundation that fuels automation, financial clarity, and decision intelligence at scale. Today, Loop serves >20% of the Fortune 100, manages over $30B in supply chain spend, and helps its clients save hundreds of millions of dollars each year.

We recently closed a $95M Series C and are backed by leading investors including Valor Equity Partners, Founders Fund, 8VC, Index Ventures, J.P. Morgan, and more than 50 industry-leading angel investors.

Our team brings deep expertise from Uber, Google, Microsoft, Meta, Amazon, Flexport, C3 AI, VMware, Box, UPS, and C.H. Robinson and we're looking for builders who want to help shape what comes next.

About The Role

As an Analytics Engineer at Loop, you will play a pivotal role in maturing the data organization. You’ll work cross-functionally across Engineering, Product, Design, Strategy & Operations, and AI Platform teams to design, build, and own the core infrastructure and data models. These systems will enable both internal and client facing analytics, accelerating data-driven decision-making throughout the company and our clients.

This is an in-person role based in our Chicago office with the expectation of being onsite 4+ days per week.

What You’ll Do


Own Core Data Models and ETL Pipelines: Design, build, and maintain Loop’s core data models and ETL processes.
Define Metrics and Governance: Establish and govern key business metrics for consistency across teams.
Maintain Data Quality and Uptime: Ensure data accuracy and reliability with strong SLAs.
Automate Data Requests: Convert ad-hoc data requests into scalable, repeatable pipelines.
Optimize Data Infrastructure: Manage and optimize data infrastructure for performance, cost, and compliance.
Develop Data Products: Create data-driven solutions and embedded analytics to support business and product teams.


Qualifications


2+ years experience building analytical products (system, dashboard, models).
Strong proficiency in SQL and Python for data transformations and automation.
Hands-on experience with modern data stack tools (e.g., Snowflake, dbt, Dagster, Airflow, Looker).
Excellent business communication skills to translate data insights into actionable business strategies.
Experience with data dashboarding and visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Superset, Mode).
Ability to manage, optimize, and scale data infrastructure.


Nice to Have


Global supply chain experience in transportation and logistics.
Previous experience setting up a data stack in a startup from scratch.
Interest or experience in working with cutting-edge open-source data tool.
Proven ability to convert ad-hoc data requests into scalable, automated pipelines.
Experience working in the logistics domain.


Compensation And Benefits

The base pay range for this role is $140,000 to $160,000 annually. This range reflects the base pay that Loop reasonably expects to pay for the position at the time of hire. Actual compensation will depend on job-related factors, including experience, skills, qualifications, location, and business needs.

In addition to base salary, this role may be eligible for equity. This role is also eligible for Loop's benefits, including health, dental, and vision insurance, 401(k) match, paid time off, paid holidays, and parental leave.

Equal Opportunity

Loop is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Why you should join Loop?

Title: Comm & Ind Field Data Spec
Description:
Overview

Position is responsible for the inspection, data collection, recording, and analysis of all assigned commercial and industrial field assessment within Marion County. Will ensure that all commercial and industrial field information is accurately measured and input into the property tax database in a timely manner so that it may be used by the Assessment Division and other city and county agencies.

The position requires the knowledge of established laws and IAAO standards, which control most of the actions in this position. Independent judgment, subject to supervisory oversight, is used when there are no available policies and procedures. Other duties and tasks are routine in nature and performed under moderate supervision following standard procedures. Position reports to Manager Commercial and Industrial Field.

Agency Summary

The purpose and mission of the Marion County Assessor’s agency is to provide the city, county, and townships located in the city with information regarding how property values are assessed. The agency is able to do this by cultivating a productive, collaborative, and compliant work environment in order for our workforce to meet the needs of the constituents of Indianapolis and Marion County.

The City of Indianapolis Marion County is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran, or disability status. We value diversity in perspectives and experiences among colleagues and the residents of this city of whom we serve.

Position Responsibilities


Ensures that all commercial and industrial property in Marion County is assessed according to Indiana law, Department of Local Government Finance rules, and the standards of the International Association of Assessing Officers (IAAO)
Performs quality control checking on work product as previously assessed to ensure all additions or deletions of property are correctly processed for all commercial and industrial parcels for reassessment
Reads and analyzes site plans and construction blueprints for commercial and industrial parcels
Consults with Commercial and Industrial Field Manager and Deputy Director of Commercial and Industrial Assessment to resolve assessment discrepancies
Coordinates with taxpayers or taxpayer designees to gain access to property as required
Makes significant contributions to the assessment of commercial and industrial parcels by applying demonstrated knowledge
Coordinates with GIS department, permit and zoning departments and other government agencies as required
Measures all attached or detached utility and storage buildings to ensure proper assessment of parcel
Evaluates and determines if parcel is Commercial and Industrial or personal property
Measures and assess commercial and industrial property including large warehouse facilities up to a million square feet or more, large sky rise facilities up to 20 or more floors with multiple offices, utility, and conference rooms
Assesses structures for required number of sprinkler systems required to meet Fire and Safety Regulations
Utilizes different measuring techniques for fireproof materials, steel, and wood to accurately assess parcel
Schedules and prioritizes routing and workloads of assigned parcels
Assists all persons who visit office, who call over the telephone or meet with manager or subordinates in the field
Must maintain confidentiality at all times
This list of duties and responsibilities is not intended to be all-inclusive and may be expanded to include other duties or responsibilities that management may deem necessary from time to time


Qualifications

High School Diploma or equivalent and a minimum of two (2) years’ prior work-related experience are required. Applicants must be certified by the DLGF as a Level II Assessor/Appraiser or be willing to earn that certification within six months of hiring. Must have knowledge of construction terminology. Effective project management, computer, and communication skills are required. Knowledge of imaging systems and other software is required. Applicants must have the ability to assist customers in a professional and courteous manner, whether it’s face-to-face, by email, or over the telephone. The position requires attention to detail and candidates must be able to type, create, and edit reports as needed, and communicate effectively, both orally and in writing. Must possess or obtain and hold a valid Indiana Driver’s License. All activities must comply with state law.

Working Conditions

Essential functions are conducted 40% of the time in a standard office environment with limited exposure to adverse environmental conditions. The other 60% requires extensive travel off-site with possible exposure to adverse environmental conditions.

Physical Conditions


Sitting at a desk the majority of the day
Some lifting, carrying, pushing and/or pulling of objects and materials of light weight (5-10) pounds
Tasks may involve extended periods of time at keyboard or workstation
Tasks require the ability to perceive and hear sounds and see visual cues or signals
Tasks require the ability to communicate orally

Title: Data Engineer II
Description:
Job Description

We are Lennar

Lennar is one of the nation's leading homebuilders, dedicated to making an impact and creating an extraordinary experience for their Homeowners, Communities, and Associates by building quality homes and providing exceptional customer service, giving back to the communities in which we work and live in, and fostering a culture of opportunity and growth for our Associates throughout their career. Lennar has been recognized as a Fortune 500® company and consistently ranked among the top homebuilders in the United States.

Join a Company that Empowers you to Build your Future

As a Data Engineer, you are responsible for analyzing large amounts of business data, solving real world problems, and developing metrics and business cases that will enable Business Insights. This is done by leveraging data from various platforms such as Jira, Portal, Salesforce. You will work with a team of Product Managers, Software Engineers and Business Intelligence Engineers to automate and scale the analysis, and to make the data more actionable to manage business at scale. You will own many large datasets, implement new data pipelines that feed into or from critical data systems.



A career with purpose.
A career built on making dreams come true.
A career built on building zero defect homes, cost management, and adherence to schedules.



Your Responsibilities On The Team



Design, implement and support an analytical data infrastructure and working knowledge of Modern Data Warehouse concepts.
Design, build, and maintain efficient and scalable data pipelines and ETL processes to process large volumes of structured and unstructured data.
Optimize data storage and retrieval methods to ensure performance, scalability, and cost-efficiency.
Manage AWS resources including EC2, S3, Glue, Lambda, API’s, IAM, Cloud Watch etc.
Interface with other technology teams to extract, transform, and load data from a wide variety of data sources using SQL and AWS big data technologies
Explore and learn the latest AWS technologies to provide new capabilities and increase efficiency
Collaborate with Data Scientists and Business Intelligence Engineers (BIEs) to recognize and help adopt best practices in reporting and analysis
Help continually improve ongoing reporting and analysis processes, automating or simplifying self-service support for customers
Maintain internal reporting platforms/tools including troubleshooting and development. Interact with internal users to establish and clarify requirements in order to develop report specifications.
Work with Engineering partners to help shape and implement the development of BI infrastructure including Data Warehousing, reporting and analytics platforms.
Contribute to the development of the BI tools, skills, culture and impact.
Write advanced SQL queries and Python code to develop solutions.
Working Knowledge of Snowflake.
Collaborate across teams to align AI initiatives with organizational goals and understanding of AI concepts
Knowledge on continuous integration/continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines and working on deployments when necessary.



Requirements



Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
3-5 years of experience in data engineering or a related role, with demonstrated success in delivering data solutions.
AWS Glue, Lambda, S3, EC2, CloudWatch, Cloud Trail.
Dbt, Snowflake, SQL, Python, Qlik.
Proficient in SQL, with the ability to write complex queries, perform query optimization, and conduct performance tuning.
Experience with NoSQL databases, such as MongoDB, Cassandra, or DynamoDB, and an understanding of their appropriate use cases.
Strong programming skills in Python, Java, or Scala, with experience in data processing frameworks (e.g., Apache Spark, Hadoop).
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and data services, such as AWS Redshift, Azure Synapse, or Google BigQuery.
Knowledge of big data technologies, including Hadoop, Spark, Kafka, and HBase, with experience in distributed data processing.
Familiarity with data orchestration tools, such as Apache Airflow for scheduling and managing data workflows.
Experience with data versioning and testing tools, such as DVC (Data Version Control) and dbt (data build tool).
Understanding of data security practices, including encryption, access controls, and data masking.



Physical & Office/Site Presence Requirements:

Regular, in-person attendance at the Lennar office during regular work hours is an essential function of this job. This is primarily a sedentary office position which requires the incumbent to have the ability to operate computer equipment, speak, hear, bend, stoop, reach, lift, and move and carry up to 25 lbs. Finger dexterity is necessary.

This description outlines the basic responsibilities and requirements for the position noted. This is not a comprehensive listing of all job duties of the Associates. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

Life at Lennar

At Lennar, we are committed to fostering a supportive and enriching environment for our Associates, offering a comprehensive array of benefits designed to enhance their well-being and professional growth. Our Associates have access to robust health insurance plans, including Medical, Dental, and Vision coverage, ensuring their health needs are well taken care of. Our 401(k) Retirement Plan, complete with a $1 for $1 Company Match up to 5%, helps secure their financial future, while Paid Parental Leave and an Associate Assistance Plan provide essential support during life's critical moments. To further support our Associates, we provide an Education Assistance Program and up to $30,000 in Adoption Assistance, underscoring our commitment to their diverse needs and aspirations. From the moment of hire, they can enjoy up to three weeks of vacation annually, alongside generous Holiday, Sick Leave, and Personal Day policies. Additionally, we offer a New Hire Referral Bonus Program, significant Home Purchase Discounts, and unique opportunities such as the Everyone’s Included Day. At Lennar, we believe in investing in our Associates, empowering them to thrive both personally and professionally. Lennar Associates will have access to these benefits as outlined by Lennar’s policies and applicable plan terms. Visit Lennartotalrewards.com to view our suite of benefits.

Join the fun and follow us on social media to see what's happening at our company, and don't forget to connect with us on Lennar: Overview | LinkedIn<https://www.linkedin.com/company/lennar/> for the latest job opportunities.

Lennar is an equal opportunity employer and complies with all applicable federal, state, and local fair employment practices laws.

Title: Accelerating Insights with Power BI for Enhanced Dashboard Functionality for Canada Via Rail
Description:
Streamlining Data Visualization for Brinker with Power BI

The Challenge

A leading company referred to here as the client, sought to enhance its data visualization and analytics capabilities by transitioning from Domo Dashboards to Microsoft Power BI. The client’s primary objective was to replicate their existing dashboards in Power BI with a refreshed focus on their customer, “Brinker.” A significant challenge was the integration of Power BI with NetSuite via an ODBC connection, especially given the large volumes of data involved. The Customer table alone contained over 400,000 records, leading to prolonged data loading times. Additionally, the client required the dashboards to be developed within a tight deadline of a few days, posing a considerable challenge given the complexity of the task.

The Solution

Our Consultancy Agency Stepped In To Assist The Client With Their Power BI Dashboard Creation, Addressing Each Challenge With Strategic Solutions

Optimized Data Integration: Recognizing the limitations of the ODBC connection, especially with the Oracle database’s compatibility issues with Power BI, we recommended an alternative approach. We suggested leveraging NetSuite Analytics Warehouse, a cloud-based BI solution that could seamlessly integrate with Power BI, thus improving data loading times and overall dashboard performance.

Custom Dashboard Development: Drawing from the example attachments and specific requirements provided by the client, we meticulously crafted Power BI dashboards that mirrored the desired layout and functionality seen in their Domo Dashboards. This included replicating various charts and widgets focusing on Brinker, ensuring a familiar yet improved user experience.

Language and Filter Adjustments: Addressing the client’s request for language updates, we ensured all dashboard labels, including those on the Backorder dashboard, were presented in English. Although adding numerous filters was identified as a time-consuming task, we engaged in a detailed discussion with the client about the implications. We agreed to prioritize essential updates to meet the project’s tight deadline.

Summary Dashboard Creation: We proposed and implemented a summary tab to enhance the dashboard’s utility. This tab featured key charts from each dashboard section, carefully selected based on the client’s specifications. For charts lacking titles, we added descriptive titles such as “Open Orders by Product Category” to ensure clarity and usefulness in the summary view.

The Outcome

The successful implementation of the Power BI dashboards significantly improved the client’s data visualization and analysis capabilities. The transition from Domo to Power BI, coupled with the strategic use of NetSuite Analytics Warehouse, resulted in faster data loading times, enhanced dashboard performance, and more insightful data analytics tailored specifically to Brinker.

The project was delivered within the ambitious deadline set by the client, thanks to our team’s expertise and efficient project management. The client was particularly pleased with the custom solutions provided, including the language corrections and the highly functional summary dashboard, which offered a consolidated view of key metrics across all tabs.

Conclusion

This case study exemplifies our consultancy agency’s ability to navigate complex data integration challenges, deliver custom Power BI solutions, and meet the specific needs of our clients within tight deadlines. Our approach not only addresses immediate technical challenges but also ensures that the solutions we provide are scalable, efficient, and tailored to our clients’ strategic goals.

The Remote Front Desk / Office Assistant role is designed to provide essential administrative support to clinical staff and serve as the initial point of contact for patients. • Manage the virtual front desk, including answering calls and responding to emails professionally and promptly.

• Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and openness to learning new software.

• Commitment to patient confidentiality and data security.

• Reliable high-speed internet and a quiet home working environment.

Title: Analytics Specialist
Description:
1. About Our Client:

The organization operates within the defense logistics sector, focusing on addressing complex challenges in supply chain and operations analytics. It supports strategic and tactical service activities by developing analytical solutions that enhance decision-making and operational efficiency.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Analytics Specialist role involves designing and delivering analytic solutions that provide actionable insights into supply chain and operational processes. This position is key in translating data into meaningful outputs that influence business decisions and improve systemic and process performance.




3. Responsibilities:

• Independently design and develop analytic solutions addressing quantitative and qualitative aspects of service activities.

• Create self-service outputs such as dashboards and visual displays.

• Translate customer needs into analytic solutions with minimal oversight.

• Analyze large, complex supply chain and operations data sets to generate insights for unclear problems.

• Identify, source, and analyze data from various systems.

• Apply statistical models and algorithms to inform process or business actions.

• Detect deviations in process or system performance and communicate findings effectively.

• Communicate accurate information clearly.




4. Requirements:

• Undergraduate degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Engineering, Economics, Business, Applied Math, or related field.

• Four years of relevant experience in Supply Chain and/or Operations analytics.

• Basic professional certification with analytics tools such as SAS or R.

• Experience with AGILE delivery lifecycle, SQL, and database design (relational/non-relational).

• Preferred: Experience with Defense Logistics Agency (DLA) and OneStream.




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Salary range is $85,000 to $115,000.




6. Benefits & Perks:

• Medical, Dental, and Vision Plans with PPO and HSA options.

• Flexible Spending Accounts for Health Care and Dependent Care.

• Health Savings Account (HSA).

• 401(k) with matching contributions.

• Roth IRA option.

• Qualified Transportation Expense with matching contributions.

• Short Term and Long Term Disability coverage.

• Life and Accidental Death & Dismemberment insurance.

• Basic and Voluntary Life Insurance.

• Wellness Program.

• Paid Time Off (PTO).

• Eleven holidays.

• Professional Development Reimbursement.

Title: Executive Administrative Assistant
Description:
About The Company

TriNet is a leading provider of comprehensive human resources solutions tailored for small to midsize businesses (SMBs). Our mission is to enhance business productivity by offering strategic HR outsourcing services that allow our clients to concentrate on core business operations and growth. We deliver a wide range of HR solutions, including payroll processing, human capital consulting, employment law compliance, and employee benefits such as health insurance, retirement plans, and workers' compensation insurance. With a nationwide presence and an experienced executive team, TriNet is committed to empowering SMBs with innovative HR services. Our stock is publicly traded on the NYSE under the ticker symbol TNET. We foster a dynamic work environment that values innovation, diversity, and high professional standards, making us a trusted partner for businesses seeking to streamline their HR functions.

About The Role

The Executive Administrative Assistant role at TriNet is a vital support position responsible for providing comprehensive administrative assistance to 2-3 Vice President level leaders based in Atlanta. The successful candidate will serve as the primary point of contact for internal and external interactions related to these leaders, managing a variety of confidential and high-priority tasks. Responsibilities include calendar management, scheduling meetings, preparing presentations, coordinating travel arrangements, and handling expense reports. The role requires proactive communication, excellent organizational skills, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. The Executive Administrative Assistant will also facilitate effective communication across senior management teams, assist with event planning, and provide backup support for other executive assistants as needed. This position offers an excellent opportunity to work closely with senior leadership and contribute to the smooth operation of the executive team within a reputable organization.

Qualifications


Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent work experience preferred
Minimum of 4+ years of experience in an executive support or administrative role
Experience working in a public company is preferred
Proficiency in MS Office Suite, especially Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint, and OneNote
Strong organizational and time management skills
Exceptional written and verbal communication abilities
High level of discretion, confidentiality, and professional ethics
Ability to prioritize tasks effectively and adapt to changing priorities
Experience with event planning, travel coordination, and meeting arrangements
Ability to communicate effectively across all organizational levels


Responsibilities


Manage calendars, schedule appointments, and coordinate meetings for Vice Presidents
Assist leaders with email management to ensure timely approvals and responses
Prepare agendas, attend staff meetings, and track action items
Coordinate travel arrangements, including booking flights, accommodations, and transportation
Assist with expense report preparation and reimbursement processes
Serve as a liaison between senior management and internal/external stakeholders
Proactively follow up on outstanding action items to ensure responsiveness
Support event planning and coordination for meetings, dinners, and offsite activities
Provide backup support to other executive assistants as needed
Ensure compliance with company policies and standards
Perform additional duties as assigned by leadership


Benefits


Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision insurance plans
Life and disability insurance coverage
401(k) savings plan with company match
Employee stock purchase plan
Paid time off (PTO) and eleven company-observed holidays
Generous leave policies and wellness programs
Opportunities for professional development and career growth
Flexible work environment, including remote work options where applicable


Equal Opportunity

TriNet is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, or any other legally protected characteristic. We encourage qualified candidates from all backgrounds to apply. Reasonable accommodations are available for applicants with disabilities throughout the recruitment process.

Title: Excel Specialist (Remote)
Description:
Role: Excel Specialist (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Job Type: Contract
Payout: $40 - $55/hour




Role Overview:

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking an Excel Expert to work on a contract basis. The role involves creating and maintaining complex financial models, automating reporting processes, and ensuring data accuracy across financial systems. You will support cross-functional teams by delivering high-quality spreadsheets and dashboards that drive business decisions.




Key Responsibilities:

• Build and maintain sophisticated Excel financial models, including forecasting, budgeting, and scenario analysis.

• Develop automated reporting solutions using Excel VBA macros and Power Query to reduce manual effort.

• Validate and cleanse financial data to ensure integrity and consistency across datasets.

• Collaborate with finance and operations teams to translate business requirements into functional spreadsheet solutions.

• Document processes, formulas, and assumptions to ensure reproducibility and transparency.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including complex formulas, pivot tables, and data validation.

• Experience writing and debugging VBA macros to automate repetitive tasks.

• Strong understanding of financial modeling principles and Excel’s Power Query/Pivot tools.

• Ability to interpret business requirements and translate them into functional spreadsheet solutions.

• Attention to detail to ensure accuracy in financial data and calculations.




More About the Opportunity:

This role offers a unique opportunity to work with a global leader in the Financial Services industry, contributing to streamlined financial reporting and data-driven decision-making. The position supports critical business functions with measurable impact.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.

With a global presence enhanced by acquisitions in France and Canada and support from private equity investments, the company focuses on advancing product development and research to deliver valuable insights.


2. About the Opportunity:

The Insights Analyst role is part of the Insights team responsible for delivering clear, data-driven presentations and actionable business recommendations to clients in the food and beverage sector. This position contributes by mastering syndicated products and supporting clients through tailored reports and presentations, ultimately enhancing client decision-making and satisfaction.


3. Responsibilities:

• Use syndicated products to extract data and create insightful, impactful stories addressing client needs

• Provide actionable insights to clients

• Deliver excellent customer service with a client-focused approach

• Suggest project enhancements beyond initial requests to improve storytelling and identify upsell opportunities

• Represent client feedback internally to improve company solutions


4. Requirements:

• BA/BS in Marketing, Business, Psychology, Economics, or related field

• 3-5 years of experience in market research, data insights, or similar analytical roles

• Strong analytical skills to synthesize diverse data into clear narratives

• Excellent organizational skills to manage multiple projects

• Effective communication and consultative skills for diverse client audiences

• Familiarity with G-Suite, intermediate PowerPoint and Excel skills

• Experience in foodservice is a plus


5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Base salary range: $70,000-$75,000

• Annual performance-based bonus: 7%

• Actual compensation based on skills, experience, and location


6. Benefits & Perks:

• Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance

• 401(k) with company match

• Annual bonus

• Unlimited paid time off

• Wellness reimbursement and access to headquarters gym
